Jan 29, 2026 at 4:31PM

### New practitioner events: Delhi, Bengaluru updates

![Image 2](https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/photo/127545635.cms)

I’m flagging three newly surfaced, practitioner‑focused additions since the last update, with immediate dates and clear relevance to your Delhi NCR and Bengaluru priorities.

**ET AI Impact Forum 2026 — Delhi, Feb 18.** A specialized forum within the India AI Impact Summit at Bharat Mandapam, Pragati Maidan, centered on four pillars: Silicon Blueprint (semiconductor manufacturing/packaging), Data Marketplace (access and security), Infrastructure Utility (compute as a utility), and Capital Engine (long‑horizon capital). Audience spans senior tech executives, semiconductor leaders, investors, deep tech founders, infra builders, and policymakers. CFP and early‑bird details are not specified. Registration is open. [Ai](https://ai.economictimes.com/ai-summit/impact-forum?utm_source=article&utm_medium=onsite&utm_campaign=registerations)

**IFCCI Roundtable on AI for Business Leaders — Bengaluru, Jan 29 (today).** Roundtable at Dassault Systèmes focused on real enterprise AI use cases, operational transformation, governance/trust, and human‑centric adoption. Confirmed participants include representatives from iSPIRT, IFCCI, Dassault Systèmes, CNRS, Université CY Cergy Paris, and industry founders. CFP and early‑bird are not applicable. [Ifcci](https://www.ifcci.org.in/communication/news/n/news/ifcci-roundtable-on-ai-for-business-leaders-use-cases-insights.html)

**Generative & Agentic AI — 2‑day workshop at IISc Bengaluru, Feb 7–8.** Hands‑on curriculum covering Generative AI foundations, GANs, VAEs, Transformers/ViTs, LLMs, fine‑tuning and PEFT, RLHF, LangChain, and RAG. Registration fee is ₹2,000; early‑bird and CFP are not specified/applicable. [District](https://www.district.in/events/generative-agentic-ai-2-day-workshop-at-iisc-bengaluru-feb7-2026-buy-tickets)

_Context:_ These add to the previously noted ET AI Conclave & Awards (Bengaluru, Feb 26) and ET GenAI Hackathon timelines—together covering Delhi NCR and Bengaluru in February with strong practitioner depth.

*   Register for the ET AI Impact Forum (Feb 18, Delhi) to secure access; no early‑bird is listed.
*   If in Bengaluru today, check availability for the IFCCI roundtable on site.
*   Book the IISc 2‑day workshop (Feb 7–8; ₹2,000) while seats remain; no early‑bird noted.

### Bengaluru ET AI Conclave adds heavyweight speakers

![Image 3](https://img.etimg.com/thumb/msid-127600284,width-1200,height-630,imgsize-1392982,overlay-economictimes/articleshow.jpg)

I’m tracking two new practitioner-focused additions that complement earlier Mumbai and Delhi NCR updates.

**ET AI Conclave & Awards 2025 — Bengaluru**

 Date: February 26, 2026. Location: Bengaluru. Practitioner forum centered on real-world AI decisions and scaled deployment, geared to CXOs, founders, senior operators, and ecosystem enablers. [M](https://m.economictimes.com/ai/ai-insights/how-attending-the-et-ai-conclave-awards-2025-strengthens-your-ai-point-of-view-and-leadership-strategy/articleshow/127599416.cms)

_Speaker lineup highlights_:

*   Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw
*   Dr. Vishal Sikka (Jury Chair)
*   Naveen Tewari; Srikanth Velamakanni; Sharad Sanghi
*   Gaurav Aggarwal; Vivek Raghavan; Paras Chopra; Vishal Dhupar; Bhuwan Lodha; Megha Yethadka; Ankush Sabharwal; Sravanth Aluru; Hari Balaji; Nirmit Parikh; Dilip Jose; Kirthiga Reddy; Abhishek Singh (IAS); Darren Mowry; Prukalpa Sankar; Sandhya Devanathan

Registration is open. [Economictimes](https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/et-ai-awards-2025) Source update. [M](https://m.economictimes.com/ai/ai-insights/how-attending-the-et-ai-conclave-awards-2025-strengthens-your-ai-point-of-view-and-leadership-strategy/articleshow/127599416.cms)

**ET GenAI Hackathon 2026**

 Multi‑phase challenge (online qualifier and finale presentations) for students, professionals, and researchers building India‑first GenAI solutions. Registration opened December 26, 2025; prototype submissions due March 1, 2026; winners announced March 28, 2026. Registration is free on Unstop. [M](https://m.economictimes.com/ai/ai-insights/dont-miss-this-ai-opportunity/articleshow/127618112.cms)

_Tracks_:

*   Innovation & Learning (Students)
*   Production-Grade Solutions (Professionals)
*   Novel AI Research (Researchers)

### New Mumbai summit and India AI CFP update

I’m flagging two material updates aligned with your focus on strong lineups and timely windows in Mumbai and Delhi NCR.

**NexTech Summit 2026 — Mumbai (newly announced)**

*   Date: February 6, 2026
*   Location: The Leela, Mumbai
*   Format: Invite-only summit for 500+ senior digital leaders (CIOs, CTOs, CAIOs, AI/ML heads)
*   Focus: Enterprise AI governance, cybersecurity resilience, data accountability, intelligence-led strategies
*   Speaker lineup: Kaustubh Dhavse; Manisha Verma; Rajesh Kannan; Atanu Roy; Milind Korgaonkar; Vikrant Deoras; Rajeevan KK; Ramesh Gurram; Ramita Vikram; additional speakers to be announced
*   Registration: [Zfrmz](https://zfrmz.in/DCpIabuOwa3UdoER4Mwy)

Announcement and details were issued today. [Itvoice](https://www.itvoice.in/nextech-summit-2026-indias-largest-cio-conference-to-convene-indias-top-cios-and-technology-leaders-in-mumbai-on-february-06)

**India AI Impact Summit 2026 — CFP timing (Delhi NCR)**

The event remains scheduled for February 16–20, 2026, with a new CFP deadline of January 31, 2026 for Expressions of Interest for the opening film — eight days from now. [Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/showcase/indiaaiimpactexpo/)

*   CFP link: [Lnkd](https://lnkd.in/gDGJWEQk)
*   Exhibitor registration: [Lnkd](https://lnkd.in/gt4tCDHF)
*   Expected scale: 200,000+ attendees; 70,000+ sqm exhibition space

_Why this matters_: NexTech adds a near-term, practitioner-relevant Mumbai option with a concentrated senior audience, while the India AI Impact Summit update introduces a short CFP window tied to a high-visibility segment of a large-scale Delhi event. These create immediate decision points on outreach and participation.

### Near-term AI events with active windows

I’m tracking upcoming AI, data, and GenAI events across Delhi NCR, Bengaluru, and Mumbai through June 2026, prioritizing strong speakers, practitioner depth, CFPs, and early-bird windows.

**Delhi NCR**

*   **India AI Impact Summit 2026** — Feb 16–20, Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi. Global tech leaders; governance, ethics, deployment; includes expo and challenges. [Impact](https://impact.indiaai.gov.in/)[Impact](https://impact.indiaai.gov.in/)
*   GAISA 2026 — Feb 10–11, Yashobhoomi, New Delhi. Real‑world AI across industries. [Tradeindia](https://www.tradeindia.com/tradeshows/131408/gaisa-global-artificial-intelligence-summit-2026.html)
*   Elets AI Summit 2026 — Jan 22, Eros Hotel, New Delhi. 60+ speakers from government and enterprise. [Indiaaisummit](https://www.indiaaisummit.in/)
*   AI Innovation Summit 2026 — Jan 21, The Westin Gurgaon. Registrations closed. [Aiinnovationsummit](https://www.aiinnovationsummit.in/)
*   AI Bharat Expo 2026 — Mar 23–25, Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi. Co‑located with Convergence India; responsible AI focus areas; multiple registration types. [Aibharatexpo](https://www.aibharatexpo.com/)[Convergenceindia](https://www.convergenceindia.org/register-now.aspx)
*   NC2026: National Conference on AI and Digital Transformation — Feb 7, Delhi. CFP deadlines passed; tiered registration pricing listed. [Easychair](https://easychair.org/cfp/NC2026)[Dias](https://dias.ac.in/23rd-national-conference-on-ai-digital-transformation/)

**Bengaluru**

*   **PyTorch Day India 2026** — Feb 7. Developer‑focused technical talks, discussions, posters. [Events](https://events.linuxfoundation.org/pytorch-day-india/)[Events](https://events.linuxfoundation.org/pytorch-day-india/register/)
*   **MLDS 2026** — Mar 26–27, Nimhans Convention Center. Agentic AI deep dives, coding challenges, case studies. [Mlds](https://mlds.analyticsindiamag.com/)
*   AWS AI Conclave 2026 — Jan 22, Sheraton Grand, Whitefield. Registrations closed. [Aws](https://aws.amazon.com/events/ai_conclave/)
*   International Conference on Sustainable Innovation in Business — Feb 27–28, JAIN University. Registration deadline passed; publication note listed. [Knowafest](https://www.knowafest.com/explore/events/2026/01/0604-international-conference-sustainable-innovation-business-integrating-academia-industry-ai-future-resilience-2026-jain-university-bengaluru)
*   Advanced Certification Programme in Agentic and Generative AI — Starts Feb, IISc Bangalore (executive, 6 months). Application deadline passed; pricing listed. [Talentsprint](https://talentsprint.com/course/agentic-and-generative-ai-iisc)

**Mumbai**

*   **Agentic AI Summit 2026** — Feb 12, Taj Santacruz. C‑suite focus; “Physics of AI” theme. [Enterpriseai](https://enterpriseai.economictimes.indiatimes.com/agentic-ai-summit)[Evvnt](https://evvnt.com/events/?_evDiscoveryPath=/event/3421752-agentic-ai-summit-ai-events-and-conference-india)
*   Gartner Data & Analytics Summit 2026 — Sep 21–22, Grand Hyatt. Data, analytics, and AI leaders. [Gartner](https://www.gartner.com/en/conferences/apac/data-analytics-india)
*   KubeCon + CloudNativeCon India 2026 — Jun 18–19. Cloud native flagship conference. [Events](https://events.linuxfoundation.org/kubecon-cloudnativecon-india/)

**CFPs and early‑bird windows**

*   BRAINCON 2026 — Early‑bird Jan 20–26; CFP deadlines passed; event Mar 20–22 (Singapore). [Braincon](https://braincon.brainallianz.org/)
*   Doctors AI Global Summit 2026 — Sep 19–20, Delhi. Early‑bird active Jan 1–Feb 15 (₹5,000–₹8,000 + GST); CFP window Aug 20–Sep 10. [Doctorsaiglobalsummit](https://doctorsaiglobalsummit.com/)
*   Cypher 2026 — 2026 dates TBA. Super early‑bird ends May 8 (₹7,000–₹19,999). [Cypher](https://cypher.analyticsindiamag.com/tickets)
*   SAIM 2026 — Feb 14–15, Chennai. CFP deadline passed. [Acsit2026](https://acsit2026.org/saim/index)[Wikicfp](http://www.wikicfp.com/cfp/servlet/event.showcfp?eventid=178043)
*   WCAIAA 2026 — Jan 30–31, Goa. CFP deadline passed. [Scrs](https://scrs.in/conference/wcaiaa2026)

_What matters now_: Prioritize Delhi’s India AI Impact Summit and Bengaluru’s PyTorch Day and MLDS for practitioner depth; lock Mumbai’s Agentic AI Summit for exec engagement; track early‑bird clocks for Doctors AI and Cypher.

## Top 5 AI Conferences in India – 2025 Edition

### 1. Cypher 2025

**Location:** KTPO Trade Centre, Whitefield, Bengaluru  
**Dates:** September 17–19, 2025  
**Focus:** India’s largest AI summit, featuring 100+ speakers, 100+ exhibitors, and over 5,000 attendees daily.  
**Who should attend:** Enterprise leaders, AI practitioners, data engineers, and tech entrepreneurs  
**Registration:** [Register Here](https://cypher.analyticsindiamag.com/register/)  
**Contact:** info@aimmediahouse.com | +91 94965 21885 / 96325 33477  
**Website:** [cypher.analyticsindiamag.com](https://cypher.analyticsindiamag.com/)

---

### 2. MachineCon GCC Summit 2025

**Location:** Taj Yeshwantpur, Bengaluru  
**Dates:** June 19–20, 2025  
**Focus:** Tailored for leaders in Global Capability Centres (GCCs), focusing on the strategic use of AI at scale within large enterprises  
**Who should attend:** Digital transformation leaders and heads of GCCs or innovation hubs  
**Registration:** Register Here  
**Contact:** info@aimmediahouse.com  
**Website:** machinecon.analyticsindiamag.com

---

### 3. Action AI Conference 2025

**Location:** New Delhi  
**Date:** September 28, 2025  
**Focus:** A one-day, power-packed event designed for practical AI implementation, bringing together professionals solving actual business and societal problems using data and automation  
**Who should attend:** Professionals, product managers, and AI consultants  
**Registration:** [Register Here](https://actionaiconf.com/)  
**Contact:** info@actionaiconf.com  
**Website:** [actionaiconf.com](https://actionaiconf.com/)

---

### 4. AI Visionaries Summit 2025

**Location:** Hyatt Regency, New Delhi  
**Dates:** May 30–31, 2025  
**Focus:** Organised by Magnivel International, this summit brings together corporate leaders from diverse sectors to discuss strategies, challenges, and solutions in the rapidly evolving field of artificial intelligence  
**Who should attend:** Business strategists and professionals interested in leveraging AI for organisational growth and innovation  
**Registration:** Register Here  
**Contact:** info@magnivelinternational.com  
**Website:** magnivelinternational.com

---

### 5. Vertical AI Summit 2025

**Location:** Bengaluru  
**Dates:** July 10–11, 2025  
**Focus:** The Vertical AI Summit focuses on domain-specific AI applications that deliver measurable business outcomes. It brings together professionals to explore real-world AI solutions in sectors like healthcare, manufacturing, logistics, and finance  
**Who should attend:** Business leaders, industry-specific AI practitioners, investors, and decision-makers looking to implement or invest in specialised AI solutions  
**Registration:** Register Here  
**Contact:** info@verticalaisummit.com  
**Website:** [verticalaisummit.com](https://verticalaisummit.com/)

These conferences offer unparalleled opportunities to network with industry leaders, gain insights into the latest AI trends, and explore practical applications of AI across various sectors. Whether you're a seasoned professional or just starting your AI journey, attending these events will provide valuable knowledge and connections to propel your career forward.

The **Elets AI Summit 2026**, is envisioned as the **national flagship platform** for dialogue, innovation, and policy exchange on artificial intelligence. The Delhi edition will spotlight how India is transforming from a data and talent rich nation into an **AI-empowered powerhouse**, where technology serves as a tool for inclusive growth and sustainable development across governments and enterprises.

Through a series of high-level plenary sessions, sectoral discussions, startup showcases, and policy dialogues, the Summit will bring together government leaders, leading enterprises, global tech companies, startups, academia, and international partners to co-create the roadmap for India’s AI future.

# Top AI Conferences to Attend in 2026

13 minutes read

We reviewed over 110 of the biggest AI conferences of 2026, shortlisted the 20 most important ones. These events highlight the must-know AI strategies and trends of 2026. If you lead a company or a department, plan to visit them to learn how AI infrastructure, regulation, and monetization are evolving.

Summarize this article in:

For businesses, these events are no longer optional. They highlight how LLMs are trained, how agentic systems operate, and how AI technologies can optimize and automate core processes. Digital marketing and SEO teams can get early insights into where search algorithms and conversational interfaces are heading. Google, Microsoft, NVIDIA, IBM, Oracle, and leading global researchers reveal architectural decisions and best practices that later materialize as real-world business drivers.

As a company helping brands stay visible in AI-driven search, **[SeoProfy](https://seoprofy.com/)** closely follows these events because they show us how people will search and interact with content in the future. We’ve handpicked the upcoming AI conferences of 2026 that actually help brands stay ahead of the curve.

## Top AI Conferences and Events of 2026

Below you’ll find a table of the best AI conferences 2026 with official dates, prices, and formats, to help you navigate the year’s top summits.

| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Conference** | **Date** | **Location** | **Price** |

| [AI DevWorld 2026](http://aidevworld.com) | February 18-20, 2026 | San Jose, CA, San Jose Convention Center | Starting from 1150 USD, Limited Access is free |
| [AI: Smart Business and Entrepreneurship Summit](http://fienta.com/ai-small-business-and-entrepreneurship-summit-2025) | February 27, 2026 | Virtual | Starting from 199 EUR/234 USD |

### AI DevWorld 2026

* **Date**: February 18-20, 2026
* **Location**: San Jose, CA, San Jose Convention Center
* **Price**: Starting from 1150 USD, Limited Access is free
* **Focus**: The latest breakthroughs in AI developer technologies, including model optimization, building agents, RAG services, security/ethics, and enterprise integration.
* **Best for**: Software engineers and Data Scientists, seeking a landscape view of new open-source libraries and neural networks.
* **Featured speakers**: Jody Bailey (CPTO, Stack Overflow); Legare Kerrison (Developer Advocate, Red Hat); Jeff Fan (Senior Solutions Architect, DigitalOcean); Erez Yalon (VP of Security Research, Checkmarx).

AI DevWorld is the largest event for artificial intelligence and machine learning developers. This year, the best speakers will present their achievements in model architecture, training, fine-tuning, and performance optimization.

The AI conference reveals Retrieval-Augmented Generation being the exact mechanism behind how Google and Perplexity cite sources. Understanding how these systems are built helps you optimize your content so it’s more likely to be “retrieved” and used in AI overviews.

### NVIDIA GTC

* **Date**: March 16–19, 2026
* **Location**: San Jose, CA
* **Price**: Starting from 495 USD, full online access is free.
* **Focus**: Physical AI, AI Factories, Agentic AI, Inference, and Accelerated Computing (CUDA-X, LLMs, Robotics, Quantum Computing, HPC).
* **Best for**: Developers (tools, training, hackathons), Researchers (latest algorithms, science applications), and Business Leaders/CXOs (strategy, infrastructure deployment).
* **Featured speaker**: Jensen Huang (NVIDIA CEO).

NVIDIA GTC is one of the premier AI conferences of 2026 focused on “inference” and “Agentic AI” (machines that can reason and perform tasks). This is exactly what powers the next generation of search bots. This event will show the breakthrough of machine intelligence in every industry.

You are expected to get hands-on training, connect with experts, build skills, and boost your career in expert-led labs and workshops. Participants will get a chance to train on NVIDIA platforms, join hackathons, and connect with like-minded people.

HumanX is one of the most impactful industry AI conferences in 2026. The event promises that each attendee will leave better equipped for the future age of artificial intelligence. It connects the most influential executives, investors, and AI minds and shows you how investors and executives decide on their investment in artificial intelligence. The event will help you move from technical SEO to business growth metrics to get bigger budgets approved.

## Other Niche and Specialized AI Events of 2026

While the major AI conferences are great for networking and high-level scaling, the real heavy lifting of industry research happens at specialized scientific and education events like ICML, ICLR, and CVPR.

Seoul will host the International Conference on Machine Learning in 2026, with a deep focus on neural network theory. The ICLR in Rio de Janeiro will share the latest insights on how models process information. The Databricks Data + AI Summit in San Francisco will explain the management of massive data pipelines required for generative models.

In the AI cybersecurity space, the SANS AI Summit will focus on the defensive side of machine learning as the threats become more sophisticated and harder to detect.

The AI for Good Global Summit in Geneva is a unique event dedicated to how intelligent tools can solve human crises. The specialized show London’s AI & Big Data Expo will give a practical look at how machine intelligence connects with things like IoT and blockchain.

## Adapting to AI Trends

Whether you are an enthusiast, a content creator, an AI marketing expert, a data professional, or a CEO focused on business process optimization, neglecting the artificial intelligence trends is no longer an option. Its implementation is not a competitive advantage anymore. It has become a necessity for meeting user expectations.

It has already integrated with search and everyday usage devices, changing how users interact with websites, content, products and services. An [AI visibility audit](https://seoprofy.com/services/ai-seo/audit/) can help you discover whether your target audience can reach your brand through ChatGPT and Overviews.  Once done, you should proceed with a well-crafted [AI SEO strategy](https://seoprofy.com/blog/ai-seo-strategy/) to ensure your website is ready for the next wave of ranking signals, AI-generated summaries, and brand visibility across LLM interfaces.
