## Training more people?

Get your team access to the full DataCamp for business platform.[For Business](https://www.datacamp.com/business)For a bespoke solution [book a demo](https://www.datacamp.com/business/demo-2).

As AI continues to grow and become more ubiquitous in all industries, it’s important to stay current with the latest trends and technologies. Generative AI, for example, was a relatively small field just last year. It is now projected to become a [$1.3 trillion industry](https://www.bloomberg.com/company/press/generative-ai-to-become-a-1-3-trillion-market-by-2032-research-finds/) in the next ten years. While [articles on how to learn AI](https://www.forbes.com/sites/forbestechcouncil/2024/01/31/artificial-intelligence-in-2024-what-it-has-in-store-for-us/?sh=7f6b68c45cb3), [blogs on machine learning](https://www.datacamp.com/blog/how-to-learn-machine-learning), and courses such as [AI Fundamentals](https://www.datacamp.com/tracks/ai-fundamentals) are great starting points for learning, AI conferences provide a unique opportunity to hear from industry leaders and stay updated with the latest trends.

AI conferences offer many benefits to data practitioners and business leaders. They provide opportunities for learning with hands-on workshops, tutorials, and technical sessions covering the latest advancements in machine learning, deep learning, and other AI subfields. Attendees learn about the newest tools and best practices directly from experts who have used and developed them. Outside of the technical benefits, they also provide great opportunities to develop your career through scheduled networking opportunities.

This article will explore ten of the best AI conferences you can attend in 2026. We’ll discuss when and where they will be hosted and some of the key factors that could make the conference an ideal fit for you.

## 1. Radar: Hybrid Human-AI Teams

*   **Date**: April 1, 2026
*   **Location**: Online
*   Cost: Free of charge

RADAR returns in 2026 with a focus on the evolving relationship between human expertise and artificial intelligence. As organizations continue to navigate the integration of AI into their workflows, this coming event will explore how successful teams are building collaborative frameworks where human insight and AI capabilities work in tandem.

The conference will bring together industry leaders and data professionals of all stripes to discuss real strategies. Attendees can expect insights into how forward-thinking organizations are approaching AI adoption, developing the skills needed to work alongside AI systems, and building cultures that embrace this technological shift.

_Full agenda and speaker details will be announced soon._

The recordings from previous years are available in case you missed them:[RADAR: AI Edition (2025)](https://www.datacamp.com/radar/ai-2025), [Radar Skills Edition](https://www.datacamp.com/radar-skills-edition-2025-recordings), [Radar Forward Edition](https://www.datacamp.com/radar-forward-edition-2024), [Radar: The Analytics Edition](https://www.datacamp.com/radar-analytics-edition), and [Radar: The AI Edition](https://www.datacamp.com/radar-ai-2024)[(2024)](https://www.datacamp.com/radar/ai-2025).

## Sign Up to Radar: The AI Edition

Join us to explore how top organizations are turning AI into a competitive advantage.

## 2. The AI Conference San Francisco

*   **Date**: Sep 30-Oct 1, 2026.
*   **Location**: San Francisco, California, US.
*   **Cost**: From $199.

[The AI Conference](https://aiconference.com/) in San Francisco will be a two-day in-person conference discussing the latest in AI. Topics include what's working in the best applied-AI startups, and technical lessons on the nuances of neural architectures, foundational models, alignment, and more. This large event will provide many opportunities for learning, networking, and listening to interesting keynote discussions.

## 3. SuperAI

*   **Date**: June 10-11, 2026.
*   **Location**: Marina Bay Sands, Singapore.
*   **Cost**: $299-$999.

If you are interested in attending an AI conference in Asia, the [SuperAI conference](https://www.superai.com/) in June is a great option. This event, which has about 5,000 attendees, many of whom are C-level executives, represents a great networking opportunity. The event also has partners from the largest AI companies in the world, like Microsoft, Google, AWS, OpenAI, and Salesforce. Save money with the Early Bird discount.

## 4. Data + AI Summit

*   **Date**: June 15-18, 2026.
*   **Location**: Hybrid - San Francisco, California, US, and available online.
*   **Cost**: $1,395-$1,895 in-person. Free for virtual admission.

The [Data + AI Summit](https://www.databricks.com/dataaisummit/) is a four-day event hosted by Databricks. It includes panel discussions, networking opportunities, and training workshops on topics such as data engineering, data governance, and machine learning. Past conference panels and training sessions are available on [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLTPXxbhUt-YVWi_cf2UUDc9VZFLoRgu0l). Discounted tickets are available with group-rate pricing.

## 5. The AI Summit London

*   **Date**: June 10-11, 2026.
*   **Location**: London, UK.
*   **Cost**: £125-£2,499.

Over two days, [AI Summit London](https://london.theaisummit.com/) will cover a wide range of topics, including how to use AI responsibly and its application in different industries. The summit has a long list of sponsors and thousands of guests, making it a great venue for networking with many passionate AI practitioners. Costs vary depending on access level.

## 6. CDAO Government

*   **Date**: September 23-24, 2026.
*   **Location**: Washington, D.C., US.
*   **Cost**: $499 in-person. Free for VP and C-level government executives.

The [CDAO Government conference](https://cdao-gov.coriniumintelligence.com/) in D.C. is unique in that it focuses on applications of AI and data specifically for government organizations. This will be the 14th year of this event, and it is perfect for those who want to understand how to apply AI’s strength to government data problems safely.

## 7. Ai4 2026

*   **Date**: August 4-6, 2026.
*   **Location**: The Venetian, Las Vegas, Nevada, US.
*   **Cost**: $1,395-$3,195.

The [Ai4 2026 conference](https://ai4.io/vegas/) in Las Vegas will feature over 1,000 speakers and 12,000 attendees, with representation from more than 90 countries. This three-day event provides plenty of opportunities to network, learn about AI, and enjoy the unique city of Las Vegas. This is a diverse conference with attendees from many industries, job titles, and specialties.

## 8. Coalesce

*   **Date**: September 15-18, 2026.
*   **Location**: Hybrid - Las Vegas, Nevada, US, and available online.
*   **Cost**: $850 in-person. Free for virtual admission.

[Coalesce](https://coalesce.getdbt.com/) is a four-day conference that can be attended in person in Las Vegas or online. Last year’s sessions are available on [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL0QYlrC86xQnT3HLh-XgvoTf9F3lbsADf). This event covers various topics, including machine learning and AI, testing, data anomaly detection, data engineering, and more. It also provides great networking opportunities for those who attend in person.

## 9. World Summit AI

*   **Date**: October 7-8, 2026.
*   **Location**: Amsterdam, Netherlands.
*   **Cost**: From €349.

The [World Summit AI conference](https://worldsummit.ai/) is a large event hosted in Amsterdam. It will include keynote speakers and an expo. The conference will offer tracks on relevant topics like Generative AI, scaling startups with AI, and AI in finance and other industries. Save money with the Early Bird discount.

## 10. Big Data Conference Europe

*   **Date**: November 25-27, 2026.
*   **Location**: Hybrid - Vilnius, Lithuania, and available online.
*   **Cost**: €700-€1,350.

The [Big Data Europe conference](https://bigdataconference.eu/) includes in-person workshops on the first day and a hybrid-style conference over the next three days. This is the 10th time this conference has run, and the agenda includes important topics in AI like ChatGPT, deep learning, and predictive analytics. This conference hosts attendees from all backgrounds, both inside and outside Europe.

## 11. NVIDIA GTC

*   **Date**: March 16-19, 2026.
*   **Location**: San Jose, California, US.
*   **Cost**: $2,172-$2,525.

Often called the "Woodstock of AI," NVIDIA GTC is the premier event for developers and researchers building the infrastructure of the future. The conference features hundreds of sessions on generative AI, robotics, and industrial digitalization. The highlight is always the keynote by CEO Jensen Huang, which sets the technical direction for the entire industry for the coming year.

## 12. India AI Impact Summit

*   **Date**: February 15-20, 2026.
*   **Location**: New Delhi, India.
*   **Cost**: Free / Invite Only.

As a major new player on the global circuit, this government-led summit focuses on the role of the "Global South" in the AI revolution. It brings together heads of state, tech leaders, and researchers to discuss inclusive AI development and geopolitical strategy. This is a massive-scale event perfect for those interested in international policy and the expanding global footprint of artificial intelligence.

## 13. HumanX

*   **Date**: April 6-9, 2026.
*   **Location**: San Francisco, California, US.
*   **Cost**: $2,650-$3,995.

HumanX is rapidly becoming the "Davos of AI," focusing less on code and more on high-level strategy, safety, and regulation. This event gathers CEOs, policymakers, and industry titans to discuss how AI will reshape the global economy. It is an ideal venue for executives looking to understand the macro-trends driving the industry and to network with the people making the decisions.

# The Best Artificial Intelligence Conferences & Events of 2026

[Learn](https://www.splunk.com/en_us/blog/learn.html) [Samantha Dunn](/en_us/blog/author/sdunn.html) 

Artificial intelligence is continuing to shape the future of industries, from healthcare and finance to manufacturing and education. In 2026, experts, innovators, and thought leaders will gather at a host of conferences and events across the globe to share the latest trends, breakthroughs, and best practices in the field.

Whether you are looking to connect with AI professionals at an intimate regional meetup or attend a large-scale global summit, we have curated a comprehensive guide to the most anticipated AI conferences and events of 2026. These gatherings provide unparalleled opportunities to deepen your knowledge, network with key players, and explore emerging technologies.

If you have an AI conference or event that you’d like to see included in our list, please reach out via email to [sadunn@cisco.com](mailto:sadunn@cisco.com) with details, including the conference name, dates, location, and website.

Without further ado, here is your ultimate guide to the best AI conferences and events of 2026!

## AI conferences and events

### [AAAI 2026 (Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence)](https://aaai.org/conference/aaai/aaai-26/)

**Dates:** January 20-27, 2026  
**Location:** Singapore  
**Cost:** Check the website

AAAI 2026 brings together the global AI research community to discuss advances in artificial intelligence theory and practice. Experience a diverse program featuring technical papers, invited talks, workshops, and competitions. Network with leading academics and industry professionals while exploring the latest trends in AI.

### [Gartner Data & Analytics Summit 2026](https://www.gartner.com/en/conferences/na/data-analytics-us)

**Dates:** March 9-11, 2026  
**Location:** Orlando, Florida  
**Cost:** Starting at $4,475

The Gartner Data & Analytics Summit 2026 is a premier event for AI leaders and data professionals. Explore how AI, data, and analytics are converging to transform business strategies and drive innovation. Attend sessions on AI governance, generative AI, and data fabric, and gain actionable insights from Gartner analysts and industry experts.

### [NVIDIA GTC AI Conference](https://www.nvidia.com/gtc/)

**Dates:** March 16-19, 2026  
**Location:** San Jose, California  
**Cost:** Starting at $2,172

NVIDIA GTC 2026 is a premier global AI conference bringing together developers, researchers, business leaders, and innovators. Experience keynote addresses from NVIDIA’s leadership, technical sessions on cutting-edge AI, deep learning, and accelerated computing, as well as hands-on training and workshops. Whether you’re interested in breakthroughs in generative AI, robotics, or edge computing, GTC offers unparalleled opportunities to network and learn from industry trailblazers.

### [HumanX](https://www.humanx.co/)

**Dates:** April 6-9, 2026  
**Location:** San Francisco, California  
**Cost:** Starting at $2,150

HumanX 2026 brings together global leaders, innovators, and practitioners to explore the intersection of artificial intelligence and human potential. Dive into keynote sessions, interactive workshops, and networking opportunities focused on AI for good, human-centered design, and the ethical advancement of technology.

### [SANS AI Cybersecurity Summit 2026](https://www.sans.org/cyber-security-training-events/ai-summit-2026)

**Dates:** April 20-27, 2026  
**Location:** Arlington, Virginia + Virtual  
**Cost:** In-Person $525 (virtual is free)

For cybersecurity professionals eager to leverage artificial intelligence (AI) in their work, the SANS AI Cybersecurity Summit 2026 is a must-attend event. With hands-on workshops and live demos, this summit offers practical, real-world skills to integrate AI and machine learning (ML) into your cybersecurity tasks for greater efficiency and effectiveness.

From applying AI in incident response and cyber defense to defending and attacking AI systems, you will gain actionable strategies you can apply immediately. Attend in person in Arlington, or virtually, for an opportunity to learn from experts, network with peers, and take your cybersecurity practices to the next level.

### [AI Con USA](https://aiconusa.techwell.com/)

**Dates:** June 7-12, 2026  
**Location:** Seattle, Washington  
**Cost:** Starting at $1,795

Attend AI Con USA in Seattle to explore the latest in AI and machine learning. With keynotes, sessions on topics like generative AI and MLOps, and hands-on training, this event is perfect for professionals eager to stay ahead. Network with experts, discover innovative solutions at the Expo, and dive into the future of AI across industries like healthcare, finance, and more. Don’t miss the opportunity to advance your knowledge and career.

### [Databricks Data + AI Summit 2026](https://www.databricks.com/dataaisummit)

**Dates:** June 15-18, 2026  
**Location:** San Francisco, California  
**Cost:** TBD

The Databricks Data + AI Summit 2026 is a must-attend event for data professionals. Over four days, explore 700+ sessions, keynotes, and workshops on topics like data engineering, machine learning, Delta Lake, and Apache Spark™. Join 20,000+ peers to discover the latest AI technologies, gain hands-on training, and network with experts. Take your skills to the next level with certifications and connect with the global data and AI community.

### [The AI Summit London](https://london.theaisummit.com/)

**Dates:** June 10-11, 2026  
**Location:** London, England  
**Cost:** Starting at £2,399

The AI Summit London 2026, taking place June 10-11 at Tobacco Dock, is the flagship event of London Tech Week. With 300+ speakers and 100+ tech companies, explore AI’s real-world impact across industries. Network with 4,500+ attendees, discover cutting-edge solutions at the Expo, and attend workshops and masterclasses. Join us to accelerate AI-driven growth and connect with industry leaders shaping the future of AI.

### [SuperAI](https://www.superai.com/)

**Dates:** June 10-11, 2026  
**Location:** Marina Bay Sands, Singapore  
**Cost:** Starting at $399 (up to $2,999)

Join 10,000+ attendees at the SuperAI Conference 2025 in Singapore this June. With 150+ speakers, 1,500+ companies, and top AI partners like Microsoft, Google, and OpenAI, it’s the perfect place for networking and discovering the latest in AI innovation. Don’t miss your chance to connect with industry leaders and shape the future of AI — register now with the Early Bird discount.

### [AI World Congress 2026](https://aiconference.london/)

**Dates:** June 23-24, 2026  
**Location:** London, United Kingdom  
**Cost:** Starting at £495 for early bird pricing

The AI World Congress 2026 stands as Europe's premier gathering for senior level decision makers artificial intelligence and robotics innovation. This flagship event brings together global leaders, researchers, and enterprises exploring the convergence of AI and robotics technologies transforming industries worldwide. Major AI brands will be represented, with speakers from Anthropic, Microsoft, IBM, and more.

### [Ai4](https://ai4.io/vegas/)

**Dates:** August 4-6, 2026  
**Location:** Las Vegas, Nevada  
**Cost:** Starting at $1,695

Ai4 2025, North America’s largest AI event, brings together 12,000+ attendees from August 4-6 at The Venetian, Las Vegas. With 1,000+ speakers and 400+ exhibitors, explore the latest in AI innovation, including generative AI and AI agents. Network with leaders across industries and accelerate your AI initiatives at this must-attend event.

### [The AI Conference](https://aiconference.com/)

**Dates:** September 30 – October 1, 2026  
**Location:** San Francisco, California  
**Cost:** TBD

Attend the two day in-person AI Conference from September 30th to October 1st in San Francisco. This premier event explores key AI topics like AGI, generative AI, ethics, and startups. Engage with top AI experts, discover cutting-edge research, and network with pioneers shaping the future of AI. Don’t miss this opportunity to stay ahead in the evolving AI landscape.

### [AI Expo Europe 2026](https://www.ai-expo.net/europe/)

**Dates:** October 20-21, 2026  
**Location:** Amsterdam, Netherlands  
**Cost:** TBD

AI Expo Europe 2026 connects technology enthusiasts, business leaders, and developers at one of the continent’s largest AI events. Explore the latest solutions in machine learning, NLP, and AI-driven automation through talks, demos, and networking sessions. Discover how organizations are deploying AI to solve real-world challenges across industries.

## Wrapping up

Whether you choose to attend an in-person or virtual AI event in 2026, there are many options to choose from to learn the latest best practices and frameworks.

If you know of another great AI or machine learning conference we should add to the list, don’t hesitate to send a suggestion to the author of this post at [sadunn@cisco.com](mailto:sadunn@cisco.com) with details including the conference URL, conference name, dates of the event, and location details.

By 2033, the AI industry is expected [to reach $3.49 trillion](https://www.grandviewresearch.com/industry-analysis/artificial-intelligence-ai-market), with growth rates that force companies to move fast just to stay relevant. With over [$109 billion](https://hai.stanford.edu/ai-index/2025-ai-index-report) in private investment, roughly 12 times more than China and 24 times more than the UK, the infrastructure for 2026 is already being built. Moreover, US institutions are churning out dozens of world-leading models and agentic workflows that drive revenue.

For tech leaders, 2026 is the year of the “AI agent.” But the real challenge is making it work inside existing systems. This is why being on the ground at US events is of utmost importance for business owners. Visiting AI conferences means talking to peers who are dealing with the same integration headaches and security risks.

Networking at these summits is often the fastest way to validate a roadmap. Informal conversations between sessions usually provide the kind of raw, honest feedback you won’t find in a marketing brochure. In this article, we review the best AI conferences scheduled for 2026 in the USA. So, check it out and see which event will help you turn the AI hype into a functional part of your business strategy.

### Top AI Events in the US in 2026 That Are Worth Visiting

Below, you will find a detailed overview of the most popular AI conferences planned for 2026 in the United States. The real value of these events is getting early access to hands-on demos and vetting vendors before you commit your budget.

### NVIDIA GTC San Jose

* **Date:** Mar 16–19, 2026
* **Location:** San Jose, California, US
* **Cost:** From $2,172 (Virtual pass is free
* **Official Website:** [nvidia.com/gtc](https://www.nvidia.com/gtc/)

NVIDIA GTC is called the “Woodstock of AI.” It is organized by NVIDIA, the company that builds the powerful chips (GPUs) and software that most of the world’s AI runs on. The company’s main goal for this event is to announce its biggest hardware and software releases for the year. The conference focuses on “AI factories,” robotics, and the infrastructure required to run massive AI models.

**Who should attend:** This is a must for developers, data scientists, and IT infrastructure leads. It’s also very useful for CTOs who need to know what hardware they’ll need to buy in the next 18 months.

### HumanX San Francisco

* **Date:** Apr 6–9, 2026
* **Location:** San Francisco, California, US
* **Cost:** From $2,995
* **Official Website:** [humanx.co](https://www.humanx.co/)

This AI Summit is probably one of the best enterprise AI conferences, created by the teams behind Money20/20 and Shoptalk. HumanX is built specifically for business executives and policymakers. It’s held at the Moscone Center and aims to bridge the “implementation gap”. Simply put, it is supposed to help leaders move from vague AI interest to clear business results. The event is famous for its “SolutionBridge” program, which directly matches corporate buyers with vetted AI vendors. Plus, Peer change sessions are also planned. They allow executives to talk privately with others in the same role.

**Who should attend:** C-suite leaders (CEOs, CFOs, CMOs), founders, and board members who need to set the strategy should definitely visit HumanX. You’ll learn to figure out the ROI of your AI spend and understand new regulations.

### Gartner Data & Analytics Summit Orlando

* **Date:** March 9–11, 2026
* **Location:** Orlando, Florida, US
* **Cost:** From $4,175 – $4,925
* **Official Website:** <https://www.gartner.com/en/conferences/na/data-analytics-us>

This event focuses on shifts from experimental pilots to building a rock-solid data foundation. As a premier global AI conference, the summit provides CDOs and CIOs with the frameworks needed to validate budgets to the board and turn raw data into actual profit. The sessions will be about managing agentic systems and controlling the hidden costs of scaling.

**Who should attend:** CDOs, CIOs, and data leaders who need to balance aggressive deployment with strict security and corporate governance.

### IBM Think Boston

* **Date:** May 4–7, 2026
* **Location:** Boston, Massachusetts, US
* **Cost:** From $1,895
* **Official Website:** <https://www.ibm.com/events/think>

IBM Think Boston is the hub for industries like banking, healthcare, and energy. The event is about the “agentic leap”, moving past simple chatbots to autonomous AI agents that actually handle complex workflows within your hybrid cloud. You will attend sessions on governance, transparency, and building models that don’t hallucinate during an audit.

**Who should attend:** CTOs, Chief Risk Officers, and Enterprise Architects.

### Data + AI Summit San Francisco

* **Date:** June 15–18, 2026
* **Location:** San Francisco, California, US
* **Cost:** Early bird from $1,195
* Official Website: [databricks.com/dataaisummit](http://databricks.com/dataaisummit)

Forget high-level slide decks; this is a practitioner’s event hosted by Databricks. The theme is Data Intelligence, essentially the shift toward making your data pipelines smart enough to feed autonomous agents without manual work. When you visit this event, you will learn more about Lakehouse architecture, open-source tools such as Spark and Delta Lake, and the messy reality of MLOps. Over 800 powerful sessions are planned.

**Who should attend:** Data engineers, machine learning scientists, and architects.

### Ai4 Las Vegas

* **Date:** August 4–6, 2026
* **Location:** Las Vegas, Nevada, US
* **Cost:** From $1,695 (Free for qualifying enterprise buyers)
* **Official Website:** [ai4.io](https://ai4.io)

Ai4 is a giant 360-degree view of the market. It has become North America’s largest meeting point for the AI community, where you’ll find sessions for healthcare, finance, retail, and more. It’s the top event where business and tech leaders meet to shape the industry’s near future. You’ll join thousands of people to see how tech works in the real world, share what’s working, and explore the latest in AI systems. Simply put, it covers every corner of the market through hands-on workshops and live demos.

**Who should attend:** Business executives, product leads, procurement officers, and everyone looking for specific, industry-aligned solutions and building a network within the broader tech ecosystem.

### The AI Conference San Francisco

* **Date:** September 29 – October 1, 2026
* **Location:** San Francisco, California, US
* **Cost:** From $199
* **Official Website:** [aiconference.com](https://aiconference.com)

It is widely considered a premier event for those focused on applied research and the foundation model ecosystem. The summit gathers 5,500+ engineers and researchers who are interested in neural architectures. The 2026 agenda is split across tracks such as “The AI Stack” and “AI Frontiers,” with a heavy focus on the intersection of data engineering and model training. You’ll find technical sessions on distributed inference and edge computing, focusing on how to run massive models on local hardware. It’s a high-energy environment designed for shaping the future of AI.

**Who should attend:** AI engineers, tech leads, and researchers.

### Embedded Vision Summit

* **Date:** May 11–13, 2026
* **Location:** Santa Clara, California, US
* **Cost:** From $655
* **Official Website:** [embeddedvisionsummit.com](https://embeddedvisionsummit.com)

Other AI business events offer high-level talks about cloud software; this summit is all about Edge AI. It focuses on the practical challenge of making vision systems work on local hardware.

It is a good idea to arrive early for the technical workshops that cover how to optimize models so they don’t overheat or drain batteries. You’ll see many real-world applications on the demo floor that show how vision tech is being used in automotive safety and retail automation. It’s a very grounded, engineering-first environment where people talk more about hardware constraints than marketing hype.

**Who should attend:** Engineers and product managers building “embodied AI”.

## How to Choose the Best AI Conference in the USA

Many useful events are planned for 2026, but choosing the best AI conferences in America may be challenging. A lot depends on your requirements: finding new vendors, networking, training the team, etc. However, you should keep in mind that all these conferences offer a fantastic opportunity to gain insights directly from industry experts. You should focus on summits that match your business goals. Let’s review a few tips that might help you make the right choice:

* **For technical implementation:** If your team needs hands-on training and deep dives into the latest trends like agentic workflows, prioritize practitioner-focused events, and Data + AI Summit and The AI Conference are the best examples
* **For executive strategy:** If you need to justify a multi-million dollar budget to the board, the Gartner Data & Analytics summit provides the most objective frameworks for ROI and enterprise governance
* **For buying and networking:** Do you want to meet new vendors? Ai4 is the most efficient choice because of its industry-specific tracks
* **For niche hardware:** Companies building physical products should skip the general software shows and head straight to the Embedded Vision Summit. This decision will help you solve specific power and sensor constraints.

All in all, the best approach is to look at the roadmap for the next year, plan your travel and registration 3-6 months in advance, and prioritize summits that align with your team’s level.

## Enterprise AI Adoption and ROI Statistics for 2026

In 2026, investors and boards are looking for a clear line on the balance sheet showing how much money AI tools can make and save. Meeting with thought leaders and peers at these summits is the best way to see how the most successful firms are hitting their targets. These networking opportunities allow you to compare your progress against the high bar set by the current market leaders.

### Key performance and efficiency metrics

Looking at the latest statistics and scientific research, we can see that some companies are only testing AI, while others have already integrated it into their operations. Check out the stats below:

* [88% of organizations report](https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/quantumblack/our-insights/the-state-of-ai?utm_source) regularly using AI in at least one business function, up from 78% the previous year
* [23% of organizations are scaling agentic AI](https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/quantumblack/our-insights/the-state-of-ai) in at least one business function; another 39% are experimenting with AI agents
* Inference costs for systems operating at the GPT-3.5 level [fell by more than 280×](https://hai.stanford.edu/ai-index/2025-ai-index-report?utm_source); hardware costs decreased by 30% per year, and energy efficiency improved by 40% annually
* Companies using AI tools report an average [14% increase in productivity](https://www.nber.org/papers/w31161) across industries
* Entry-level employees improve their performance by [34% when using AI](https://www.nber.org/papers/w31161) to compensate for skill gaps
* Netflix saves around [$1 billion per year in customer retention](https://marketingino.com/the-netflix-recommendation-algorithm-how-personalization-drives-80-of-viewer-engagement/?utm_source) through its recommendation algorithms

### The 2026 AI labor market

The main reason why these conferences are so popular is the search for talent. The thing is that building an effective AI strategy requires decent experience and, of course, understanding the current pressure on the US labor market:

* Demand for prompt engineers grew by over [135% in 2025](https://www.glassdoor.com/Salaries/us-ai-engineer-salary-SRCH_IL.0,2_IN1_KO3,14.htm), and modern businesses look for people who know how to get real results from AI tools
* By 2030, [AI may replace 92 million jobs](https://www.weforum.org/press/2025/01/future-of-jobs-report-2025-78-million-new-job-opportunities-by-2030-but-urgent-upskilling-needed-to-prepare-workforces/), but it will also create 170 million new ones, mostly in higher-skilled fields. Clerical and admin roles will shrink the fastest
* Around [92% of students already use AI](https://www.hepi.ac.uk/reports/student-generative-ai-survey-2025/) in their studies, and enrollment in GenAI courses has jumped 195%
* AI specialists earn between [$112,000 and $178,000](https://www.glassdoor.com/Salaries/us-ai-engineer-salary-SRCH_IL.0,2_IN1_KO3,14.htm) a year on average, so we can see how valuable these skills have become
* Despite the hype, [63% of U.S. workers](https://www.pewresearch.org/social-trends/2025/02/25/u-s-workers-are-more-worried-than-hopeful-about-future-ai-use-in-the-workplace/?gad_source=1&gad_campaignid=22378837192&gbraid=0AAAAA-ddO9EXKcxgMdQxewVP_d6xEkFAk&gclid=CjwKCAiAwNDMBhBfEiwAd7ti1HcjrwodapbIO2weOwQY3i6N4Vsq9cwu9xyd2ns3EX50vjRu5BlcGhoCM3oQAvD_BwE) rarely or never use AI, and only 16% use it daily at work
* [Most users are under 50 (73%)](https://www.pewresearch.org/social-trends/2025/02/25/u-s-workers-are-more-worried-than-hopeful-about-future-ai-use-in-the-workplace/?gad_source=1&gad_campaignid=22378837192&gbraid=0AAAAA-ddO9EXKcxgMdQxewVP_d6xEkFAk&gclid=CjwKCAiAwNDMBhBfEiwAd7ti1HcjrwodapbIO2weOwQY3i6N4Vsq9cwu9xyd2ns3EX50vjRu5BlcGhoCM3oQAvD_BwE), and more than half have a bachelor’s degree

## Conclusion

The volume and depth of AI conferences in the US in 2026 show that the industry has moved past mere experimentation into a sophisticated era of agentic systems and industrial-scale integration. Whether you are aiming for the hardware-heavy sessions of NVIDIA GTC or the executive strategy rooms of HumanX, these events offer the essential high-level networking and technical validation required to thrive in a $3 trillion market.

Success this year won’t just come from attending the right summits, but from effectively translating those insights into a resilient, production-ready roadmap. ## FAQ

#### [What are the most popular AI conferences in the USA in 2026?](#1753794701933-5edbc9be-0051)

The largest summits that gather AI leaders from around the world include NVIDIA GTC in San Jose, HumanX in San Francisco, and Ai4 in Las Vegas. They are the best hubs for data strategy and governance.

#### [What makes NVIDIA GTC the "Woodstock of AI"?](#1753794701942-309762f2-6854)

This event marshals hardware architects, data science researchers, investors, and other professionals under one roof. Plus, its major announcements often set the tone for the year ahead. NVIDIA reveals the next generation of chips and software libraries that make training world models possible. If you want to see the physical limits of emerging technologies, you should definitely visit the summit.

#### [Which conference is best for C-suite leaders and CTOs?](#1753794757928-320dbbd9-ee4d)

HumanX and the Gartner Data & Analytics Summit are the top choices in this case. These conferences focus on the ROI of deploying AI and managing the transition to agentic AI.

#### [What is the Ai4 conference?](#1766055271383-546e2040-ff67)

It is the largest bridge between technical builders and business leaders. Ai4 organizes tracks by industry (Healthcare, Finance, Retail, etc.). If you need to see how deep learning is being applied in a specific commercial context, this event is for you. On top of that, it’s a massive networking hub for anyone looking to vet vendors or hire talent.

#### [Are there any high-value free or low-cost AI events?](#1766055287433-379bbedc-6206)

The major “boots on the ground” summits have high ticket prices, but NVIDIA GTC and the Data + AI Summit provide free digital access to their keynote speeches and certain sessions. So, it’s a good opportunity to experience AI breakthroughs and see what’s new without attending these events in person.

#### [Which 2026 events focus specifically on Generative AI and LLMs?](#1766055303535-44505197-2587)

The AI Conference in San Francisco is the most concentrated gathering for LLM development. In 2026, the focus is on agentic AI, systems that execute tasks. IBM Think is also a primary spot for seeing how these models are audited for enterprise use.

#### [How is HumanX different from a standard tech summit?](#1772014123328-d8926391-35da)

This summit is specifically structured to close the gap between emerging technologies and their implementation in the corporate world. Their “SolutionBridge” program acts like a matchmaking service, connecting government organizations and enterprises with the right tech partners.

#### [Where should developers and ML engineers go to sharpen their skills?](#1772821172854-2a3b1a72-7d16)

Data + AI Summit and The AI Conference offer the most technical depth. They usually offer many sessions on the mechanics of deep learning, model pruning, and the complexities of deploying AI. Plus, they attract professionals from leading research labs and tech companies, so you can meet interesting people who have solid experience in building AI tools.

#### [How does the US AI scene compare to Europe in 2026?](#1772821187327-a6b4e983-9811)

If we are talking about private investments or the commercialization of agentic AI, the US leads in 2026. American summits are for those who want to see the biggest hardware launches.

European events like VivaTech are worth attending, but the conversations there lean heavily toward policy, ethics, and the EU AI Act. So, the cultural difference is noticeable. The US ecosystem moves fast and prioritizes shipping, but Europe prioritizes governance. Both matter, but they are playing very different games right now.

#### [How do I choose which event to attend or sponsor?](#1772821203417-6ad320f9-d46d)

Start with your 2026 goal:

* **To Build:** Choose practitioner-heavy events like the Data + AI Summit.
* **To Lead:** Send your C-suite to HumanX or Gartner.
* **To Sell:** Sponsor Ai4 for the sheer volume of industry-diverse buyers. For government organizations or highly regulated sectors, IBM Think provides the most relevant focus.

When you’re ready to move past the ‘demo’ phase, Glorium Technologies can help you turn AI concepts into functional software.

# 23 AI Conferences Worth Attending in 2026: Complete Guide with Dates, Locations, and What to Expect

ALM Corp

Artificial intelligence has moved beyond experimental technology into production systems that process billions of transactions daily. In 2026, AI conferences serve as critical venues where academic breakthroughs meet enterprise deployment, where regulatory frameworks take shape, and where the next generation of AI systems debuts. This guide examines 23 conferences scheduled for 2026, analyzing what each offers based on confirmed speakers, published agendas, and historical attendance data.

The AI conference landscape in 2026 reflects the technology’s maturation. Events now segment into distinct categories: academic conferences where papers undergo peer review and establish the theoretical foundations that power tomorrow’s applications, enterprise summits where organizations share deployment experiences and ROI measurements, developer-focused gatherings centered on model architecture and training techniques, and policy forums where governance frameworks emerge.

## Understanding the 2026 AI Conference Ecosystem

The conference circuit in 2026 addresses specific stages of AI development and deployment. Academic conferences like NeurIPS, ICML, and ICLR function as peer-reviewed venues where researchers present novel architectures, training methodologies, and benchmark results that inform commercial development cycles. These events typically see paper submission six months before the conference date, with acceptance rates between 20-30 percent indicating rigorous quality standards.

Enterprise conferences focus on business value, featuring case studies from organizations that have moved AI from pilot projects to production systems. Speakers at these events present metrics on operational efficiency gains, cost reductions, and revenue impacts. The content addresses implementation challenges including data quality issues, model governance, regulatory compliance, and workforce adaptation.

Developer conferences provide hands-on training in specific frameworks, model architectures, and deployment tools. These events feature workshops where participants build and fine-tune models, optimize inference performance, and integrate AI capabilities into existing applications. The technical depth makes these conferences valuable for practitioners implementing AI systems.

## Major Academic AI Conferences 2026

### AAAI-26: Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence

**Dates:** January 20-27, 2026  
 **Location:** Singapore EXPO, Singapore  
 **Registration:** Pricing available through the official registration portal  
 **Paper Submission Deadline:** July 26, 2025 (abstract registration)

The Fortieth AAAI Conference represents one of the longest-running AI research conferences globally. AAAI-26 expects to receive over 9,000 paper submissions across AI subfields including machine learning, natural language processing, computer vision, robotics, and knowledge representation. The conference’s OpenReview submission system enables transparent peer review, with accepted papers published in AAAI proceedings.

The technical program spans eight days, including workshops and tutorials on specialized topics like multi-agent systems, explainable AI, and reinforcement learning. Singapore’s position as an AI research hub makes it accessible for researchers from Asia-Pacific regions while maintaining strong participation from North America and Europe. Past AAAI conferences have averaged 8,000-10,000 attendees, making it one of the largest academic AI gatherings.

AAAI workshops cover emerging areas before they develop into full conference tracks. The 2026 edition includes workshops on foundation models, AI safety, and applications in healthcare and climate science. Tutorial sessions provide structured learning for researchers entering new subfields.

### ICLR 2026: International Conference on Learning Representations

**Dates:** April 23-27, 2026  
 **Location:** Riocentro Convention and Event Center, Rio de Janeiro, Brazil  
 **Registration:** Starting at $250 for early registration (student rate); virtual attendance from $100  
 **Paper Submission Deadline:** September 20, 2025 (abstract registration)

ICLR has established itself as the premier venue for representation learning research, the subfield that produces the architectures powering large language models, vision transformers, and multimodal systems. The conference uses OpenReview for its entire process, making all submitted papers, reviews, and author responses publicly accessible. This transparency creates higher accountability in peer review.

The 2026 program location in Rio de Janeiro marks ICLR’s expansion into Latin America, a region with growing AI research capacity. The conference program includes oral presentations for top-scoring papers, poster sessions for all accepted papers, and workshops exploring cutting-edge topics. Workshops scheduled for April 26-27 cover areas like efficient neural architectures, AI safety, and learning with limited data.

ICLR’s impact extends beyond paper presentations. The conference serves as a recruiting venue where PhD students and postdocs connect with industry research labs. Companies including OpenAI, Google DeepMind, Meta AI Research, and Anthropic typically maintain strong presence, conducting interviews and presenting their latest research directions.

### CVPR 2026: Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition

**Dates:** June 3-7, 2026 (workshops/tutorials June 3-4; main conference June 5-7)  
 **Location:** Denver Convention Center, Denver, Colorado  
 **Registration:** Starting at $900 for non-member visitors  
 **Paper Submission Deadline:** November 14, 2025 (papers); November 21, 2025 (supplementary materials)

CVPR represents the flagship conference for computer vision research. The 2026 edition expects submissions across image recognition, object detection, semantic segmentation, video understanding, 3D reconstruction, and generative models for visual content. Recent years have seen acceptance of 2,000-3,000 papers from 10,000+ submissions, indicating both the field’s growth and competitive selection standards.

The conference features separate deadlines for paper submissions and supplementary materials, allowing authors to include additional results, visualizations, or code that supports their main paper. The review process uses double-blind evaluation, with area chairs overseeing consistency across reviewers.

Industry participation at CVPR reflects computer vision’s commercial importance. Autonomous vehicle companies, social media platforms, smartphone manufacturers, and robotics firms present research and recruit talent. The exhibition floor showcases commercial vision systems, annotation tools, and compute infrastructure. Workshops cover specialized topics including medical imaging, satellite imagery analysis, and fair and responsible computer vision.

### ICML 2026: International Conference on Machine Learning

**Dates:** July 6-12, 2026 (July 6: Expo/Tutorial Day; July 7-9: Main Conference; July 10-12: Workshops)  
 **Location:** COEX Convention & Exhibition Center, Seoul, South Korea  
 **Registration:** Pricing to be announced  
 **Paper Submission Deadline:** January 28, 2026 (papers); February 4, 2026 (supplementary materials)

ICML stands alongside NeurIPS and ICLR as one of three premier machine learning research conferences. The 2026 conference in Seoul brings the event to Asia, where significant ML research capacity has developed. The technical program covers supervised and unsupervised learning, reinforcement learning, optimization, probabilistic models, and theoretical ML.

The conference structure includes tutorials on day one, providing structured introductions to areas like causal inference, graph neural networks, or optimization theory. The main conference features oral presentations highlighting exceptional papers and poster sessions where authors present to smaller groups. Workshops on days five through seven cover specialized topics and emerging areas not yet mature enough for main conference tracks.

ICML’s Journal-to-Conference track allows papers published in select ML journals to be presented at the conference, increasing paper visibility and enabling authors to network with the conference community. This innovation addresses the growing challenge of disseminating research across multiple venues.

### NeurIPS 2026: Neural Information Processing Systems
