## Emerging Focus Areas in 2026 AI Conferences

### Agentic AI and Autonomous Systems

The 2026 conference circuit reflects growing interest in agentic AI – systems that can plan multi-step workflows, use tools, and execute tasks with minimal human intervention. This represents evolution beyond language models that generate text to systems that can accomplish goals.

AI Agent Conference 2026 in New York specifically addresses this capability level, examining architectures for goal-directed behavior, tool use and API integration, multi-agent coordination, and making agent behavior interpretable. Sessions cover practical implementation including defining agent objectives, constraining agent behavior to prevent unintended actions, and evaluating whether agents successfully accomplish tasks.

NVIDIA GTC emphasizes “Physical AI” – applying agentic capabilities to robots and autonomous vehicles that must perceive environments, plan actions, and execute movements safely. This extends AI from digital environments into physical systems with real-world consequences.

The model context protocol (MCP) emerges as a framework for agent-to-agent communication, enabling specialized agents to collaborate on complex tasks. Conference sessions examine MCP implementation, agent orchestration patterns, and managing workflows across multiple AI systems.

### AI Governance and Regulatory Compliance

The EU AI Act’s implementation creates compliance requirements that many 2026 conferences address. The regulation classifies AI systems by risk level, with high-risk systems facing requirements for documentation, testing, human oversight, and accuracy thresholds.

Generative AI Summit London dedicates sessions to EU AI Act compliance, examining which systems qualify as high-risk, documentation requirements, conformity assessment procedures, and penalties for non-compliance. Organizations deploying AI in Europe require this guidance to ensure legal operation.

IAPP Global Summit (privacy and AI governance) addresses data protection alongside AI governance, examining GDPR compliance for AI systems, documentation of data usage, individual rights regarding automated decisions, and cross-border data transfer requirements.

Governance sessions across conferences examine practical implementation: establishing AI ethics boards, reviewing systems for bias before deployment, monitoring deployed systems for drift in behavior, and documenting decisions to demonstrate compliance.

### Multimodal AI and Foundation Models

The evolution from language-only models to systems that process text, images, audio, and video creates new capabilities that 2026 conferences explore. CVPR dedicates sessions to vision-language models that can understand images and answer questions about them, generate images from text descriptions, or perform visual reasoning.

Foundation models that can be adapted to diverse tasks through prompting or fine-tuning represent a shift from task-specific models to general-purpose systems. Conferences examine when to use foundation models versus training specialized models, how to evaluate foundation model capabilities, and techniques for adapting them to specific domains.

The multimodal track at conferences like ICML and NeurIPS examines architectural challenges in processing different data modalities, alignment techniques that connect visual and textual representations, and benchmarks for evaluating multimodal understanding.

### Sustainable AI and Environmental Considerations

Training large AI models consumes significant energy, creating environmental impact that conferences increasingly address. The AI Summit London’s Sustainable AI Innovation track examines techniques for reducing training costs including efficient architectures, better optimization algorithms, and hardware improvements.

Sessions cover measuring AI’s environmental impact using metrics like CO2 emissions per training run, carbon-aware computing that schedules training when clean energy is available, model efficiency techniques including pruning and quantization, and comparing AI’s energy consumption to benefits it enables.

Presentations examine tradeoffs between model size and capability, approaches to achieving specific performance targets with minimal parameters, and whether techniques like transfer learning and few-shot learning can reduce training requirements.

## Maximizing Conference Value: Strategic Attendance Planning

### Selecting Conferences Aligned with Objectives

Different conferences serve distinct objectives. Professionals should align conference selection with their current priorities and career stage.

Academic researchers targeting paper publication should prioritize NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, CVPR, or AAAI based on their subfield. These conferences provide feedback on research through the review process, even for rejected papers. Accepted papers bring visibility within the research community and enhance academic credentials.

ML engineers and data scientists implementing AI systems benefit most from developer-focused conferences like AI DevWorld, AI DevSummit, and NVIDIA GTC. These events provide practical skills through hands-on workshops, exposure to latest tools and frameworks, and networking with practitioners solving similar problems.

Business executives evaluating AI strategy should attend enterprise-focused conferences like HumanX, AI4, or Gartner’s Data & Analytics Summit. These events present AI’s business value through case studies with actual metrics, address governance and compliance considerations, and facilitate networking with executives at similar career levels.

Startup founders seeking investment or partnerships benefit from conferences with strong venture capital attendance like AI4’s startup track, SuperAI’s startup competition, or World Summit AI’s investor networking. These venues provide exposure to potential investors and partners.

### Pre-Conference Preparation Strategies

Effective conference attendance begins weeks before the event. Reviewing the published agenda and speaker list enables identifying priority sessions and potential networking contacts. Many conferences publish paper preprints or presentation abstracts weeks early, allowing advance reading of key research.

For academic conferences, reading accepted papers in advance enables asking informed questions during presentations and identifying researchers working on related problems. This preparation transforms poster sessions from random browsing to targeted networking with specific researchers.

Enterprise conferences often publish speaker bios and company information. Researching speakers’ organizations and their AI initiatives provides context for understanding presentations and formulating relevant questions.

Setting specific conference objectives—whether learning particular technical skills, identifying potential vendors, recruiting talent, or establishing partnerships—focuses attention on highest-value activities. Without clear objectives, the overwhelming number of sessions and attendees can lead to unfocused attendance.

### Session Selection and Scheduling

Most conferences run parallel tracks, requiring selection from competing sessions. Priority should go to sessions aligned with conference objectives, presentations by recognized experts, and talks covering novel topics without widely available alternatives.

Academic conferences typically designate oral presentations for highest-scoring papers, making these sessions high priority for staying current with research frontiers. Poster sessions enable direct conversation with authors about their work, often more valuable than listening to brief oral presentations.

Workshop sessions at academic conferences provide structured learning on specialized topics, valuable when entering new subfields. Tutorial sessions offer introductions to areas like reinforcement learning, causal inference, or graph neural networks from researchers who established the fields.

Enterprise conferences often schedule keynotes and featured sessions during general session times to ensure full attendance. These sessions typically announce major initiatives or present synthesis of industry trends. Breakout sessions enable diving deeper into specific topics with smaller audiences.

Building session schedules should include transition time between sessions, networking breaks, and exhibit hall time. Overscheduling creates rushed attendance without time to process information or connect with other attendees.

### Networking Strategies and Tactics

Conferences provide unique networking opportunities that don’t exist in normal work contexts. The concentration of professionals with shared interests creates high-bandwidth interaction rare in distributed work environments.

Academic conferences enable connecting with researchers whose papers you’ve read, discussed in reading groups, or cited in your work. Introducing yourself after presentations or at poster sessions initiates relationships that can evolve into collaborations, PhD student mentorship, or job opportunities.

Enterprise conferences facilitate meeting peers facing similar challenges. Structured networking sessions, roundtable discussions, or simply conversations during breaks surface shared problems and potential solutions. Many attendees find these informal discussions as valuable as formal sessions.

Vendor exhibitions provide concentrated access to product demonstrations and sales engineers who can answer detailed technical questions. Rather than scheduling individual vendor calls over months, conference expo time enables evaluating multiple solutions in days.

Social events including conference receptions, dinners, and parties create informal settings conducive to relationship building. These events often feature conversations that wouldn’t occur during formal sessions.

Effective networking requires clear introduction of who you are and what you’re interested in, preparation to describe your work concisely, genuine interest in others’ work rather than merely promoting your own, and follow-up after conferences to maintain relationships.

### Post-Conference Actions and Follow-Through

Conference value extends beyond attendance through deliberate follow-up. Organizing notes while information remains fresh, sharing learnings with colleagues who didn’t attend, and following up with contacts met sustain conference momentum.

Many conferences provide recordings of keynotes and sessions, enabling review of sessions you attended and catching sessions you missed. Reviewing recordings while taking detailed notes creates lasting reference material.

Research papers from academic conferences become available through conference proceedings or preprint servers. Reading papers related to your work in detail, implementing described techniques, and building on published methods extends conference value beyond the event itself.

Following up with networking contacts within days of conference end maintains relationship momentum. Brief emails referencing specific conversation topics, sharing promised information, or proposing specific next steps convert conference introductions into sustained relationships.

Implementing techniques learned at conferences demonstrates value to organizations and advances personal capabilities. Whether that’s trying new model architectures, adopting governance frameworks, or piloting AI applications, implementation converts conference attendance from consumption to productive capability.

## Conference Trends Shaping the 2026 Landscape

### Hybrid Event Formats Become Standard

The 2026 conference landscape includes hybrid options at most major events. NVIDIA GTC offers full online access free, enabling global participation without travel costs. SANS AI Cybersecurity Summit provides in-person attendance at $525 while offering free virtual access, acknowledging different organizational travel policies.

Hybrid formats expand access to conference content while maintaining in-person networking value. Virtual attendees can watch keynotes, access session recordings, and sometimes participate in Q&A, while missing informal networking that creates lasting professional relationships.

Conference organizers balance hybrid elements to preserve in-person value while expanding reach. Some provide livestreamed keynotes and selected sessions while keeping workshops and networking in-person only. Others create dedicated virtual networking platforms attempting to facilitate online connections.

### Increased Emphasis on Practical Implementation

The 2026 conference circuit shows evolution from AI capabilities to AI deployment challenges. Sessions increasingly address topics like calculating AI ROI, managing change as AI transforms workflows, governing AI to prevent harmful outputs, and measuring AI system performance in production.

This shift reflects AI’s maturation from research topic to production technology. Organizations moving beyond pilots to scaled deployment encounter challenges around data quality, model governance, integration with existing systems, and workforce adaptation that conferences now address.

Case study sessions present actual implementation experiences with documented results. Speakers share specific metrics achieved, challenges encountered, solutions developed, and lessons learned. This contrasts with vendor presentations emphasizing capabilities without acknowledging implementation complexity.

### Specialization and Vertical Focus

General AI conferences continue alongside increasing specialization. Industry-specific events address AI applications in healthcare, financial services, manufacturing, retail, or government, examining how AI addresses sector-specific challenges and regulatory requirements.

Technical specialization creates events focused on computer vision, natural language processing, reinforcement learning, or AI infrastructure. This enables deeper technical content than general conferences can provide.

Role-specific conferences address distinct audiences: AI4 for business executives, AI DevWorld for developers, Gartner’s summit for data and analytics leaders, and academic conferences for researchers. This segmentation enables content appropriate for different expertise levels and decision-making authority.

### Global Distribution of Events

The 2026 conference calendar reflects AI’s global nature, with major events across North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific, and emerging presence in Latin America and Middle East. ICLR’s Brazil location, ICML’s Seoul venue, and SuperAI’s Singapore setting demonstrate expansion beyond traditional North American and European locations.

Regional conferences address local considerations including regulatory frameworks varying by jurisdiction, languages requiring specialized NLP models, data residency requirements affecting cloud deployment, and business models adapted to local market conditions.

Geographic distribution enables attendance without long-distance travel, expanding participation especially for practitioners with limited travel budgets. It also surfaces regional AI research and innovation that might receive less visibility at North America or Europe-centered events.

## Practical Conference Considerations

### Registration and Early Planning

Most conferences offer tiered pricing with significant discounts for early registration. AI4’s early bird pricing starts at $1,395 versus standard pricing reaching higher levels. CVPR and other academic conferences typically discount registration months before the event.

Booking accommodations early prevents higher rates as conference attendees fill nearby hotels. Conference organizers often negotiate room blocks at nearby hotels, providing guaranteed availability and rates.

Many conferences sell out weeks before the event, particularly smaller specialized summits with capped attendance. Monitoring registration status and registering promptly prevents missing desired conferences.

### Travel and Logistics Planning

International conferences require passport validity, potential visa applications, and understanding local requirements. ICLR’s Brazil location and ICML’s Seoul venue involve visa processes for many attendees, requiring lead time for applications.

Conference venues in major cities typically offer good airport connections, hotel options at various price points, and restaurant choices. Smaller conference locations may require rental cars or understanding local transit systems.

Planning extra days for exploring host cities, meeting local colleagues, or recovering from jet lag improves conference experience. Arriving exhausted and leaving immediately after the final session misses networking opportunities and creates stressful experience.

### Budget Considerations and Cost Management

Conference costs extend beyond registration to include travel, accommodation, meals, and lost work time. Enterprise conferences like Gartner’s summit with £3,750 registration create significant total costs including flights and hotels.

Academic conferences typically cost less, with student rates starting around $250-300 for early registration. Some conferences waive registration fees for accepted paper authors, offsetting costs for researchers presenting work.

Virtual attendance options dramatically reduce costs by eliminating travel and accommodation expenses. While missing in-person networking, virtual attendance enables accessing conference content and learning from presentations at fraction of in-person cost.

Some organizations have conference attendance budgets or professional development funds covering registration and travel. Others require demonstrating conference relevance to job responsibilities and committing to sharing learnings with colleagues upon return.

### Employer-Sponsored Attendance

Proposing conference attendance to employers requires clearly articulating expected value. Specifics help: “This conference has sessions on deploying large language models in production, which directly applies to our customer service automation project” proves more compelling than “This is an important AI conference.”

Identifying specific sessions aligned with current projects or initiatives, explaining how conference learnings will advance organizational objectives, and committing to sharing knowledge through presentations or documentation increases approval likelihood.

Some organizations require selection of lower-cost options when available. Demonstrating investigation of alternatives—”Conference X costs $2,000 registration plus travel, while Conference Y offers similar content for $1,500 registration plus minimal travel since it’s local”—shows cost consciousness.

## Emerging Conference Topics for Late 2026 and Beyond

### Reasoning AI and Chain-of-Thought

Advanced AI systems are developing capabilities for multi-step reasoning, breaking complex problems into steps, evaluating intermediate results, and adapting approaches based on those results. This “chain-of-thought” reasoning represents evolution beyond pattern matching to logical inference.

Future conferences will examine architectures enabling reasoning, techniques for training reasoning capabilities, and evaluating whether AI systems truly reason versus memorizing reasoning patterns. This capability level would enable AI handling novel situations beyond training distribution.

### Multimodal Foundation Models

Systems that process text, images, audio, and video together create new application possibilities: AI that can watch instructional videos and answer questions, systems that can generate videos from text descriptions, or assistants that can understand environment through vision and respond via speech.

Conferences will address architectural challenges in multimodal processing, alignment techniques that connect representations across modalities, and applications uniquely enabled by multimodal understanding versus separate single-modality systems.

### AI Safety and Alignment

As AI systems become more capable and autonomous, ensuring they behave in alignment with human values becomes critical. Conference discussions will examine technical approaches to AI alignment, frameworks for defining desired behavior, methods for detecting misalignment before deployment, and governance structures for oversight.

The academic conferences including NeurIPS, ICML, and ICLR already feature workshops on AI safety. Growing capabilities will likely expand safety from workshop topic to full conference track or dedicated conference.

### Edge AI and Efficient Models

Running AI on devices rather than cloud servers creates requirements for models operating under strict latency, memory, and power constraints. Conference content will expand around model compression, quantization, neural architecture search for efficiency, and specialized hardware for edge deployment.

Applications including autonomous vehicles, robotics, smartphone features, and IoT devices drive requirements for efficient models. Conferences will showcase techniques achieving strong performance within resource constraints.

## Industry-Specific Conference Tracks

### Healthcare and Life Sciences

AI conferences increasingly feature dedicated healthcare tracks examining clinical applications, drug discovery, medical imaging, genomics, and healthcare operations. These sessions address unique healthcare challenges including regulatory approval for diagnostic AI, clinical validation requirements, and liability considerations.

Organizations including hospitals, pharmaceutical companies, medical device manufacturers, and healthcare payers attend to learn how peers apply AI, understand regulatory landscape, and identify solution providers.

### Financial Services

Banking, insurance, and asset management applications of AI create specialized conference content around fraud detection, credit risk modeling, algorithmic trading, customer service automation, and regulatory compliance including model explainability requirements.

Financial services sessions examine balancing AI’s capabilities with regulatory requirements, managing risk when AI systems make high-stakes decisions, and explaining AI decisions to auditors and regulators.

### Manufacturing and Supply Chain

AI applications in manufacturing include predictive maintenance, quality control, process optimization, and autonomous vehicles in facilities. Supply chain applications span demand forecasting, inventory optimization, and logistics planning.

Conference sessions present case studies from manufacturers deploying AI on factory floors, address integration with existing industrial control systems, and examine ROI from specific applications.

### Retail and E-commerce

Retail AI applications include demand forecasting, dynamic pricing, personalized recommendations, inventory optimization, and customer service automation. E-commerce specifically adds applications in search, product discovery, and fraud detection.

Conference content examines competitive dynamics as AI becomes standard, measuring AI’s impact on key metrics like conversion rates or customer lifetime value, and balancing personalization with privacy.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**What are the most important AI conferences to attend in 2026?**

The most important conference depends on your role and objectives. For academic researchers, NeurIPS (December 6-12, Sydney), ICML (July 6-12, Seoul), ICLR (April 23-27, Rio de Janeiro), and CVPR (June 3-7, Denver) represent top-tier venues for publishing and learning about research advances. For business executives focused on enterprise AI deployment, HumanX (April 6-9, San Francisco), AI4 (August 4-6, Las Vegas), and Gartner Data & Analytics Summit (May 11-13, London) provide business-focused content with ROI case studies and implementation frameworks. For developers and ML engineers building AI systems, NVIDIA GTC (March 16-19, San Jose), AI DevWorld (February 18-20, San Jose), and Databricks Data + AI Summit (June 15-18, San Francisco) offer technical depth and hands-on training.

**How much do AI conferences typically cost in 2026?**

AI conference costs vary significantly by conference type and format. Academic conferences like ICLR, ICML, CVPR, and NeurIPS typically charge $250-900 for registration, with student rates starting around $250 and early registration discounts available. Enterprise conferences cost more, with Gartner’s summit starting at £3,750 ($5,046), HumanX at $2,650 for all-access passes, and AI4 starting at $1,395 early bird pricing. Developer conferences range from $1,000-2,500. Several conferences including NVIDIA GTC offer free virtual attendance, enabling content access without travel costs. Total conference costs including travel, accommodation, and meals often exceed registration fees, particularly for international conferences requiring flights and multi-night hotel stays.

**Can I attend AI conferences virtually in 2026?**

Many 2026 conferences offer virtual or hybrid attendance options. NVIDIA GTC provides full online access free, making keynotes and technical sessions accessible globally. SANS AI Cybersecurity Summit offers in-person attendance at $525 but provides free virtual access. Academic conferences including ICLR offer virtual registration typically costing $100-150, significantly less than in-person attendance. Virtual attendance provides access to presentations, often through livestreaming and recorded sessions, but typically misses networking opportunities, workshops, and expo access that make in-person attendance valuable. For those with budget constraints, limited travel approval, or scheduling conflicts, virtual attendance enables accessing conference content at substantially lower cost.

**What is the difference between academic and enterprise AI conferences?**

Academic conferences like NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, and CVPR focus on novel research contributions that advance AI’s theoretical foundations and capabilities. Papers undergo rigorous peer review, with acceptance rates typically 20-30 percent. Content emphasizes new model architectures, training algorithms, theoretical analysis, and benchmark results. Attendees primarily include PhD students, professors, and industry researchers. Enterprise conferences like HumanX, AI4, and Gartner’s summits focus on AI’s business applications and deployment. Content features case studies with ROI metrics, implementation frameworks, governance approaches, and vendor solutions. Attendees include executives, business leaders, data scientists, and practitioners deploying AI in organizations. Academic conferences advance the field’s knowledge frontier, while enterprise conferences address practical deployment at scale.

**How do I choose which AI conference to attend?**

Conference selection should align with your objectives, role, and career stage. First, clarify your primary objective: learning about research advances, acquiring technical implementation skills, understanding business applications and ROI, networking with specific communities, presenting your work, or recruiting talent. Next, match conference focus to objective: academic conferences for research, developer conferences for technical skills, enterprise conferences for business focus. Consider your expertise level—some conferences assume significant technical background while others provide introductory content. Review conference agendas, speaker lists, and past year presentations to assess content relevance. Factor in budget including registration, travel, and accommodation costs. Finally, consider timing and location—can you attend at the scheduled date, and is travel feasible?

**Are AI conferences worth the cost and time investment?**

AI conferences provide value that’s difficult to replicate through other means, though ROI varies by individual circumstances. For researchers, conferences enable presenting work for feedback, staying current with research trends, and networking with potential collaborators or employers. Many researchers consider major conference publication essential for academic career advancement. For practitioners, conferences provide concentrated technical learning from experts, exposure to tools and techniques that can be applied immediately, and networking with peers solving similar problems. For executives, conferences offer business-focused perspectives on AI’s value, implementation frameworks, and connections with leaders at other organizations. The concentrated nature of conferences—dozens of relevant sessions over days, hundreds of relevant attendees in one location—creates high-bandwidth learning and networking impossible to replicate through distributed activities. However, virtual attendance options and recorded presentations enable accessing content at substantially lower cost for those where budget is primary concern.

**What should I do to prepare before attending an AI conference?**

Effective conference preparation begins weeks before the event. Review the published agenda and identify priority sessions aligned with your objectives. Read speaker bios and research their organizations to understand context for their presentations. For academic conferences, read accepted papers relevant to your work—most are available through preprint servers weeks before conferences. Set specific conference objectives whether that’s learning particular skills, meeting specific people, identifying vendor solutions, or establishing partnerships. Research attendees if the conference provides attendee lists or directories, identifying people you want to meet. Prepare your introduction—a concise explanation of who you are and what you do that can be delivered in 30 seconds. Arrange meetings with people you specifically want to connect with rather than relying entirely on chance encounters. Finally, plan logistics including booking accommodations, arranging travel, understanding local transportation, and scheduling any extra days for exploration or recovery from travel.

**How can I maximize networking value at AI conferences?**

Effective conference networking requires intentional approach rather than passive attendance. Before the conference, identify specific people you want to meet based on their work, role, or organization. Use conference apps or attendee directories to locate them, and send brief messages proposing meetups. During sessions, arrive early and sit near aisles where conversation is easier during breaks. Ask thoughtful questions during Q&A, making yourself visible to speakers and audience. Visit poster sessions at academic conferences, enabling direct conversation with researchers about their work. Attend social events including receptions and dinners where informal setting facilitates relationship building. In conversations, focus on learning about others’ work rather than only promoting your own. Take brief notes after conversations including names, topics discussed, and follow-up actions. Exchange contact information and connect on professional networks like LinkedIn. After the conference, follow up within days while momentum remains. Send brief messages referencing specific conversation points, share promised information, or propose next steps. These follow-up actions convert conference introductions into sustained professional relationships.

**What are the major AI trends that will be featured at 2026 conferences?**

The 2026 conference landscape reflects several major AI trends. Agentic AI—systems that can plan multi-step workflows, use tools, and execute complex tasks with minimal human intervention—features prominently at conferences including NVIDIA GTC, AI4, and specialized events like AI Agent Conference. This represents evolution from AI that generates text to AI that accomplishes goals. Multimodal foundation models that process text, images, audio, and video together enable applications from video understanding to generation. Conferences including CVPR, ICLR, and ICML feature research on multimodal architectures and applications. AI governance and regulatory compliance receive increased attention given the EU AI Act’s implementation, with sessions addressing compliance requirements, governance frameworks, and responsible AI practices. Efficient AI and sustainability address the environmental impact of training large models, with techniques for reducing computational requirements. RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) that grounds language models in proprietary data enables enterprise applications, featured at developer conferences. Finally, AI’s business value and ROI measurement appear across enterprise conferences as organizations move from pilots to scaled deployment.

**What are the submission deadlines for academic AI conferences in 2026?**

Academic conferences have submission deadlines months before the conference date. AAAI-26 (January 20-27, Singapore) has abstract registration deadline July 26, 2025, with paper submission shortly after. ICLR 2026 (April 23-27, Rio de Janeiro) has abstract registration September 20, 2025 and full paper submission September 27, 2025. CVPR 2026 (June 3-7, Denver) has paper submission deadline November 14, 2025 with supplementary materials due November 21, 2025. ICML 2026 (July 6-12, Seoul) has paper deadline January 28, 2026 and supplementary materials February 4, 2026. NeurIPS 2026 (December 6-12, Sydney) has submission deadline May 11, 2026. Researchers planning to submit should note these deadlines require completed research months before conferences, accounting for several weeks of review, author response periods, and final decision communication. Most conferences use OpenReview or similar platforms for submission and review management.

**How do hybrid AI conferences work in 2026?**

Hybrid conferences combine in-person attendance with virtual access, attempting to provide broad access while maintaining in-person networking value. Implementation varies by conference. NVIDIA GTC provides full online access free, livestreaming keynotes and sessions while offering recordings shortly after. In-person attendees access hands-on labs, networking events, and expo demonstrations not available virtually. SANS AI Cybersecurity Summit charges $525 for in-person attendance but provides free virtual access to sessions, acknowledging different organizational travel policies. Academic conferences like ICLR typically offer virtual registration around $100 with access to recorded talks but not workshops or poster sessions. Virtual attendees can often participate in Q&A through online platforms. Some conferences create dedicated virtual networking spaces attempting to facilitate online connections, though these typically provide limited value compared to in-person interaction. The hybrid model enables accessing conference content globally but recognizes that networking, hands-on workshops, and informal interactions that create lasting professional relationships remain largely in-person experiences.

**What technical background is needed to benefit from AI conferences?**

Required technical background varies significantly by conference type and specific sessions. Academic conferences like NeurIPS, ICML, and ICLR assume strong mathematics background including linear algebra, calculus, probability theory, and statistics, plus familiarity with machine learning fundamentals. Paper presentations often dive into technical details requiring this background for comprehension. Developer conferences like AI DevWorld and NVIDIA GTC assume programming proficiency and practical ML experience but emphasize implementation over theory. Workshops and tutorials at these events provide hands-on training requiring ability to code in Python and use ML frameworks. Enterprise conferences like HumanX and AI4 require less technical background, focusing on business applications, implementation frameworks, and ROI measurement accessible to executives and business leaders without deep technical expertise. Many conferences offer tutorial sessions, workshops, or introductory tracks providing background for those entering the field. Reviewing conference agendas and past presentations helps assess whether content matches your current knowledge level.

**Can startups and small companies benefit from attending AI conferences?**

AI conferences provide significant value for startups and smaller organizations, often with dedicated programs and pricing to facilitate participation. Many conferences including AI4, SuperAI, and World Summit AI feature startup showcases where early-stage companies present to investors and potential partners. These create concentrated access to venture capital firms and corporate development teams. Startup-focused passes at conferences like HumanX ($950 versus $2,650 for standard access) and discounts at other events reduce cost barriers. Conferences enable startups to learn from organizations further along deployment paths without paying consulting fees, identify potential customers and partners, recruit technical talent from attendees, and establish visibility in the AI community. The networking value particularly benefits startups lacking established relationships. A startup founder can meet more potential investors, customers, and partners in three days at a conference than in months of individual meetings. Many conference attendees specifically seek emerging vendors offering innovative solutions, creating receptive audience for startup pitches.

**What’s the difference between NeurIPS, ICML, and ICLR?**

NeurIPS, ICML, and ICLR represent the three premier machine learning research conferences, all featuring rigorous peer review and high-impact research. They differ in focus areas, community culture, and submission processes. NeurIPS (Neural Information Processing Systems) is the largest, regularly drawing 12,000-15,000 attendees, with broad scope covering neural networks, machine learning theory, optimization, reinforcement learning, and applications. It features extensive workshop program and industry presence. ICML (International Conference on Machine Learning) focuses specifically on machine learning methods including supervised learning, unsupervised learning, reinforcement learning, and learning theory. It maintains strong connection to statistical machine learning foundations. ICLR (International Conference on Learning Representations) emphasizes representation learning—the techniques underlying deep learning, including neural architectures, optimization methods, and theoretical understanding. ICLR pioneered OpenReview for transparent peer review where papers, reviews, and author responses are publicly accessible. All three conferences publish accepted papers, run parallel tracks, and include poster sessions. Researchers typically target conferences based on paper topic fit, timing, and submission deadlines. Publishing at any of these three conferences signals high-quality research.

**How far in advance should I register for AI conferences?**

Registration timing significantly impacts cost and availability. Most conferences offer substantial early bird discounts 3-6 months before the event. AI4’s early bird pricing starts at $1,395 versus higher standard rates. Academic conferences typically provide 20-30 percent discounts for early registration. Registration should occur as soon as dates work with your schedule and you’ve secured organizational approval if needed. Popular specialized summits with capped attendance can sell out weeks or months early. Gartner’s summits, HumanX, and similar executive-focused events often sell out given limited capacity and high demand. Monitoring registration status and acting promptly prevents missing desired conferences. Academic conferences rarely sell out but significantly discount early registration. For example, ICLR typically opens registration 6+ months before the conference with early rates available for several months. Hotel room blocks negotiated by conference organizers also fill early, forcing later registrants to book more expensive options or stay farther from venues. Planning conference attendance 6-12 months ahead enables capturing discounts and ensuring availability.

**What value do AI conference expo halls and vendor areas provide?**

Conference expo halls provide concentrated access to AI vendors, tools, and solutions that would require months of individual vendor calls to evaluate otherwise. Vendors staff booths with sales engineers and product specialists who can answer detailed technical questions, demonstrate products with real data, and explain pricing and implementation timelines. This enables evaluating competing solutions efficiently. NVIDIA GTC’s expo features 400+ companies across AI infrastructure, development tools, and applications. Databricks Summit’s expo emphasizes tools complementing the data platform including integration, visualization, and industry-specific applications. The AI Summit London’s expo showcases 100+ vendors with live demos. Beyond vendor evaluation, expos provide exposure to emerging companies and technologies you might not encounter through normal channels. Many attendees discover solutions they weren’t actively seeking but that address problems they face. Vendor booths often distribute technical resources, offer conference-specific pricing, and can facilitate trials or POCs. Some conferences provide expo-only passes at reduced cost for those primarily interested in solution exploration rather than educational sessions.

**How do AI conferences address ethical and responsible AI topics?**

Ethical AI, safety, fairness, and responsible development receive increasing conference attention as AI systems impact consequential decisions. Most major conferences now include dedicated ethics tracks, workshops, or sessions addressing these topics. AAAI features workshops on AI safety and beneficial AI. ICLR and NeurIPS include workshops examining fairness, accountability, and transparency. Enterprise conferences address practical governance implementation—how to review AI systems for bias before deployment, establish human oversight, document decisions for regulatory compliance, and measure fairness across demographic groups. Sessions examine case studies of AI systems that produced biased outcomes, technical methods for detecting and mitigating bias, and governance frameworks including ethics review boards and model cards documenting system characteristics. The EU AI Act’s implementation drives significant conference discussion about regulatory compliance, required documentation, testing standards for high-risk systems, and penalties for non-compliance. Dedicated conferences like AIES (AI, Ethics, and Society) focus entirely on these issues, featuring interdisciplinary research spanning computer science, law, philosophy, and social sciences.

**What is the best way to follow up after AI conferences?**

Effective post-conference follow-up converts conference attendance from one-time event to sustained professional value. Within 2-3 days after returning, while details remain fresh, organize your notes into coherent summary of key learnings. Many organizations require formal trip reports for conference attendance; even without that requirement, documenting insights creates lasting reference material. Reach out to contacts you met with brief personalized messages referencing specific conversation points. “I enjoyed discussing RAG implementation challenges in healthcare at AI4. As mentioned, I’m forwarding our case study on handling PHI in vector databases” provides context and continues conversation. Connect with contacts on professional networks like LinkedIn, often including brief note about where you met. Schedule internal presentations to share conference learnings with colleagues, both fulfilling organizational expectations and solidifying your understanding through teaching. Download and organize conference materials including slides, papers, and recordings for future reference. Many conferences provide presentations weeks after events. Identify 2-3 specific techniques, tools, or approaches learned at the conference to implement in your work, converting passive learning into active capability development. Join conference-related communities or mailing lists to maintain connection with community between annual events.

**Are there regional differences in how AI conferences approach topics?**

Regional conferences reflect local priorities, regulatory environments, and business contexts. European conferences including The AI Summit London and World Summit AI Amsterdam dedicate significant attention to EU AI Act compliance, GDPR data protection, and ethical AI frameworks given Europe’s stronger regulatory approach. Sessions examine specific compliance requirements, documentation standards, and risk management. Asian conferences like SuperAI in Singapore and ICML in Seoul address considerations including supporting multiple languages with specialized NLP models, data residency requirements that affect cloud deployment architecture, and business models adapted to regional markets. Middle Eastern conferences examine AI’s role in economic diversification efforts, smart city development, and industry specific to Gulf regions. North American conferences tend to emphasize business ROI, startup ecosystem dynamics, and venture capital perspectives reflecting Silicon Valley influence. Latin American conferences including ICLR 2026 in Rio de Janeiro highlight regional AI research capacity and applications addressing local challenges. These regional differences make attending conferences in different geographies valuable for understanding global AI landscape beyond dominant Silicon Valley perspective.

**What emerging AI topics should I watch for at 2026 conferences?**

Several emerging topics will feature prominently at 2026 conferences based on research trends and industry developments. Agentic AI systems that can plan workflows, use tools, and accomplish complex tasks autonomously represent evolution beyond current language models. Conferences will examine architectures enabling agency, tool use patterns, multi-agent coordination, and evaluation frameworks. Reasoning AI that can perform multi-step logical inference, evaluate approaches, and adapt based on intermediate results extends capabilities beyond pattern matching. Chain-of-thought prompting and related techniques will see extensive research presentation. Multimodal foundation models processing text, images, audio, and video create new application possibilities from video understanding to cross-modal generation. Efficient AI addressing sustainability concerns through model compression, architecture improvements, and training optimization responds to growing attention on AI’s environmental impact. AI safety and alignment techniques ensuring systems behave according to human values become increasingly important as capabilities grow. Edge AI enabling on-device intelligence without cloud connectivity has implications for autonomous vehicles, robotics, and privacy-sensitive applications. Small language models achieving strong performance with dramatically fewer parameters than frontier models enable broader deployment and reduced costs.

Moving forward, the integration of AI across industries accelerates, with organizations measuring actual business value beyond initial excitement. These conferences offer venues where measurement frameworks emerge, implementation approaches mature, and the technology’s next chapter takes shape through thousands of practitioners learning, sharing, and connecting. Whether your focus lies in research frontiers, technical implementation, business deployment, or societal implications, the 2026 conference circuit provides opportunities to engage with the AI community at a pivotal moment in the technology’s evolution.

Whether you're a startup founder, C-suite executive, or developer, attending the right conference this year can provide actionable insights, networking opportunities, and exposure to cutting-edge AI applications. Here's a quick rundown of the **top 5 AI conferences in Europe for 2026**:

These events are your gateway to connecting with global leaders, exploring the latest AI trends, and staying ahead in the rapidly evolving AI landscape.

## 1. [RAISE Summit](https://www.raisesummit.com/) (Paris, France)

![RAISE Summit](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/66f11b59c8366f8c2c215596/699d6fa422202692be48bb00_b9809a81140ec8366f15221ded661aab.jpeg)

### Dates and Location

The RAISE Summit is set to take place on **8–9 July 2026** at the [Carrousel du Louvre](https://www.viparis.com/en/our-venues/les-salles-du-carrousel-en), located at 99 Rue de Rivoli, 75001 Paris, France [[1]](https://www.raisesummit.com). Its proximity to the iconic [Louvre Museum](https://www.louvre.fr/en) adds a touch of prestige to the event. Beyond the main summit, attendees can enjoy a Side Events Week, transforming Paris into a hub for AI enthusiasts with exclusive dinners, workshops, and networking parties. The event promises a dynamic blend of professional growth and cultural immersion.

The summit offers several pricing tiers to cater to different needs:

This framework ensures a balance between cutting-edge research and practical applications. A new highlight this year is the **CxO Summit**, an exclusive program for Fortune 1000 executives. This invitation-only event focuses on benchmarking AI strategies through tailored sessions and private networking.

"The CxO Summit exists so companies don't just talk about AI, they leave RAISE with real partnerships, pilots, and signed deals" – Hadrien de Cournon, co-founder [[3]](https://www.newswire.com/news/raise-summit-the-worlds-leading-ai-event-returns-to-paris-july-8-9-22678160)

### Networking Opportunities

Networking is a cornerstone of the RAISE Summit. With over 9,000 attendees expected, the event combines scale with a focus on meaningful connections. An AI-powered matching platform facilitates one-on-one meetings, while Side Events Week extends networking opportunities beyond the main venue.

## 2. World Summit AI (Amsterdam, Netherlands)

### Dates and Location

Mark your calendars for 7–8 October 2026, when the World Summit AI will take place at the [TAETS Art and Event Park](https://www.taets.com/en) in Zaandam, just outside Amsterdam. As the centerpiece of World AI Week - which features over 100 side events - this summit turns Amsterdam into a buzzing epicenter for AI professionals. It’s a prime opportunity to dive into the latest advancements and connect with leading minds in the field.

### Key Focus Areas

The 2026 theme, *"Guardians of Tomorrow: Shaping the New AI Paradigm,"* delves into how human intention influences AI's journey from analytical tools to systems capable of reasoning. The summit is divided into several focused tracks:

For those looking to dig deeper, interactive workshops provide hands-on technical sessions to complement the main tracks.

### Networking Opportunities

The summit aligns perfectly with Europe’s push for AI innovation, drawing an impressive mix of executives, entrepreneurs, investors, academics, and policymakers [[6]](https://www.iamsterdam.com/en/business/calendar/events/all/world-summit-ai-2026). The multi-track format encourages collaboration across disciplines, while World AI Week’s informal events create ideal spaces for building meaningful professional connections. Whether you're a technical expert or a business leader, this is the place to meet the people shaping the future of AI.

## 3. [GITEX AI EUROPE](https://www.gitexeurope.com/) (Berlin, Germany)

![GITEX AI EUROPE](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/66f11b59c8366f8c2c215596/699d7034241f8b60ab5c5583_1a1ecfc206f1bcf259e590beb8bd24f8.jpeg)

### Dates and Location

GITEX AI EUROPE 2026 is scheduled for **30 June to 1 July 2026** at the [Messe Berlin](https://www.messe-berlin.de/en) Exhibition Centre (South Entrance) in Berlin, Germany [[7]](https://www.gitexeurope.com). This two-day event brings together Europe's top AI initiatives in one dynamic venue. Berlin, a city celebrated for its forward-thinking culture, provides the perfect backdrop for meaningful discussions and advancements in AI.

### Key Focus Areas

The event dives into practical AI applications, industrial advancements, and the concept of digital sovereignty within Europe. It’s divided into four key zones:

These zones explore a range of industries and technologies, including AI & GenAI, Quantum Computing, Cloud & Data Centres, and Big Data. Additionally, sectors like Industry 4.0, HealthTech, FinTech, GreenTech, and Smart Cities take center stage. Emphasis is placed on fostering public-private partnerships and cross-border collaborations, aligning with Europe’s push for AI solutions that are both impactful and compliant with regulations.

### Networking Opportunities

The event is a hotspot for global connections, attracting over **30,000 tech and business leaders** from more than 100 countries, alongside **2,000 enterprises and startups** and **600 investors** [[8]](https://events.zexprwire.com/event/gitex-ai-europe-2026). With over **500 speakers from around the world**, the conference also features specialised sessions that enable meaningful exchanges across diverse AI fields. Whether you're looking to connect with industry giants or emerging innovators, the networking potential here is unmatched.

## 4. [Big Data Conference Europe](https://bigdataconference.eu/) (Vilnius, Lithuania)

![Big Data Conference Europe](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/66f11b59c8366f8c2c215596/699d7035241f8b60ab5c558c_38c1e09f2f83ec86291ef2791501f43e.jpeg)

![Big Data Conference Europe](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/66f11b59c8366f8c2c215596/699d7035241f8b60ab5c558c_38c1e09f2f83ec86291ef2791501f43e.jpeg)

### Dates and Location

The 10th edition of the Big Data Conference Europe is set to take place from **24 to 27 November 2026** in Vilnius, Lithuania. Workshops will kick off the event on 24 November at **V. Gerulaičio g. 10**, while the main conference sessions will run from 25 to 27 November at **Multikino, Ozo st. 18**. Whether you prefer to attend in person or online, the hybrid format ensures flexibility for all participants [[9]](https://bigdataconference.eu).

### Cost Range

Ticket prices depend on the level of participation. A **4-Day Full Ticket** is priced at **€1,730**, but if you act early, you can grab it for **€900 (excl. VAT)** with the Blind Bird discount. A **3-Day Onsite and Online Ticket** costs **€1,350**, or **€700 (excl. VAT)** with the same discount. To take advantage of the **50% Blind Bird discount**, make sure to register by **5 March 2026**. Additionally, teams of **10 or more** can enjoy an **extra 15% discount** by contacting the organisers directly [[9]](https://bigdataconference.eu). These pricing options make it accessible for both individuals and teams to join this content-packed event.

### Key Focus Areas

The conference brings together top minds to discuss **Big Data**, **High Load**, **Data Science**, **Machine Learning**, and **AI**. The 2026 programme will dive into cutting-edge topics like **Generative AI**, **Quantum Computing**, **Ethical AI**, **Multimodal AI**, and **DataOps**, organised into four distinct tracks. Across four days, attendees can look forward to **over 60 talks** and **five hands-on workshops**, all designed to provide **deep technical knowledge**.

"Big Data Conference Europe was a genuinely refreshing experience, prioritising deep technical insights. The organisers struck a perfect balance of professional efficiency and humble hospitality." – *Manish Kumar, Platform Engineer at Mercari* [[9]](https://bigdataconference.eu)

This carefully curated agenda ensures professionals leave with actionable insights to navigate the rapidly evolving landscape of AI and data technologies.

### Networking Opportunities

Expect a vibrant mix of over **700 attendees** from **35+ countries**, including **50% senior-level professionals** and **5% executives**. The organisers have gone the extra mile to foster connections through interactive games, quizzes, and evening activities.

"The sessions were diverse and well-structured, making it easy to stay engaged throughout the day. Even more impactful were the conversations with industry peers – full of sharp insights and practical advice." – *Radoslaw Zynda, IT Analyst at DNV* [[9]](https://bigdataconference.eu)

The attendee profile is equally diverse, with **Data Analysts & Scientists (28%)**, **Data Engineers (27%)**, and **Software Developers (13%)** forming the majority. This mix creates an ideal environment for cross-functional collaboration and knowledge-sharing [[9]](https://bigdataconference.eu).

## 5. [AI Summit London](https://london.theaisummit.com/) (London, United Kingdom)

![AI Summit London](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/66f11b59c8366f8c2c215596/699d7035241f8b60ab5c5588_93ea6b11a17e25155d8183633e22b81d.jpeg)

### Dates and Location

The 10th annual AI Summit London is set for **10–11 June 2026** at [Tobacco Dock](https://www.tobaccodocklondon.com/), London (E1W 2SF). As the flagship event of [London Tech Week](https://londontechweek.com/), it offers a central and accessible location for international attendees [[10]](https://london.theaisummit.com)[[12]](https://london.theaisummit.com/ai-summit-london-faqs).

### Cost Range

Tickets range from **£25 to £1,399**, depending on the type of pass selected. Options include Delegate Passes, VIP All Access Passes, and Tobacco Dock Campus Passes. Discounts are available for groups of three or more delegates from the same organization. Additionally, start-ups, investors, academics, and government representatives can take advantage of special rates. A limited number of free passes are also available for registered charity workers [[12]](https://london.theaisummit.com/ai-summit-london-faqs)[[13]](https://londontechweek.com/ai-summit).

### Key Focus Areas

This year’s summit emphasizes the **practical application of AI** with a focus on "Bottom-Line Impact." It introduces four new stages:

With **5,000+ attendees**, **300+ speakers**, and **100+ sponsors and exhibitors**, the event provides actionable strategies to enhance operations across industries [[10]](https://london.theaisummit.com).

## FAQs

### Which 2026 AI conference best fits my role (developer, founder, C‑suite)?

Choosing the best AI conference in 2026 really depends on what you're looking for and your specific role in the field. One standout option is the **RAISE Summit in Paris**, which caters to a variety of attendees with its tailored tracks:

This broad approach makes the RAISE Summit a compelling choice for professionals at all levels.

That said, other events might align better with niche interests. For example, **[AAAI](https://aaai.org/) 2026** focuses heavily on research, making it ideal for academics or those deeply involved in AI studies. Alternatively, the **AI Summit in Paris**, taking place in July 2026, is another excellent option for exploring the latest developments and trends in the industry.

### How can I plan for EU AI Act and compliance sessions at these events?

To make the most of AI conferences in Europe and prepare for sessions on the EU AI Act and compliance, start by carefully reviewing the event agenda. Look for sessions that focus on AI governance, regulation, and compliance topics. **Register early** to ensure you get access to these tracks, as they may fill up quickly.

Take the initiative to connect with organisers or speakers ahead of time. They can provide insights into workshops or panels that align with your interests. Networking events are another key opportunity - especially those featuring policymakers or industry leaders involved in shaping AI regulations.

Before attending, spend time reviewing the EU AI Act itself. Familiarising yourself with its key provisions will help you engage more effectively during sessions. Bring along specific questions or challenges you’re facing to guide your learning and refine your compliance strategies.

### What’s the smartest way to maximise networking and meetings onsite?

To get the most out of networking at AI conferences like **RAISE Summit 2026 in Paris**, take advantage of the event's networking tools. Many conferences provide **specialised apps or platforms** that let you connect with other attendees even before the event begins. This is a great way to identify potential collaborators or key industry figures you'd like to meet.

Consider joining **pre-event activities**, such as dinners, workshops, or informal gatherings. These smaller settings often make it easier to strike up meaningful conversations. Another smart move is to schedule meetings ahead of time using the official event platforms. This ensures you make the most of your time and don’t miss out on key connections.

During the conference, look out for **themed sessions or networking lounges** tailored to specific topics or industries. These spaces are designed to bring like-minded professionals together, making it easier to meet people with shared interests.

Ultimately, **proactive planning** and seizing opportunities to participate in exclusive activities will help you build strong professional relationships at events like these.

## Training more people?

Get your team access to the full DataCamp for business platform.[For Business](https://www.datacamp.com/business)For a bespoke solution [book a demo](https://www.datacamp.com/business/demo-2).

As AI continues to grow and become more ubiquitous in all industries, it’s important to stay current with the latest trends and technologies. Generative AI, for example, was a relatively small field just last year. It is now projected to become a [$1.3 trillion industry](https://www.bloomberg.com/company/press/generative-ai-to-become-a-1-3-trillion-market-by-2032-research-finds/) in the next ten years. While [articles on how to learn AI](https://www.forbes.com/sites/forbestechcouncil/2024/01/31/artificial-intelligence-in-2024-what-it-has-in-store-for-us/?sh=7f6b68c45cb3), [blogs on machine learning](https://www.datacamp.com/blog/how-to-learn-machine-learning), and courses such as [AI Fundamentals](https://www.datacamp.com/tracks/ai-fundamentals) are great starting points for learning, AI conferences provide a unique opportunity to hear from industry leaders and stay updated with the latest trends.

AI conferences offer many benefits to data practitioners and business leaders. They provide opportunities for learning with hands-on workshops, tutorials, and technical sessions covering the latest advancements in machine learning, deep learning, and other AI subfields. Attendees learn about the newest tools and best practices directly from experts who have used and developed them. Outside of the technical benefits, they also provide great opportunities to develop your career through scheduled networking opportunities.

This article will explore ten of the best AI conferences you can attend in 2026. We’ll discuss when and where they will be hosted and some of the key factors that could make the conference an ideal fit for you.

## 1. Radar: Hybrid Human-AI Teams

*   **Date**: April 1, 2026
*   **Location**: Online
*   Cost: Free of charge

RADAR returns in 2026 with a focus on the evolving relationship between human expertise and artificial intelligence. As organizations continue to navigate the integration of AI into their workflows, this coming event will explore how successful teams are building collaborative frameworks where human insight and AI capabilities work in tandem.

The conference will bring together industry leaders and data professionals of all stripes to discuss real strategies. Attendees can expect insights into how forward-thinking organizations are approaching AI adoption, developing the skills needed to work alongside AI systems, and building cultures that embrace this technological shift.

_Full agenda and speaker details will be announced soon._

The recordings from previous years are available in case you missed them:[RADAR: AI Edition (2025)](https://www.datacamp.com/radar/ai-2025), [Radar Skills Edition](https://www.datacamp.com/radar-skills-edition-2025-recordings), [Radar Forward Edition](https://www.datacamp.com/radar-forward-edition-2024), [Radar: The Analytics Edition](https://www.datacamp.com/radar-analytics-edition), and [Radar: The AI Edition](https://www.datacamp.com/radar-ai-2024)[(2024)](https://www.datacamp.com/radar/ai-2025).

## Sign Up to Radar: The AI Edition

Join us to explore how top organizations are turning AI into a competitive advantage.

[Watch the Recordings](https://www.datacamp.com/radar/ai-2025)

![Image 1](https://media.datacamp.com/legacy/v1712909491/Radar_AI_484c2efd18.png?w=3840)

## 2. The AI Conference San Francisco

*   **Date**: Sep 30-Oct 1, 2026.
*   **Location**: San Francisco, California, US.
*   **Cost**: From $199.

[The AI Conference](https://aiconference.com/) in San Francisco will be a two-day in-person conference discussing the latest in AI. Topics include what's working in the best applied-AI startups, and technical lessons on the nuances of neural architectures, foundational models, alignment, and more. This large event will provide many opportunities for learning, networking, and listening to interesting keynote discussions.

## 3. SuperAI

*   **Date**: June 10-11, 2026.
*   **Location**: Marina Bay Sands, Singapore.
*   **Cost**: $299-$999.

If you are interested in attending an AI conference in Asia, the [SuperAI conference](https://www.superai.com/) in June is a great option. This event, which has about 5,000 attendees, many of whom are C-level executives, represents a great networking opportunity. The event also has partners from the largest AI companies in the world, like Microsoft, Google, AWS, OpenAI, and Salesforce. Save money with the Early Bird discount.

## 4. Data + AI Summit

*   **Date**: June 15-18, 2026.
*   **Location**: Hybrid - San Francisco, California, US, and available online.
*   **Cost**: $1,395-$1,895 in-person. Free for virtual admission.

The [Data + AI Summit](https://www.databricks.com/dataaisummit/) is a four-day event hosted by Databricks. It includes panel discussions, networking opportunities, and training workshops on topics such as data engineering, data governance, and machine learning. Past conference panels and training sessions are available on [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLTPXxbhUt-YVWi_cf2UUDc9VZFLoRgu0l). Discounted tickets are available with group-rate pricing.

## 5. The AI Summit London

*   **Date**: June 10-11, 2026.
*   **Location**: London, UK.
*   **Cost**: £125-£2,499.

Over two days, [AI Summit London](https://london.theaisummit.com/) will cover a wide range of topics, including how to use AI responsibly and its application in different industries. The summit has a long list of sponsors and thousands of guests, making it a great venue for networking with many passionate AI practitioners. Costs vary depending on access level.

## 6. CDAO Government

*   **Date**: September 23-24, 2026.
*   **Location**: Washington, D.C., US.
*   **Cost**: $499 in-person. Free for VP and C-level government executives.

The [CDAO Government conference](https://cdao-gov.coriniumintelligence.com/) in D.C. is unique in that it focuses on applications of AI and data specifically for government organizations. This will be the 14th year of this event, and it is perfect for those who want to understand how to apply AI’s strength to government data problems safely.

## 7. Ai4 2026

*   **Date**: August 4-6, 2026.
*   **Location**: The Venetian, Las Vegas, Nevada, US.
*   **Cost**: $1,395-$3,195.

The [Ai4 2026 conference](https://ai4.io/vegas/) in Las Vegas will feature over 1,000 speakers and 12,000 attendees, with representation from more than 90 countries. This three-day event provides plenty of opportunities to network, learn about AI, and enjoy the unique city of Las Vegas. This is a diverse conference with attendees from many industries, job titles, and specialties.

## 8. Coalesce

*   **Date**: September 15-18, 2026.
*   **Location**: Hybrid - Las Vegas, Nevada, US, and available online.
*   **Cost**: $850 in-person. Free for virtual admission.

[Coalesce](https://coalesce.getdbt.com/) is a four-day conference that can be attended in person in Las Vegas or online. Last year’s sessions are available on [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL0QYlrC86xQnT3HLh-XgvoTf9F3lbsADf). This event covers various topics, including machine learning and AI, testing, data anomaly detection, data engineering, and more. It also provides great networking opportunities for those who attend in person.

## 9. World Summit AI

*   **Date**: October 7-8, 2026.
*   **Location**: Amsterdam, Netherlands.
*   **Cost**: From €349.

The [World Summit AI conference](https://worldsummit.ai/) is a large event hosted in Amsterdam. It will include keynote speakers and an expo. The conference will offer tracks on relevant topics like Generative AI, scaling startups with AI, and AI in finance and other industries. Save money with the Early Bird discount.

## 10. Big Data Conference Europe

*   **Date**: November 25-27, 2026.
*   **Location**: Hybrid - Vilnius, Lithuania, and available online.
*   **Cost**: €700-€1,350.

The [Big Data Europe conference](https://bigdataconference.eu/) includes in-person workshops on the first day and a hybrid-style conference over the next three days. This is the 10th time this conference has run, and the agenda includes important topics in AI like ChatGPT, deep learning, and predictive analytics. This conference hosts attendees from all backgrounds, both inside and outside Europe.

## 11. NVIDIA GTC

*   **Date**: March 16-19, 2026.
*   **Location**: San Jose, California, US.
*   **Cost**: $2,172-$2,525.

Often called the "Woodstock of AI," NVIDIA GTC is the premier event for developers and researchers building the infrastructure of the future. The conference features hundreds of sessions on generative AI, robotics, and industrial digitalization. The highlight is always the keynote by CEO Jensen Huang, which sets the technical direction for the entire industry for the coming year.

## 12. India AI Impact Summit

*   **Date**: February 15-20, 2026.
*   **Location**: New Delhi, India.
*   **Cost**: Free / Invite Only.

As a major new player on the global circuit, this government-led summit focuses on the role of the "Global South" in the AI revolution. It brings together heads of state, tech leaders, and researchers to discuss inclusive AI development and geopolitical strategy. This is a massive-scale event perfect for those interested in international policy and the expanding global footprint of artificial intelligence.

## 13. HumanX

*   **Date**: April 6-9, 2026.
*   **Location**: San Francisco, California, US.
*   **Cost**: $2,650-$3,995.

HumanX is rapidly becoming the "Davos of AI," focusing less on code and more on high-level strategy, safety, and regulation. This event gathers CEOs, policymakers, and industry titans to discuss how AI will reshape the global economy. It is an ideal venue for executives looking to understand the macro-trends driving the industry and to network with the people making the decisions.

### The Best AI Events in Europe (2026 Guide)

Looking to connect with AI leaders or explore the latest trends? Europe’s AI scene in 2026 offers a packed calendar of conferences tailored to different goals - from networking with C-level executives to diving into technical workshops. Here’s what you need to know:

**Quick Tip**: Choose events based on your goals - whether it’s forming partnerships, understanding compliance, or exploring technical advancements. Plan ahead to maximize networking opportunities and ROI.

## New Frontier AI - Bringing Europe back to the AI Development Table | DLD26

###### sbb-itb-e314c3b

## [RAISE Summit](https://raisesummit.com/): Paris AI Conference at Carrousel du Louvre

![RAISE Summit](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/66f11b59c8366f8c2c215596/699d123dc4ece51252ea966c_7638e9d2fe2c7d3b341e2eecacceaf63.jpeg)

![RAISE Summit](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/66f11b59c8366f8c2c215596/699d123dc4ece51252ea966c_7638e9d2fe2c7d3b341e2eecacceaf63.jpeg)

Scheduled for 8–9 July 2026 at [Le Carrousel du Louvre](https://www.westfield.com/fr/france/carrouseldulouvre), the RAISE Summit brings together industry leaders to tackle the challenges of AI adoption and infrastructure.

### Event Features and Scale

This summit is set to attract over 9,000 attendees, feature 350+ speakers, and host representatives from more than 2,000 companies worldwide. Impressively, over 80% of participants are C-level executives or founders. Notable speakers for 2026 include **Mark Cuban** (Entrepreneur), **Vlad Tenev** (CEO, [Robinhood](https://robinhood.com/)), **Charlie Kawwas** (President, [Broadcom](https://www.broadcom.com/home)), and **Pat Gelsinger** (GP, PlayGround Global & former Intel CEO).

The agenda revolves around **"The 4Fs"**:

In addition to the main sessions, the **Machina Summit** on 7 July 2026 will spotlight Physical AI innovations, while the **CxO Summit** provides a private setting for executive strategy discussions. A startup competition featuring over 1,000 startups will compete for a €5 million prize pool, and the Global AI Hackathon will engage over 8,000 participants with a €200,000 prize at stake.

A week of "Side Events" across Paris extends networking opportunities, featuring exclusive dinners, workshops, and meetups hosted by global innovators. PRO and VIP ticket holders also gain access to a networking app designed to help attendees schedule meetings and foster business connections before the event.

These features are complemented by a variety of ticket options tailored to meet diverse attendee objectives.

### Ticket Prices and Plans

| Ticket Type | Early-Bird Price | Regular Price | Key Features |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **PRO** | €999 + VAT | €1,799 + VAT | Access to exhibition/stages, networking app, Ecosystem Party, food & drinks |
| **VIP** | €1,899 + VAT | €2,999 + VAT | All PRO perks, VIP lounge, Machina Summit access, startup pitch decks, industrial reports |
| **VIP MAX** | €3,499 + VAT | €4,599 + VAT | All VIP perks, plus the "Royal Dinner" executive evening event |
| **DEVELOPER** | €599 + VAT | N/A | Includes PRO features; eligibility required |
| **STARTUP** | €599 + VAT | N/A | Includes PRO features; designed for early-stage companies |
| **MACHINA** | €699 + VAT | N/A | Full-day Machina Summit access (talks, demos, robot showcases) |
| **VOLUNTEER** | FREE | N/A | Event access for selected applicants |

Early-Bird pricing offers a significant discount, with PRO tickets saving 44% compared to regular prices. VIP and VIP MAX tickets include Machina Summit access (normally €699) and exclusive benefits like industrial reports and startup pitch decks, which are especially valuable for investors and corporate leaders.

These ticket plans are thoughtfully designed to match the summit's diverse offerings.

### Benefits of Attending RAISE Summit

The summit is crafted to deliver actionable outcomes. A dedicated networking app allows attendees to arrange meetings in advance, while the VIP lounge provides a quieter space for in-depth discussions. For VIP MAX ticket holders, the **Royal Dinner** offers an intimate setting for executive-level connections.

"The serendipity that can happen when you're together in a physical space is life-changing."

For companies exploring AI vendors or scouting technical talent, the startup competition and hackathon offer direct access to innovative solutions and skilled developers. Meanwhile, industrial reports and startup pitch decks provide valuable insights into emerging trends and technology strategies. The 4Fs framework ensures the agenda covers everything from infrastructure and application development to risk management and long-term planning - helping attendees tackle multiple priorities efficiently.

This focused approach ensures the summit delivers measurable value for all participants.

## Other AI Events in Europe (2026)

In addition to the RAISE Summit, Europe is home to several notable AI events in 2026. These events cater to a wide range of interests, from policy-making and business applications to academic advancements.

### [Rise of AI Conference](https://riseof.ai/conference-2026/) (Berlin)

![Rise of AI Conference](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/66f11b59c8366f8c2c215596/699d71406dbf58d3f32661d1_e9fc35b252d9fe934e23ae0e97f5d56c.jpeg)

![Rise of AI Conference](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/66f11b59c8366f8c2c215596/699d71406dbf58d3f32661d1_e9fc35b252d9fe934e23ae0e97f5d56c.jpeg)

On 5–6 May 2026, Berlin will host the 10th edition of the Rise of AI Conference. This event brings together political leaders, researchers, and top executives. It features two main tracks: the **Meta Stage**, focusing on policy discussions and ecosystem growth, and the **Applied AI Stage**, which highlights business demos and implementation strategies.

Prominent speakers include Franziska Giffey (Mayor of Berlin), Stefan Mesken (Chief Scientist at [DeepL](https://www.deepl.com/)), and Dr. Ulli Waltinger (Vice President at [Siemens AG](https://www.siemens.com/en-us/)). Attendance is capped at 300 participants, ensuring a close-knit networking environment. Ticket prices start at €999 for the Singularity Pass, with Premium Passes available at €1,299, which include access to an exclusive speaker dinner [[3]](https://riseof.ai/conference-2026).

"When Germany rises, it's also thanks to people like Veronika and Fabian - who thoughtfully bring together the knowledgeable and powerful." - Dr. Ramin Assadollahi, Chairman of the Board & Founder, ExB group [[3]](https://riseof.ai/conference-2026)

From Berlin, we move to London, where another major AI event showcases the technology's transformative business potential.

### [AI Summit London](https://london.theaisummit.com/)

![AI Summit London](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/66f11b59c8366f8c2c215596/699d71406dbf58d3f326619e_99a569dc518f05f41c96429e26b77682.jpeg)

Taking place on 10–11 June 2026 at [Tobacco Dock](https://www.tobaccodocklondon.com/), the AI Summit London is the centrepiece of London Tech Week. With over 5,000 attendees and 300+ speakers across 8 stages, this conference delves into how AI is reshaping industries like finance, healthcare, and retail.

The event is supported by an Ambassador Chapter featuring industry leaders such as Lawrence Ampofo ([Vodafone](https://www.vodafone.com/)), Matthew Candy ([IBM](https://www.ibm.com/)), and Mara Pometti ([Mastercard](https://www.mastercard.com/global/en.html)) [[4]](https://london.theaisummit.com).

Next, for those interested in academic breakthroughs, the IJCAI-ECAI 2026 in Bremen offers an in-depth exploration of cutting-edge research.
