## How to Select the Right AI Event

![Europe AI Events 2026 Comparison: Dates, Prices, and Focus Areas](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/66f11b59c8366f8c2c215596/699d71406dbf58d3f32661a8_699adc11efc60cc2af08fe83-1771757748371.jpg)

Europe AI Events 2026 Comparison: Dates, Prices, and Focus Areas

Choosing the right AI conference in Europe can feel overwhelming with so many options available. Start by clarifying your main objective. For example, if you're exploring automation platforms, trade shows with a strong vendor presence are ideal. On the other hand, if you're focused on understanding EU compliance and the EU AI Act, look for events offering direct engagement with policymakers shaping these regulations [[2]](https://www.data-unplugged.de/en/blog/ai-conferences-europe-2026).

Your professional role also plays a big part. C-level executives aiming for high-level deal-making should prioritise events where decision-makers dominate the attendee list. Meanwhile, developers and data scientists may get more value from conferences that focus on technical case studies rather than sales-oriented sessions.

Budget is another factor to weigh carefully. Align your spending with your goals, and use the table below to get a quick snapshot of some key AI events.

### Event Comparison Table

| Event | Date | Location | Attendees | Price Range | Main Focus |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Rise of AI** | 5–6 May | Berlin, Germany | Hybrid/Limited | Free online streaming | Policy & Applied AI |
| **AI Summit London** | 10–11 June | London, UK | 5,000+ | £2,399+ | Enterprise Implementation |
| **IJCAI-ECAI 2026** | 15–21 August | Bremen, Germany | N/A | N/A | Academic Research |

### Matching Events to Your Goals

It's not realistic - or necessary - to attend every conference. Instead, focus on two or three that align closely with your objectives. For instance, if you're a startup founder looking for investors, seek out events with a strong investor presence. Enterprise leaders aiming to integrate AI across departments should consider attending alongside key team members to strategise effectively.

Don't forget to factor in the full cost of attendance, including travel, accommodation, and the time commitment. Networking opportunities are also worth considering. Smaller, more intimate gatherings often allow for deeper, more meaningful connections compared to massive festivals with over 10,000 attendees. Many events now offer networking apps, enabling you to schedule meetings in advance and make the most of your time [[1]](https://www.raisesummit.com/tickets-2026).

## Conclusion

This guide highlights how Europe's AI events equip professionals with opportunities to spark meaningful innovation and forge strategic partnerships. The events showcased here cater to a wide range of professional interests, from research-focused conferences to business-oriented summits, ensuring there's something for everyone.

One standout is the **RAISE Summit**, a prime event for top-tier networking and actionable AI insights. With ticket prices starting at €999 for PRO access and going up to €3,499 for the VIP MAX experience, it offers flexible options tailored to diverse networking ambitions and budgets.

Attending in person provides unmatched opportunities for connection. Whether you're tackling intricate regulatory issues, diving into cutting-edge automation tools, or scouting for investment prospects, selecting the right event and fully immersing yourself in its offerings can make a measurable impact on your outcomes.

Plan wisely, prepare thoroughly, and participate actively - these events hold the potential to shape your organization's AI trajectory well beyond 2026. The relationships and knowledge you gain could be pivotal for your long-term strategy.

## FAQs

### Which AI event should I choose for my role?

The best AI event for you depends on what you’re looking to achieve. If your goal is maximising ROI and connecting with top industry leaders, the **RAISE Summit in Paris** on *8–9 July 2026* should be on your radar. For those focused on ethics, regulation, or cutting-edge tech developments, the **World Summit AI in Amsterdam** happening *7–8 October 2026* is a great choice. Meanwhile, if robotics and industrial automation are your priorities, don’t miss **Machina 2026** in Paris on *7 July 2026*.

### How can I maximise networking ROI at RAISE Summit?

To get the most out of your networking efforts at RAISE Summit, dive into the event's structured opportunities. Attend key gatherings like the VIP dinner, Speakers Lounge, and Ecosystem Party to meet peers and industry leaders in a more focused setting. Take full advantage of the event's networking app to connect with others and lock in meetings ahead of time. Also, fine-tune your pitch and conversation points beforehand, especially for pre-event sessions such as Y-Combinator x RAISE and founder nights. These steps will help you make meaningful connections and leave a lasting impression.

### What extra costs should I budget beyond the ticket?

When budgeting for your trip, don't forget to account for expenses beyond the ticket price. These can include **accommodation**, **meals**, **transportation**, and even **optional networking or social events**. Such costs usually aren’t covered in the ticket price and will depend on your personal choices and travel plans.

## International Artificial Intelligence Conferences in Singapore 2026

Immerse yourself in the vibrant sphere of Artificial Intelligence Conference in Singapore 2026, where revolutionary AI breakthroughs are unveiled and explored. These conferences serve as dynamic hubs where experts and enthusiasts from diverse fields convene to exchange insights on the cutting-edge developments in artificial intelligence. Whether your interests lie in machine learning, robotics, natural language processing, or computer vision, these gatherings encompass a broad spectrum of topics pivotal to the AI landscape.

Engage with leading researchers, industry luminaries, and policymakers to remain abreast of the latest trends and advancements shaping the technological landscape. From ethical considerations to real-world applications, Artificial Intelligence Conferences in Singapore 2026 offer invaluable opportunities to deepen your understanding of AI and its societal implications. Join the discourse, expand your professional network, and contribute to shaping the future trajectory of artificial intelligence, both within your Singapore and across the globe.

# Saas Events to Attend in 2026

Published on:

January 5, 2026

![Illustration on four people thinking about the names of saas events](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/66b21755b610026119fc6fbd/6964b8a537a2245faecbe42c_Saas%20Events%20to%20Attend%20in%202026.webp)

### Follow us

### Share

If 2025 was about adapting to change, 2026 is about making deliberate bets—on markets, GTM motion, and where founders spend their limited time and attention. Events still matter, but only when chosen with intent.

This guide builds on our 2025 SaaS events list and updates it for 2026. We’ve retained events that consistently deliver founder value, added ecosystem and fintech conferences that matter for modern SaaS, and excluded noise.

The aim is to help founders and operators decide which events are worth their time.

## 1. SaaSBoomi Annual ’26

**Location**: Chennai, India

**Dates**: March 18–20, 2026

**Who should attend**

SaaSBoomi Annual is best suited for founders and operators who are hands-on with product and technical decisions. If you are building or evolving a SaaS product where AI meaningfully affects architecture, workflows, or team structure, the content will be relevant.

SaaSBoomi Annual ‘26 is a focused, builder-oriented conference for SaaS and AI teams thinking deeply about product, engineering, and systems design. The event is structured to reduce surface-level discussion and concentrate on how software companies are adapting their products and organizations for AI-driven development.

The audience spans early, growth, and scale-stage founders, along with product, engineering, and data leaders who are actively shipping AI into production. Sessions draw heavily from real implementation work, including how teams are re-architecting products, handling data and model complexity, and managing the operational trade-offs that come with AI-native systems.

Content is organized into curated tracks based on company stage and function. This allows conversations to stay specific and peer-relevant, rather than broadly inspirational. A distinguishing feature of the event is its emphasis on candid discussion of failures and constraints, which helps ground sessions in practical decision-making rather than retrospective success narratives.

[Click here to register!](https://saasboomi.org/events/annual-26/)

## 2. Human X 2026

**Location**: San Francisco, USA

**Dates**: April 06–09, 2026

**Who should attend**

AI Business leaders, innovators, and industry professionals from sectors like financial services, healthcare, and technology.

HumanX is the ultimate event for anyone curious about AI and its real-world implications in various industrial sectors.

This event not only focuses on the speakers but also offers hands-on workshops where you can explore AI’s influence in fields like healthcare, finance, and more. You’ll also get the chance to participate in live demos, ask that one question that is crippling in your mind, and connect with other tech enthusiasts.

You may be a professional or just starting out, Human X, 2026 is for everyone. So don’t miss out; this is where the future of AI unpacks itself in front of your eyes.

[Click here to register!](https://www.humanx.co/)

## 3. Chargebee Beelieve

**Locations**: San Francisco, US

**Dates**: April 15–16, 2026

**Who should attend**

Subscription businesses, product managers, finance professionals, and investors.

Chargebee Beelieve promises to be worth the time for professionals in the subscription industry, featuring keynotes from renowned leaders across SaaS, media, and direct-to-consumer sectors. Attendees can expect engaging discussions on evolving revenue strategies and subscriber-retention techniques and learn about fundraising and IPO readiness.

This will also focus on hands-on workshops and networking opportunities, allowing participants to connect with industry experts and share their growth stories.

With an inclusive atmosphere welcoming everyone from startups and established companies, this event is not just about learning; it’s about shaping the future of subscription-managed business.

[Click here to register!](https://www.chargebee.com/events/beelieve)

## 4. SaaStock USA

**Location**: Austin, USA

**Dates**: April 15–16, 2026

**Who should attend**

SaaStock USA is well suited for founders and operators who have clear commercial goals tied to attendance. If you are looking to meet potential investors, partners, customers, or senior peers, and are willing to invest time in pre-event planning - the format can be effective.

SaaStock USA is a mid-sized conference designed around structured networking and deal-oriented conversations for B2B SaaS and AI companies. The event places less emphasis on large keynote programming and more on pre-arranged meetings between founders, operators, and investors.

A defining feature of SaaStock USA is its meeting-first format. Attendees are encouraged to pre-book 1:1 conversations using matchmaking tools, with the goal of creating focused interactions rather than serendipitous networking. As a result, much of the value comes from preparation - selecting the right meetings and being clear about objectives going into the event.

[Click here to register!](https://saastock-usa.com/)

## 5. Stripe Sessions

**Locations**: San Francisco, US

**Dates**: April 29–30, 2026

**Who should attend**

Perfect for developers, businesses, and startup founders interested in the latest trends in payments and the digital economy.

One of Stripe's flagship conferences, this event gathers developers, business leaders, and industry experts to explore the future of payments and the digital economy with respect to the evolution of AI.

You will engage with thought leaders through keynote speeches and interactive workshops that cover the latest trends in payment solutions.

What makes Stripe Sessions unique is its hands-on approach, featuring product demos and breakout sessions that provide you with great insights that are tailored to various business needs.

This event welcomes everyone from startups to established businesses and leaders, making it a perfect networking hub for you.

You can expect to walk away with new tools and connections that can help you and your business in an ever-evolving landscape.

[Click here to register!](https://stripesessions.com/)

## 6. SaaStr Annual 2026

**Location**: San Francisco, USA

**Dates**: May 12–14, 2026

**Who should attend**

SaaStr Annual is most useful for founders and leaders already operating a SaaS or AI business and navigating scale. If you are past initial product validation and working through growth, efficiency, or organizational challenges, the content will be directly relevant.

It is a strong fit for B2B SaaS and AI founders post–product-market fit, revenue, product, and engineering leaders scaling teams, operators integrating AI into go-to-market or internal workflows, and investors focused on AI-driven B2B companies.

SaaStr Annual 2026 is a three-day gathering for founders and operators building and scaling B2B SaaS and AI companies. It brings together builders, executives, and investors with a clear emphasis on execution over inspiration.

The 2026 agenda reflects the current reality of the market. AI is no longer a standalone topic; it runs through product, sales, support, and internal operations. Sessions are led by founders and operators who have deployed AI in production—shipping AI features, running AI-assisted go-to-market motions, and redesigning teams around AI-native workflows.

The content is practical and tightly curated. Talks focus on how scaling companies actually change—where AI delivers leverage, where it introduces friction, and how operating models evolve as complexity increases. Beyond sessions, the event is designed for structured interaction, with curated meetings, AMAs, and small-group discussions with peers and investors.

[Click here to register!](https://www.saastrannual.com/)

## 7. NY Tech Week

**Location**: New York, US

**Dates**: June 01–07, 2026

**Who should attend**

Startup founders, tech enthusiasts, and investors looking to discover new business opportunities.

NY Tech Week 2026 is a dynamic showcase of the latest innovations in technology. This event features product-focused sessions organized by startups and industry leaders throughout New York City.

You can expect hands-on demonstrations, product launches, and knowledgeable talks from influential figures. The key highlights of the events include interactive workshops on emerging technologies such as AI, blockchain, and fintech, providing practical approaches to work and renewing your business strategy.

You will also have the chance to explore solutions from top startups and established companies, making it an invaluable opportunity for product managers, developers, and entrepreneurs.

[Click here to register!](https://www.tech-week.com/)

## 8. HubSpot INBOUND 2026

**Location**: Boston, USA

**Dates**: September 16–18, 2026

**Who should attend**

INBOUND is best suited for founders and leaders responsible for go-to-market and revenue systems. If your company is investing heavily in marketing, sales, customer success, or RevOps, and especially if HubSpot is a core part of your stack - the event can be useful.

INBOUND 2026 is HubSpot’s annual conference focused on go-to-market execution, customer acquisition, and revenue operations. While closely tied to the HubSpot ecosystem, the event has broader relevance for SaaS teams thinking about how marketing, sales, and customer experience work together at scale.

The agenda spans marketing strategy, sales execution, RevOps alignment, and customer engagement, with increasing emphasis on automation and AI-driven workflows. Sessions tend to blend strategic framing with practical examples, often drawn from companies actively operating inside HubSpot or comparable GTM stacks.

[Click here to register!](https://www.inbound.com/)

## 9. Money20/20

**Locations**: Las Vegas, USA

**Dates**: October 18–21, 2026

**Who should attend**

Fantastic opportunity for fintech leaders, payment mediators, and startup founders eager to explore the latest trends and technologies in the financial ecosystem.

Money20/20 USA 2026 will take place at The Venetian, Las Vegas. This premier fintech event is set to attract over 11,000 attendees from various sectors, including banking, payments, technology, retail, and policy.

The event will focus on more than 500 sessions across multiple stages, focusing on critical topics like AI in finance, instant payments, and regulatory changes in the fintech sector.

Attendees will have ample networking opportunities to connect with industry leaders, innovators, and potential partners, making Money20/20 USA 2026 a crucial gathering for anyone involved in the global money ecosystem and interested in knowing more about fintech and AI.

[Click here to register!](https://www.money2020.com/)

## 10. TechCrunch Disrupt 2026

**Location**: San Francisco, USA

**Dates**: October 13–15, 2026

**Who should attend**

Perfect for startup founders to pitch their ideas and investors looking for the next big opportunity.

TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, taking place in San Francisco, is a premier event for the tech community.

Expect a dynamic schedule with over 300 sessions that highlight the working and functioning of startups through pitch competitions and product demos that will help provide a platform for emerging talent to gain visibility.

You can engage in hands-on workshops covering critical topics such as fundraising strategies, scaling operations, and talking about new technologies.

Whether you're a startup founder, investor, or tech enthusiast, TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 offers valuable insights and connections that can help your business move forward in the ever-evolving tech landscape.

[Click here to register!](https://techcrunch.com/events/tc-disrupt-2026/)

## 11. SF Tech Week 2026

**Location**: San Francisco, USA

**Dates**: October 05–11, 2026

**Who should attend**

Startup founders, tech enthusiasts, and entrepreneurs who are eager to learn about technology and crypto.

Tech Week takes place across four cities in the United States - Boston, New York City, San Fransisco, and Los Angeles this year.

This decentralized conference, presented by Andreessen Horowitz, aka a16z, features over 2,000 events across San Francisco, attracting over 75,000 attendees from various sectors.

You can expect to hear from influential speakers like Marc Andreessen and Ben Horowitz, along with a diverse lineup of entrepreneurs and thought leaders of their field.

The week includes engaging workshops, panel discussions, and networking opportunities designed to create collaboration among startups, investors, and tech enthusiasts.

If you’re seeking new partnerships or newer insights into emerging trends, SF Tech Week offers a unique platform to elevate your tech journey and answer what you are looking for.

[Click here to register!](https://www.tech-week.com/)

## **Why Should You Attend These Events?**

Attend these SaaS events in 2026 for unique opportunities to learn from industry leaders, engage in hands-on workshops, and connect with like-minded professionals.

Each event is designed to give rise to innovation and provide a platform for networking, making them essential for founders, product managers, and investors.

Also, meet Inkle at these exciting events! We’re eager to connect with you, share and engage in meaningful conversations about the future of SaaS and technology.

Our team will be present at these gatherings, ready to discuss how we can collaborate and innovate together. Whether you’re a founder, investor, or industry professional, we’d love to hear your thoughts, learn, and grow.

Don’t miss the chance to meet us in person.

Stop by and say hello!

# SaaS Conferences: Events for Founders and Operators in 2026

Author :

Published On :

Updated On :

![saas-conferences](https://vendelux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/saas-conferences.png)

SaaS has reached a more demanding phase.

Growth is harder to earn. Buyers are more cautious. Founders are expected to balance speed with efficiency, while teams are pushed to deliver more impact with less stress. In this environment, learning in isolation is no longer enough.

That is why SaaS conferences matter in 2026.

SaaS conferences bring together founders, executives, investors, and operators who are actively building, scaling, and running SaaS businesses. These events create space for honest conversations, shared learning, and practical problem-solving that cannot happen over webinars or blog posts.

With dozens of SaaS events on the calendar, the challenge is not finding conferences. The challenge is choosing the ones that actually move your business forward.

This guide explains how SaaS conferences work, what they are best used for, and how to approach attending them with clear intent.

## What SaaS Conferences Are Designed to Deliver

SaaS conferences sit at the intersection of product, growth, go-to-market, and operations. Unlike general tech events, they focus on the realities of recurring revenue businesses and the decisions that determine long-term success.

Most SaaS conferences fall into a few distinct categories.

**Founder- and operator-led conferences**  
Events like SaaStr Annual are built around real experiences. SaaS founders, executives, and operators share unfiltered stories about what worked, what failed, and what they would do differently. These conferences often attract SaaS leaders, growth leaders, and decision makers looking for practical guidance.

**Growth, marketing, and revenue-focused events**  
Many SaaS conferences center on growth strategies, customer success, pricing, retention, and expansion. Marketing professionals, sales leaders, and customer success teams attend these events to learn how SaaS companies drive sustainable growth.

**Investor and ecosystem events**  
Some SaaS events bring together investors, startups, and technology providers. These conferences support relationship building, fundraising conversations, and insight into where capital and innovation are moving next.

**Community-driven and regional conferences**  
From the San Francisco Bay Area to Las Vegas and global hubs like Web Summit, these events emphasize community, networking, and peer learning across the SaaS industry.

## Why SaaS Conferences Matter in 2026

The SaaS industry is no longer about chasing growth at any cost.

Founders and executives now focus on efficiency, durable revenue, and strong fundamentals. SaaS conferences reflect this shift. They prioritize actionable insights, practical frameworks, and real examples over hype.

SaaS professionals attend these conferences to:

For many SaaS founders, conferences also provide something harder to find elsewhere: perspective. Hearing how other teams navigate complexity helps leaders make better decisions with more confidence.

## Common Themes Across SaaS Conferences

While each SaaS conference has its own emphasis, several themes appear consistently in 2026.

**Scaling with discipline**  
Sessions focus on how SaaS businesses scale faster without sacrificing margins or culture.

**Customer success and retention**  
Customer success plays a central role, with discussion around onboarding, expansion, and long-term value.

**Product-led and go-to-market innovation**  
SaaS conferences explore how teams align product, marketing, and sales in changing markets.

**Investment and funding realities**  
Investors and founders discuss valuation, capital efficiency, and what “good” looks like in the current market.

**Next generation leadership**  
Many conferences highlight leadership development and how executives and founders evolve as companies grow.

## How to Choose the Right SaaS Conference

With so many SaaS conferences and SaaS events available, choosing wisely matters.

Consider the following before committing.

**Who it’s for**  
Some conferences are best for early-stage startups. Others focus on later-stage SaaS companies and experienced executives.

**Format**  
Look for panel discussions, workshops, roundtable discussions, and in person sessions that match how you learn best.

**Audience quality**  
The value of a SaaS conference often comes from who attends. Like minded professionals and peers drive better conversations than sheer volume.

**Location and timing**  
Major SaaS conferences often take place in San Francisco, the Bay Area, or Las Vegas. Balance travel time with expected outcomes.

## How to Approach SaaS Conferences Strategically

SaaS conferences deliver the most value when approached with intention.

### Set Clear Outcomes

Before attending, decide what you want to gain. Outcomes may include:

Clear focus shapes how you spend time at the event.

### Prioritize Real Conversations

SaaS conferences are built around networking. Fantastic networking opportunities often happen outside sessions, during evening events, workshops, or informal meetups.

Focus on meaningful conversations rather than collecting contacts.

### Apply What You Learn

The best SaaS conferences leave you with ideas you can act on. Share insights with your team and turn learning into execution.

## Using Vendelux to Plan SaaS Conferences and Book Meetings

SaaS conferences bring together founders, executives, investors, marketing professionals, and technology providers. Without clarity on who is attending, teams often rely on chance meetings.

Vendelux helps SaaS teams approach conferences with precision.

Teams can define their ideal customer profile (ICP), upload target account lists, or connect CRM data to see which SaaS conferences attract the right attendees. This makes it easier to identify the best SaaS conferences, top SaaS events, and must attend events based on real attendee data.

Before the event, Vendelux enables teams to:

## Planning a SaaS Conference Calendar for 2026

Most SaaS teams attend multiple conferences each year. A clear calendar helps avoid overload and supports better planning.

Strong planning balances large flagship conferences with smaller community events, workshops, and regional gatherings. It ensures the right people attend the right events and that conference time supports broader business goals.

# India SaaS & Marketing Tech Summit 2026

**Bengaluru, India**

### What to expect

As AI and next-gen platforms reshape enterprise technology, marketing and sales leaders must accelerate growth, deepen customer value, and prove impact. This summit brings together CMOs, CROs, growth strategists, and product marketers from SaaS, cloud, and enterprise tech to share AI-enabled GTM plays and data-driven growth frameworks.

### Conference format

**This 2-day conference addresses key challenges in mega-project delivery, focusing on:**

In addition to our traditional formats—keynotes, panel discussions, and expert presentations—we’re introducing interactive sessions for enhanced learning and practical application.

#### DAY 1

**Focus**: Aligning national infrastructure visions with mega-project execution. Explore how senior leaders, PMOs, and investors collaborate to deliver giga-scale infrastructure efficiently and sustainably. Learn about governance, risk management, and project prioritization within Vision 2030 portfolios.

#### DAY 2

**Focus**: Driving execution through technology, AI, and sustainable practices. Discover how digital twins, predictive analytics, smart city solutions, and ESG frameworks are applied in Gulf mega-projects. Learn practical strategies for workforce upskilling, supply chain optimization, and leveraging technology for resilient infrastructure.

# Top SaaS Events & Conferences to Attend in 2026-27

![](https://secure.gravatar.com/avatar/3b3330af978bbf7fb52a272b6bb23f98cc7a537d6c59b90dabde3635ded60a36?s=48&d=mm&r=g)

Sagrika Jain

SaaS events remain one of the highest-ROI investments a SaaS business can make. When so much of the workday is mediated by AI tools and async communication, the value of a room full of people solving the same problems is not diminishing. It is compounding.

The real return from SaaS conferences does not come from the keynote stage. It comes from the hallway conversation with the CRO who just restructured her team. The dinner where you meet your next VP of Sales before they’re on the market. The investor introduction comes from being in the right building at the right time.

The [2024 SaaStr survey of 500+ SaaS operators](https://www.saastr.com/) found that 68% of respondents cited “peer networking” as the primary value driver of conference attendance.

This guide maps every SaaS event worth attending in 2026 – 27. The conferences and events are organised around the role that will get the most from each, with dates, entry fees, and explanations of why they belong on your calendar.

## What Are the Best SaaS Events for Founders in 2026-27?

Founders need a different kind of event than everyone else on this list. They are not shopping for tools or sessions; they are looking for validation, peer community, investor visibility, and the kind of frank conversation that only happens when 300 people who have made the same mistakes are in the same room.

### [SaaStr Annual 2026](https://www.saastrannual.com/)

**Dates:** May 12–14, 2026  
**Location:** San Mateo County Events Center, San Francisco Bay Area, CA  
**Price:** Free community passes (limited) | Full access from ~$1,000  
**Attendance:** 10,000+ founders, VCs, and executives  
**Theme:** Winning in the Age of AI

SaaStr Annual is the world’s largest SaaS-specific conference. According to [SaaStr’s own event data](https://www.saastr.com/), the 2026 edition will feature 300+ sessions across dedicated tracks for CROs, CMOs, CCOs, and [GTM leaders](https://ultratalent.com/gtm-leaders/). For founders specifically, the density of VC access and peer-level conversation makes this worth the flight from anywhere in the world. The free community pass tier makes it one of the most accessible saas founder events on the calendar.

### [SaaStock USA 2026](https://www.saastock.com/)

**Dates:** April 15–16, 2026  
**Location:** Palmer Events Center, Austin, TX  
**Price:** ~$499 early bird | ~$999+ standard

SaaStock brings together B2B SaaS founders, operators, and investors with an explicit focus on scaling from $1M to $100M ARR. What makes SaaStock stand out among SaaS networking events is its curation — attendees are overwhelmingly operators and founders, not vendors. As a result, the conversation quality is consistently high.

### [SaaStock Europe 2026](https://www.saastock.com/)

**Dates:** October 13–14, 2026  
**Location:** Dublin, Ireland  
**Price:** €719 (Pre-seed/Seed, early bird) | €1,199–€1,499 (Series A+ and investors)

SaaStock’s Dublin flagship is one of the best SaaS leadership conferences in Europe for scaling businesses. The tiered pricing by company stage is genuinely founder-friendly, and the investor density makes it a productive stop for anyone within 12 months of a fundraise.

### [MicroConf US 2026](https://microconf.com/)

**Dates:** April 12–14, 2026  
**Location:** Portland, OR  
**Price:** ~$500–$1,500

MicroConf is intentionally small, around 250 attendees, and that is the point. It is the original community for self-funded SaaS founders and the conversations are frank by design. No vendor floor, no corporate keynotes, just founders comparing notes on churn models, pricing experiments, and product decisions. Among SaaS entrepreneurship events, there is nothing quite like it.

### [MicroConf Europe 2026](https://microconf.com/)

**Dates:** TBC (historically autumn)  
**Location:** Reykjavik, Iceland  
**Price:** Check [microconf.com](https://microconf.com/) for pricing when announced

The European edition of MicroConf brings the same bootstrapped founder community to Iceland. For self-funded European SaaS operators, this is the most relevant community event on the continent.

### [TechCrunch Disrupt 2026](https://techcrunch.com/events/)

**Dates:** October 13–15, 2026  
**Location:** San Francisco, CA  
**Price:** From ~$895 | Startup Battlefield entries separate

Disrupt skews more media-facing than operator-focused. But for early-stage founders seeking visibility with investors and press, the Startup Battlefield competition remains one of the highest-profile launchpads in tech. Best for founders actively seeking visibility in Series A.

## Which SaaS Conferences Are Best for Sales Leaders?

The best SaaS sales conferences in 2026 have moved decisively away from motivational keynotes toward operationally dense content on AI-assisted selling, GTM motion design, and buyer behaviour change. These are the ones that matter.

### [Dreamforce 2026](https://www.salesforce.com/dreamforce/)

**Dates:** September 15–17, 2026  
**Location:** San Francisco, CA  
**Price:** From $1,599 | Multiple tiers available  
**Attendance:** 40,000+ in-person and virtual

Dreamforce is the largest enterprise sales conference in the world and the centrepiece of the Salesforce ecosystem. The 2026 edition builds on the Agentforce platform, with a major focus on AI-assisted selling, Sales Cloud automation, and revenue intelligence. [Salesforce’s event page](https://www.salesforce.com/dreamforce/) confirms the September window — early registration typically opens six months in advance. For teams running Salesforce, this is effectively mandatory.

### [Gartner CSO & Sales Leader Conference 2026](https://www.gartner.com/en/conferences/na/sales-us)

**Dates:** May 19–20, 2026  
**Location:** Las Vegas, NV  
**Price:** From ~$3,500 | Early bird discount of $525 available before March 2026

The Gartner CSO conference is all about strategy. Every session is grounded in Gartner research data, quota design, AI impact on the buyer journey, sales talent retention, targeting CROs, VP Sales, and Chief Sales Officers at mid-market and enterprise companies. Among SaaS sales conferences, this is the most research-grounded.

### [SaaStr Annual 2026 — Sales & CRO Track](https://www.saastrannual.com/)

**Dates:** May 12–14, 2026  
**Location:** San Francisco Bay Area, CA  
**Price:** From free (community) | ~$1,000 full access

SaaStr’s dedicated CRO and sales leadership track features operators who have scaled from $1M to $100M ARR, covering comp design, territory planning, pipeline quality, and the founder-led-to-repeatable-GTM transition.

### [Revenue Operations Summit — Multiple Cities](https://www.corinium.com/)

**Dates:** March 11–12, 2026 (New York) | Multiple dates across Austin, San Francisco, Boston  
**Price:** From ~$300–$800 depending on city

This event series covers the GTM alignment topics most sales leaders need: CRM, pipeline forecasting, AI in sales ops, and marketing-to-sales orchestration. Accessible geography across multiple US cities makes it one of the most practical SaaS networking events for revenue leaders.

## What Are the Top Revenue Operations Conferences?

RevOps has matured from a job title into a strategic function — and the conferences that serve it have matured accordingly. These are operationally dense, technically grounded events attended by practitioners who are building systems, not attending theory sessions.

### [RevOpsAF US Conference 2026](https://revopsaf.com/)

**Dates:** May 6–7, 2026  
**Location:** San Francisco, CA  
**Price:** ~$400–$800 (community-priced, check [revopsaf.com](https://revopsaf.com/) for 2026 pricing)

RevOpsAF is the first SaaS conference built by RevOps practitioners, for RevOps practitioners. The agenda is crowd-sourced from the community — which means sessions reflect real challenges rather than vendor talking points. Topics span CRM architecture, attribution modelling, AI in revenue workflows, and GTM alignment. The most community-driven of all the RevOps conferences on this list.

### [Revenue Operations Summit New York 2026](https://www.corinium.com/)

**Dates:** March 11–12, 2026  
**Location:** Convene 360 Madison, New York, NY  
**Price:** From ~$400–$800

The New York edition draws from the financial services, enterprise software, and media SaaS ecosystems that dominate the city. Co-location with GTM Engineering and Sales Enablement tracks on some dates gives it a broader scope than its single-function title suggests.

### [ARRtist Summit 2026](https://arrlist.org/)

**Dates:** October 8, 2026  
**Location:** Berlin, Germany  
**Price:** Historically €500–€900 (check [arrlist.org](https://arrlist.org/) for 2026 pricing)

ARRtist Summit is Europe’s most metrics-dense SaaS event. The Berlin edition brings together the continent’s top SaaS operators and finance leaders for rigorous discussion around recurring revenue models, unit economics, and revenue quality. For European RevOps and finance leaders, this is the one SaaS leadership conference that speaks their language precisely.

### [Gartner CSO & Sales Leader Conference 2026](https://www.gartner.com/en/conferences/na/sales-us)

**Dates:** May 19–20, 2026  
**Location:** Las Vegas, NV

Beyond its sales leadership content, the Gartner CSO conference runs deep material on revenue operations — pipeline forecasting accuracy, CRM adoption, and AI integration into sales workflows. RevOps leaders who need Gartner research to support internal investment cases will find this particularly useful.

## What Are the Best SaaS Customer Success Conferences?

Customer success has become a revenue function — not a support function — and the events that serve it now reflect that shift. The best SaaS customer success conferences in 2026 sit at the intersection of retention strategy, product analytics, and commercial expansion.

### [Gainsight Pulse 2026](https://www.gainsight.com/pulse/)

**Dates:** May 27–28, 2026  
**Location:** Caesars Forum, Las Vegas, NV  
**Price:** ~$1,000–$2,000 | Gainsight customers typically receive discounts  
**Attendance:** 5,000+ CS professionals

Pulse is the world’s largest customer success conference. According to [Gainsight’s event page](https://www.gainsight.com/pulse/), the 2026 Las Vegas edition focuses on customer-led growth: the thesis that elite CS drives expansion revenue, not just retention. Sessions cover churn prevention, digital CS at scale, AI in health scoring, and the CS-to-product feedback loop. Among SaaS customer success conferences, Pulse is non-negotiable. If you attend one CS event in 2026, this is it.

### [Product-Led Summit Berlin 2026](https://productled.com/summit)

**Dates:** October 14, 2026  
**Location:** The Ritz-Carlton, Berlin, Germany  
**Price:** Historically €600–€1,200 (check [productled.com](https://productled.com/summit) for 2026 pricing)

The Product-Led Summit Berlin sits at the intersection of product and customer success — specifically the retention and monetisation outcomes PLG models drive. For CS leaders at product-led companies, sessions cover activation, adoption, expansion, and the feedback loops between product usage data and CS interventions.

### [SaaStr Annual 2026 — CCO Track](https://www.saastrannual.com/)

**Dates:** May 12–14, 2026  
**Location:** San Francisco Bay Area, CA  
**Price:** From free (community) | ~$1,000 full access

SaaStr’s Chief Customer Officer track is consistently one of the strongest at the conference. The 2026 edition addresses how AI is changing CS economics — AI-assisted QBRs, automated health scoring, and the shifting ratio of CSMs to accounts as AI handles routine touchpoints.

### [SaaStock USA and Europe 2026 — CS Sessions](https://www.saastock.com/)

Both SaaStock USA (April, Austin) and SaaStock Europe (October, Dublin) run dedicated customer success programming within their broader GTM tracks. For CS leaders who also want founder and revenue context alongside CS content, SaaStock offers the cross-functional breadth that single-function CS events cannot.

## Which SaaS Marketing Conferences Should You Attend in 2026?

SaaS marketing conferences have split into two clear camps: brand-and-demand generalists and deeply technical performance or PLG specialists. The best ones for 2026 sit at the intersection.

### [Demand & Expand 2026](https://demandandexpand.com/)

**Dates:** March 18–19, 2026  
**Location:** New York, NY  
**Price:** ~$500–$1,000 (check [demandandexpand.com](https://demandandexpand.com/) for confirmed pricing)

Demand & Expand tackles the most pressing challenge in SaaS marketing right now: how to blend PLG and sales-led acquisition into a coherent GTM model. Sessions are built for CMOs and GTM leaders scaling past the self-serve ceiling. Among SaaS marketing events, this is the one most directly focused on the hybrid GTM motion that most B2B SaaS companies are navigating in 2026.

### [Forrester B2B Summit 2026](https://www.forrester.com/event/b2b-summit-north-america/)

**Dates:** April 26–29, 2026  
**Location:** Phoenix, AZ  
**Price:** From $2,650+

Forrester’s B2B Summit is the most research-grounded marketing conference on this list. Every session is anchored in Forrester’s buyer behaviour data, and the analyst access allows marketing leaders to pressure-test strategies against independent research. For enterprise SaaS marketers, this is essential.

### [Adobe Summit 2026](https://summit.adobe.com/)

**Dates:** April 19–22, 2026  
**Location:** Las Vegas, NV  
**Price:** From $2,195

Adobe Summit is the flagship enterprise MarTech conference. The 2026 edition focuses on AI-powered personalisation, content supply chain, and integrated customer data across digital touchpoints. The hands-on labs and product roadmap access are the primary draws for SaaS marketers running complex multi-channel programmes.

### [Content Marketing World 2026](https://www.contentmarketingworld.com/)

**Dates:** October 5–7, 2026  
**Location:** San Diego, CA  
**Price:** Historically $1,200–$1,800 (check [contentmarketingworld.com](https://www.contentmarketingworld.com/) for 2026 pricing)

Content Marketing World is the world’s largest gathering of content marketers, with 100+ sessions across content strategy, SEO, thought leadership, and video. For SaaS marketers building inbound through conten the depth here is unmatched.

### [SaaStr Annual 2026 — CMO Track](https://www.saastrannual.com/)

**Dates:** May 12–14, 2026  
**Location:** San Francisco Bay Area, CA  
**Price:** From free | ~$1,000 full access

SaaStr’s CMO track covers positioning, category creation, demand generation in an AI-saturated content landscape, and the metrics SaaS boards actually care about. For B2B SaaS marketers, the operator peer density makes this the highest-signal SaaS marketing conference of the year.

### [Product-Led Summit Washington 2026](https://productled.com/summit)

**Dates:** January 28, 2026  
**Location:** Washington, D.C.  
**Price:** Check [productled.com](https://productled.com/summit) for pricing

One of the earliest events in the calendar, the Washington edition focuses on how established SaaS businesses adopt PLG principles without disrupting existing sales motions. Useful for marketing leaders at post-IPO or mid-market companies navigating the PLG transition.

## General SaaS Conferences Worth Your Calendar in 2026

These events do not fit neatly into a single function — they are broader gatherings where the value is cross-functional, and networking spans every SaaS discipline.

### [SaaSiest 2026](https://saasiest.com/)

**Dates:** May 5, 2026  
**Location:** Slagthuset, Malmö, Sweden  
**Price:** Historically €600–€900 (check [saasiest.com](https://saasiest.com/) for 2026 pricing)

SaaSiest is the Nordic region’s flagship SaaS event — one of the most curated in Europe. The Malmö venue keeps it intimate by design, elevating the quality of conversation over scale. For European SaaS leaders who want to build genuine relationships rather than collect business cards, SaaSiest consistently overdelivers.

**Dates:** April 27, 2026  
**Location:** MGM Grand Resort, Las Vegas, NV

Kaseya Connect is the leading event for MSPs and IT operations teams within the SaaS ecosystem. The 2026 edition focuses on cybersecurity automation, AI-driven managed services, and MSP commercial models. Highly relevant for SaaS companies building for the IT and security buyer.

### [SuiteWorld 2026](https://www.netsuite.com/portal/events/suiteworld.shtml)

**Dates:** October 26, 2026  
**Location:** Las Vegas, NV  
**Price:** Historically $1,500–$2,500 for non-customers (check [NetSuite’s events page](https://www.netsuite.com/portal/events/suiteworld.shtml) for confirmed pricing)

SuiteWorld is NetSuite’s annual flagship conference and the most important event in the ERP and financial software SaaS calendar. For SaaS companies selling into the CFO function — or those running their own business on NetSuite — this is a high-value destination for learning and partnership development.

## SaaS Events and Conferences to Watch in 2027

The 2027 SaaS conference calendar is still forming, but based on historical cadence, the major recurring events are expected to continue as follows:

* **[SaaStr Annual 2027](https://www.saastrannual.com/)** — San Francisco Bay Area, May/September window. Registration typically opens 6 months in advance.
* **[SaaStock Europe 2027](https://www.saastock.com/)** — Dublin, October. Early-bird pricing usually opens 9 months out.
* **[Gainsight Pulse 2027](https://www.gainsight.com/pulse/)** — Las Vegas, May/June residency.
* **[Dreamforce 2027](https://www.salesforce.com/dreamforce/)** — San Francisco, September.
* **[MicroConf 2027](https://microconf.com/)** — US (spring) and Europe (autumn) editions.
* **[Gartner CSO & Sales Leader Conference 2027](https://www.gartner.com/en/conferences/na/sales-us)** — May window, location TBC.

The major trend heading into 2027: AI-native tracks are becoming the primary programming spine at most major SaaS conferences, replacing the broad GTM content that dominated 2022–2024 calendars. Conferences that fail to credibly address AI’s impact on SaaS business models will lose attendance share to those that do. Expect new AI-specific SaaS events to emerge in this window — particularly around Agentic GTM and AI-assisted revenue operations.

## How to Choose the Right SaaS Event for Your Role and Stage

With 25 events competing for your budget and 15 working days of conference time across the year, a clear decision framework matters.

1. **SaaS founders under $3M ARR** → [MicroConf](https://microconf.com/), [SaaStock USA](https://www.saastock.com/), [SaaStr Annual](https://www.saastrannual.com/) (free community pass)
2. **SaaS founders raising or scaling $3M–$30M ARR** → [SaaStr Annual](https://www.saastrannual.com/), [SaaStock USA](https://www.saastock.com/) and Europe, [TechCrunch Disrupt](https://techcrunch.com/events/)
3. **VP Sales and CROs** → [Dreamforce](https://www.salesforce.com/dreamforce/), [Gartner CSO Conference](https://www.gartner.com/en/conferences/na/sales-us), [SaaStr Annual](https://www.saastrannual.com/) (CRO track)
4. **RevOps leaders** → [RevOpsAF](https://revopsaf.com/), [Revenue Operations Summit](https://www.corinium.com/) (nearest city), [ARRtist Summit](https://arrlist.org/)
5. **Customer Success directors** → [Gainsight Pulse](https://www.gainsight.com/pulse/) (non-negotiable), [SaaStr Annual](https://www.saastrannual.com/) (CCO track)
6. **CMOs and marketing leaders** → [SaaStr Annual](https://www.saastrannual.com/), [Forrester B2B Summit](https://www.forrester.com/event/b2b-summit-north-america/), [Demand & Expand](https://demandandexpand.com/), [Content Marketing World](https://www.contentmarketingworld.com/)
7. **European-based teams** → [SaaStock Europe](https://www.saastock.com/), [SaaSiest](https://saasiest.com/), [ARRtist Summit](https://arrlist.org/), [Product-Led Summit Berlin](https://productled.com/summit)

The SaaS networking events that pay off most are those you attend with a specific objective — a type of person to meet, a question to get answered, or a decision to pressure-test. Go with intent.

## FAQ — SaaS Events & Conferences

**What are the best SaaS conferences to attend in 2026?**  
The best SaaS conferences in 2026 are SaaStr Annual (May, San Francisco — from free), Gainsight Pulse (May, Las Vegas — ~$1,000–$2,000), SaaStock USA (April, Austin — from $499), Dreamforce (September, San Francisco — from $1,599), and Forrester B2B Summit (April, Phoenix — from $2,650). The right event depends on your role — see the role-by-role breakdown above.

**What are the best free SaaS events in 2026?**  
SaaStr Annual offers limited free community passes for 2026 — making it the highest-value free SaaS event available. Kaseya Connect is free for existing Kaseya customers. Some virtual sessions at Dreamforce are also available for free. Most in-person SaaS conferences charge between $400 and $3,500 for full access.

**What is SaaStr Annual and who should attend?**  
SaaStr Annual is the world’s largest SaaS-specific conference, held annually in the San Francisco Bay Area. It draws 10,000+ founders, VCs, CROs, CMOs, and GTM executives across three days. It is most valuable for SaaS founders scaling between $1M and $100M ARR, and for GTM leaders wanting peer-level conversations with operators at similar stages.

**Are there SaaS conferences specifically for customer success teams?**  
Yes. The most important SaaS customer success conference is Gainsight Pulse (May 27–28, Las Vegas) — the world’s largest CS-specific gathering. SaaStr Annual also runs a dedicated Chief Customer Officer track. SaaStock USA and Europe both include CS programming within their broader GTM tracks.

**What are the best SaaS marketing conferences in 2026?**  
The best SaaS marketing conferences in 2026 are Demand & Expand (March, New York), Forrester B2B Summit (April, Phoenix), Adobe Summit (April, Las Vegas), SaaStr Annual CMO Track (May, San Francisco), and Content Marketing World (October, San Diego). For B2B SaaS specifically, SaaStr and Demand & Expand are the most operator-relevant.

**What are the top RevOps conferences in 2026?**  
The top revenue operations conferences in 2026 are RevOpsAF US (May, San Francisco), Revenue Operations Summit New York (March), ARRtist Summit (October, Berlin), and the Gartner CSO & Sales Leader Conference (May, Las Vegas). RevOpsAF is the most community-driven; Gartner is the most research-grounded.

**Do SaaS events run in 2027?**  
Yes — all major SaaS conferences are expected to run in 2027 on their established annual cadences. SaaStr Annual, Gainsight Pulse, Dreamforce, SaaStock Europe, and MicroConf have all confirmed ongoing annual formats. 2027 specific dates and locations will be announced through their respective official sites, typically 6–9 months in advance.

**How much does it cost to attend a SaaS conference?**  
SaaS conference prices in 2026 range from free (SaaStr community passes, Kaseya Connect for customers) to $3,500+ (Gartner CSO Conference). The majority of mid-tier events fall between $500 and $1,800. Early bird discounts of 20–40% are available at most events 3–6 months before the conference date.

## Final Thoughts

The SaaS events calendar in 2026 is more role-specific and operationally focused than ever. The era of the monolithic tech conference trying to serve every function at once is giving way to tightly focused gatherings where the ROI is real and measurable.

For GTM and sales leaders specifically, the right SaaS conference is the one where your peers are solving the same problem you are working on this quarter. That overlap — of timing, function, and urgency — is what separates an event that changes how you operate from one that is just a line item on an expense report.

For UltraTalent clients and candidates navigating the SaaS hiring market, these events are also where the talent moves. The best AEs, CROs, and CS leaders in SaaS attend these gatherings — and so do the companies competing to hire them. Knowing which rooms to be in, and when, is its own form of GTM intelligence.

### “Has SPRING become the new FALL for SaaS events?”

[Ray Rike](https://substack.com/@rayrike1)

Mar 05, 2025

# **SaaS Events - 2025**

## *“Has SPRING become the new FALL for SaaS events?”*

As a former SaaS operator, I was not a fan of trade shows that were primarily focused on extracting sponsor dollars for booths under the auspices of brand awareness and lead generation.

I did enjoy industry conferences that had top tier speakers who were willing to share their insights, experiences, strategies and tactics that I could take back to my company peers to discuss if they were applicable to our company. The bonus of some events was when I could sit at lunches or dinners that were structured around “birds of a feather” topics that I was interested in learning more about from industry peers that had different or even more experience than my own, but shared interest in the same subject matter.

Thanks for reading SaaS Barometer Newsletter! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.

Now as an event creator and host, I invest a lot of time evaluating the aspects of events that work best as measured by the value of the learnings for attendees, ability to develop meaningful connections that last beyond the final session and for sponsors who were able to create brand awareness with their executive and economic buyers that they did not previously know.

Below are some SaaS events I have attended, spoke at or hosted that I believe are worthy of your time to evaluate and consider sponsoring or attending.

---

### **[Pavilion CMO Summit](https://www.joinpavilion.com/cmo-summit): April 17th in Atlanta, GA**

The CMO Summit is a premier event designed for Chief Marketing Officers and senior marketing leaders in B2B SaaS. Hosted by **Pavilion**, a global community of go-to-market (GTM) leaders, the summit focuses on the latest trends, challenges, and strategies in modern marketing.

The event is known for its strong networking environment, and content from leading CMOs across the SaaS industry.

*👉 Exclusive for CMOs & Senior Marketing Leaders – This is a high-level event tailored for marketing executives, providing a space for in-depth discussions and networking.*

*👉 Actionable Insights – Sessions cover topics like demand generation, brand positioning, pipeline acceleration, marketing attribution, AI in marketing, and more.*

*👉 Top-Tier Speakers – Industry-leading CMOs from top SaaS and enterprise companies share real-world insights and strategies.*

*👉Networking Opportunities – The event brings together top marketing professionals for peer-to-peer learning, collaboration, and mentorship.*

*👉 Workshops & Panels – Expect a mix of keynote presentations, interactive panel discussions, and breakout sessions designed to provide practical takeaways.*

---

### **[SaaStock USA](https://saastock-usa.com/): May 13th - 14th in Austin, TX**

SaaStock USA is a conference for SaaS founders, executives, and investors looking to scale their businesses, build connections, and gain insights from top industry leaders. It is part of the global SaaStock series, which originated in Dublin, Ireland, and has expanded to major markets, including North America.

It will be interesting to see how the acquisition of SaaSOpen is integrated into SaaStock USA 2025. SaaStock is introducing a new [SaaS Finance Forum](https://saastock-usa.com/finance-forum/) this year, sponsored by Sage and will include Ben Murray, David Appel and Ray Rike as featured speakers.

*👉 Focuses on growth strategies, customer acquisition, fundraising, and product-led growth for scaling SaaS companies.*

*👉 Features successful SaaS founders, investors, and executives from high-growth companies sharing real-world insights.*

*👉 Connect with VCs, angel investors, and fellow SaaS entrepreneurs looking to collaborate and fund promising startups.*

*👉 Hands-on sessions covering marketing, sales, churn reduction, and scaling operations.*

*👉 Ideal for early-stage to growth-stage SaaS companies looking for strategies to accelerate their revenue and expansion.*

---

### **[SaaStr Annual](https://www.saastrannual.com/): May 13th - 15th in San Mateo, CA**

SaaStr Annual was first introduced in 2015 is now one of the largest and longest running SaaS conferences, bringing together a reported 10,000+ founders, executives, investors, and revenue leaders to share insights, network, and learn how to scale their businesses. It’s known for its top-tier speakers, valuable networking opportunities and trade show floor to maximize sponsorships.

Recently SaaStr introduced new sessions to attract a broader audience including the Mayfield AI Demo Stage in 2025, a CMO Brunch and a CFO Summit at SaaStr Annual 2024 which are both rumored to be repeated at SaaStr Annual 2025.

*👉100% Focused on Scaling SaaS Businesses – Learn from top SaaS leaders, unicorn founders, and VCs on how to scale from $1M to $100M+ ARR.*

*👉 300+ Tactical Sessions & Workshops – Hands-on learning in growth, sales, marketing, customer success, fundraising, and leadership.*

*👉Networking with 10,000+ Attendees – Meet founders, executives, and investors from SaaS companies worldwide.*

*👉VC & Investor Matchmaking – Over 1,000+ VCs and investors attend, making it a great event for fundraising and partnerships.*

*👉 Outdoor Festival Vibe – SaaStr Annual is held outdoors in San Francisco, with networking lounges, mentoring sessions, and evening parties.*

---

### **[Benchmarkit SaaS Executive Dinner Series](https://www.benchmarkit.ai/executive-dinner-series-events/25-nyc-may): May 19th in NYC**

Benchmarkit and The SaaS CFO conduct a series of intimate dinners for SaaS CFOs and CEOs to discuss the latest trends and challenges for the financial stewards of SaaS companies. Limited to 50 guests, this dinner will be taking place on a luxury boat circling Manhattan for three hours. Seats are limited and carefully curated to ensure the highest quality discussions and learnings that can be applied to the attendees company.

*👉100% Focused on topics that CFOs and CEOs can learn from and apply in their company*

*👉 Intimate and elevated experiences with executive peers who understand or have been in the other attendees roles*

*👉Each table has a dedicated topic and moderator to keep the discussions moving and focused on the challenges and best practices each attendee is open to sharing*

---

### **[Benchmarkit SaaS Metrics Executive Summit NYC](https://www.benchmarkit.ai/saas-metrics-executive-summit/new-york-city): May 20th in New York City**

Building upon the SaaS Metrics Palooza virtual event and Executive Dinner Series, this first of its kind in-person event was purpose built for SaaS CFOs and CEOs who are the financial stewards of their SaaS company. A half-day event that begins with a keynote speaker, followed by four different roundtable breakout sessions that are led by industry thought leaders who are experts on trending topics that are challenging SaaS financial leaders such as: 1) ARR calculation and reporting in a usage-based environment; 2) Planning, Budgeting and Forecasting in a variable pricing world; 3) GTM metrics and analytics frameworks that provide CFOs and CEOs visibility into how different GTM input signals will impact planned and forecasted performance and; 4) Enterprise Value creating frameworks that optimize revenue growth performance and maximize company value; 5) Introduction and monetization of new AI functionality and products.

*👉 Move beyond listening to great speakers, and learn by interacting with industry peers who are looking to solve or have solved the same challenges your are facing*

*👉 Meet and learn from industry thought leaders who are sought out for their deep experience and understanding of the business challenges you face and the latest strategies to address*

*👉 Each breakout roundtable is conducted twice to ensure audience members can attend at least two of the most relevant discussions in a compressed half-day timeframe*

*👉 Following the end of the half-day session, birds of a feather tables for lunch allow for attendees to continue the discussion with their peers and the industry leaders*

---

### **[Gainsight Pulse](https://gainsightpulse.com/us/): May 28th - 29th in Las Vegas**

Gainsight Pulse is one of the largest customer success and product experience conferences in the world, bringing together CS, CX, and SaaS leaders to discuss the latest trends in customer retention, expansion, and product-led growth. Hosted by Gainsight, this event is a must-attend for professionals focused on customer success, revenue retention, and driving Net Revenue Retention (NRR).

*👉 Customer Success & Product-Led Growth Focus – Learn from top CS and SaaS leaders on how to improve retention, reduce churn, and drive expansion revenue.*

*👉 Industry-Leading Speakers – Features CSOs, CCOs, founders, and product leaders from high-growth SaaS companies.*

*👉 Workshops & Tactical Sessions – Covers customer success strategies, AI in CS, community-led growth, and monetization strategies.*

*👉 Networking with 5,000+ Attendees – Connect with CS, CX, and product professionals, plus investors and thought leaders.*

*👉 CS & Product Innovation – Showcases the latest technology, automation, and data-driven strategies to improve customer outcomes.*

---

### **[Pavilion CRO Summit](https://www.joinpavilion.com/cro-summit): June 3rd in Denver, CO**

The Pavilion CRO Summit is an exclusive event designed for Chief Revenue Officers (CROs) and senior sales, marketing, and revenue leaders. Hosted by Pavilion, a leading global community for GTM executives, this summit focuses on strategies to drive sustainable revenue growth, improve sales efficiency, and align go-to-market (GTM) functions

*👉* Exclusive for CROs & Revenue Leaders – This event is tailored specifically for senior executives responsible for revenue growth, sales, marketing, and customer success.

*👉* Actionable, Data-Driven Insights – Sessions cover scaling revenue, sales efficiency, AI in sales, and pipeline optimization.

*👉* Top SaaS & GTM Experts – Learn from experienced CROs, sales leaders, and investors from high-growth SaaS companies and enterprises.

*👉* Networking with Peers & Investors – Connect with fellow CROs, revenue leaders, and potential partners to exchange best practices and explore business opportunities.

---

### **Benchmarkit SaaS Metrics Executive Summit: September 10th in San Francisco**

Building upon the SaaS Metrics Palooza virtual event, executive Dinner Series, and the first SaaS Metrics Executive Summit in New York this in-person event is for SaaS CFOs and CEOs who are the financial stewards of their SaaS company. A half-day event that starts with a keynote session is then followed by four different roundtable breakout sessions that are led by an industry thought leaders and experts on topics that are currently trending and challenging SaaS financial leaders.

*👉 Move beyond listening to great speakers, and learn by interacting with industry peers who are looking to solve or have solved the same challenges your are facing*

*👉 Meet and learn from industry thought leaders who are sought out for their deep experience and understanding of the business challenges you face and the latest strategies to address*

*👉 Each breakout roundtable is conducted twice to ensure audience members can attend at least two of the most relevant discussions in a compressed half-day timeframe*

*👉 Following the end of the half-day session, birds of a feather tables for lunch allow for attendees to continue the discussion with their peers and the industry leaders*

---

### **[HubSpot Inbound](https://www.inbound.com/): September 3rd - 5th in San Francisco**

INBOUND is HubSpot’s annual conference, bringing together marketing, sales, customer success, and business leaders from around the world. It’s one of the largest digital marketing and business growth conferences, featuring top-tier speakers, actionable insights, and networking opportunities.

*👉 World-Class Speakers – Past speakers include Barack Obama, Oprah Winfrey, Reese Witherspoon, and HubSpot’s top executives. Expect big-name keynote speakers from tech, business, and media.*

*👉 Marketing, Sales, & Customer Success Focus – Sessions cover everything from inbound marketing, AI in sales, SEO, content strategy, automation, and RevOps.*

*👉 HubSpot Product Announcements – Exclusive insights into new HubSpot CRM features, AI-powered tools, and marketing automation updates.*

*👉 Workshops & Breakout Sessions – Hands-on training with marketing, sales, and CX experts to gain actionable strategies.*

*👉 Networking & Partner Meetups – Connect with industry peers, HubSpot partners, and potential clients in dedicated networking lounges.*

---

### **[Pavilion GTM2025](https://attendgtm.com/): September 25th - 26th in Washington D.C**

Pavilion GTM 2025 is a premier event designed for go-to-market (GTM) leaders, including CROs, CMOs, VPs of Sales, Marketing, Customer Success, and Revenue Operations. Hosted by Pavilion, this summit focuses on the latest strategies, tools, and best practices for driving revenue growth, improving sales efficiency, and aligning marketing, sales, and customer success functions.

*👉 Exclusive for GTM Leaders – The event is tailored for senior executives and revenue leaders looking to refine their GTM strategy.*

*👉Tactical & Data-Driven Insights – Sessions cover topics like pipeline generation, AI in sales, marketing attribution, and customer success-driven revenue growth.*

*👉Top-Tier Speakers – Features CROs, CMOs, founders, and GTM experts from high-growth SaaS and enterprise companies.*

*👉 Networking & Peer Learning – Connect with hundreds of revenue leaders, investors, and industry experts to exchange insights.*

---

### **[SaaS Metrics Palooza ‘25](https://www.benchmarkit.ai/saas-metrics-palooza-24): October 7th and 8th (virtual)**

SaaS Metrics Palooza is the largest event dedicated to the sharing of best practices for how SaaS operators and investors use metrics and benchmarks in decision making. This virtual event attracts over 2,500 attendees with an NPS score of 80 and an intent to return of over 98%.

SaaS Metrics Palooza is known for its top tier speakers and industry experts such as Geoffrey Moore (Crossing the Chasm), Nick Mehta (Gainsight), Dave Kellogg (Kellblog), Godard Abel (G2), Byron Deeter (Bessemer Ventures), Alex Clayton (Meritech Capital), Sam Jacobs (Pavilion), Jacco Van Der Kooij (Winning by Design), David Spitz (KeyBanc Benchmark Report), Nathan Latka, Ben Murray (SaaS CFO) Jon Miller (Marketo), CJ Gustafson (Mostly Metrics), Kyle Poyar (OpenView) and Mark Roberge (Stage 2 Capital) to highlight just a few..

This event is ideal for anyone looking to gain data-driven insights into how to measure, optimize, and scale a SaaS business effectively.

*👉 Deep-Dive into SaaS Metrics and Benchmarks – Learn how to track and improve critical SaaS metrics, including ARR, CAC, LTV, NRR, GRR, Burn Rate, Rule of 40, Rule of X and many more.*

*👉 Expert Speakers & Panelists – Features SaaS founders, CEOs, CFOs, VCs, analysts, and GTM leaders sharing insights on measuring and accelerating revenue growth efficiency*

*👉 Virtual & Accessible – 100% online, making it easy to attend from anywhere in the world.*

*👉 Investor & VC Perspectives – Learn what investors look for in SaaS businesses and how to present your metrics for fundraising success.*

---

### **[SaaStock Europe](https://saastock-europe.com/): October 14th - 15th in Dublin, Ireland**

​SaaStock Europe is a premier annual conference that brings together SaaS (Software as a Service) founders, executives, and investors from across Europe. The event serves as a hub for networking, learning, and growth within the SaaS community.​

*👉 The conference attracts a diverse group of participants, including early-stage startups, scaling companies, and established enterprises, all eager to share insights and collaborate.​*

*👉 Attendees can expect a rich agenda featuring keynote speeches, workshops, and bootcamps. These sessions are designed to provide* **actionable insights on** scaling businesses, enhancing revenue, and navigating the challenges unique to the SaaS industry.

---

### **[Salesforce Dreamforce](https://www.salesforce.com/form/dreamforce/df25-save-the-date/): October 14th - 16th in San Francisco**

**​**Dreamforce has been Salesforce's flagship annual conference since 2003, bringing together a global community of professionals for learning, inspiration, and networking.

Attendees can anticipate visionary presentations from Salesforce executives and industry leaders, focusing on topics like AI, automation, and digital transformation.

*👉 The conference offers a variety of workshops, breakout sessions, and training labs, catering to diverse roles from administrators to developers. Participants can gain actionable insights and pursue certifications.*

*👉 Salesforce+ Streaming: For those unable to attend in person, Salesforce provides a virtual experience through Salesforce+, allowing global access to key sessions and product announcements.*

*👉 Beyond the main agenda, numerous community-led events and meetups are planned, offering additional networking opportunities and knowledge sharing.*

---

### **[SaaS North](https://saasnorth.com/): November 5th and 6th in Ottawa, Canada**

SaaS North is Canada's premier conference dedicated to SaaS professionals, serving as a central hub for founders, executives, investors, and their teams to connect, share knowledge, and foster growth within the SaaS ecosystem.

*👉 The conference offers sessions tailored to various aspects of SaaS business, including leadership, finance, product development, customer success, sales, and marketing. Attendees can gain tactical knowledge from industry experts and successful entrepreneurs. ​[SaaS North](https://saasnorth.com/program-overview/)*

*👉 Past events have featured prominent figures such as Neil Patel (Co-Founder, Neil Patel Digital), Stephany Lapierre (Founder & CEO, Tealbook), Tope Awotona (CEO & Founder, Calendly), and Tobias Lütke (Founder, Shopify).*

*👉 With over 2,000 attendees and 800 companies expected, SAAS NORTH provides ample opportunities for networking, collaboration, and deal-making among SaaS professionals.*

*👉 The conference hosts pitch events, such as the SAAS NORTH Pitchfest, where startups can showcase their innovations to potential investors and partners*

---

### **Summary**

As you can see from the above, there are so many different SaaS conferences and events that one can attend. Ideas to ensure you are attending the right event for you and to get the largest ROI from your time and capital investment - below are some key evaluation criteria for which event to attend:

❓ What is the primary goal of attending and how will you measure the ROI you receive

* *If generating leads via a trade show booth is your goal - attend the biggest like Dreamforce, Inbound or SaaStr which also include a “have some fun” element*
* *If learning from peers in a more intimate setting is your goal - consider executive dinners that are curated to ensure the majority of attendees have similar backgrounds and topics of interest*
* *If learning from and meeting founders and entrepreneurs in earlier stage companies, events like SaaStock are a good fit*
* *If learning from the industry’s thought leaders from the comfort of your own office or home in a live or on-demand environment check out SaaS Metrics Palooza*
* *If meeting and learning from peers in your specific function, like Sales or Marketing is your goal, the CMO, CRO and GTM events held by Pavilion are an excellent choice*
* *If meeting and learning from peers in your part of the world, especially Europe it’s hard to go wrong with SaaStock Europe*

[Register now](https://www.benchmarkit.ai/b2b-marketing-benchmarks-webinar) to secure your spot and get ahead of the latest trends!*
