# SaaS Events To Attend in 2025; You Cannot Miss Them

![Heer Chheda](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%2096%2096'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![Heer Chheda](https://secure.gravatar.com/avatar/3400b83f4449911ac73b720711e73c7d4b211a4c917568c1b77a5e88328851b7?s=96&d=mm&r=g)

As we gear up for 2025, there is a list of events that you need to be on the lookout for. These events present opportunities for you and your peers to interact, learn, gather *‘ungoogleable’* insights, and have some fun.

Whether your experiences have been good or bad, there’s always something you can learn. Let’s take a look at them.

## 16 SaaS events to attend in 2025

From major gatherings like the Saastr Annual in San Mateo to niche conferences that focus on product-led growth, here’s a list of 15 must-attend events

### SaaStr Annual

[SaaStr Annual](https://www.saastrannual.com/) is truly a highlight in the SaaS community, bringing together over **10,000 founders, executives, and investors**. With more than **250 speakers**, including industry leaders and innovative thinkers, this event offers a wealth of insights and strategies that can spark your curiosity and inspire new ideas.

You’ll have the chance to participate in **over 2,000 meetings**, AMAs, and one-on-one roundtables, making it a fantastic opportunity to network and learn from others in the field. Plus, the targeted matchmaking sessions with **1,000+ VCs** give you direct access to potential investors, allowing you to explore funding opportunities in a meaningful way.

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%200%200'%3E%3C/svg%3E)![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXcV10ilQEgRZXYVF1Evezj64IiAvQYDqUc2vHPte-S1OY7Zedj9b4LAsrd2s7vrUCftOj6iGe3BbLVlwCMCNO5ce3OD-ZE-PAhiG6R4IqWIl5W9Dkf7gKll2gPmOkFi6dQN09HnQnsnc36_y8jg6bUyv5w?key=4CGFEYXU1NRS2XTIBkd40w)
![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXcV10ilQEgRZXYVF1Evezj64IiAvQYDqUc2vHPte-S1OY7Zedj9b4LAsrd2s7vrUCftOj6iGe3BbLVlwCMCNO5ce3OD-ZE-PAhiG6R4IqWIl5W9Dkf7gKll2gPmOkFi6dQN09HnQnsnc36_y8jg6bUyv5w?key=4CGFEYXU1NRS2XTIBkd40w)

Date: May 13 to May 15, 2025

Location: San Mateo, California

Price: Starting from $1,149 per attendee.

### SaaStock USA

[SaaStock](https://saastock-usa.com/) offers **SaaScity bootcamps** that dive into essential topics like CEO strategies, revenue retention, and marketing mastery. You’ll also find dedicated events such as **Startup Day** and the **Global Pitch Competition Day**, which are fantastic opportunities to engage with investors and decision-makers.

At the heart of SaaStock is the goal of building meaningful connections with peers and industry leaders. With the help of an AI-powered networking app, you can easily connect with fellow attendees who share your aspirations and vision, making it easier to forge valuable relationships.

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%200%200'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXfVdN1w1jyFvfDyBovHSvauGQJORTSKelDH_xmcFKX8vsvQDh1xqkmLvTcHylpLVCv11Jcp-Z2KqhBEMyurhQ6EX1COogT16T1XkD9DpG87hlVYljxX4-sq0na70FvYL06P2rSCqSJXAPiqBzcfJAfVGA?key=4CGFEYXU1NRS2XTIBkd40w)

Date: May 12th to May 14th, 2025

Location: Austin, Texas

Price: TBD

### SXSW

[SXSW](https://www.sxsw.com/conference/) is a vibrant event that spans multiple industries, bringing together top talent in SaaS, film, music, and culture, creating a rich melting pot of opportunities and innovations. It’s a place where you can dive into cutting-edge ideas, discover new interests, and connect with a diverse range of professionals.

The event covers a wide array of topics, from advertising and brand experience to climate sustainability and emerging technologies, ensuring there’s something for everyone to engage with. You can look forward to inspiring keynotes and sessions featuring prominent figures like Dr. Peter Attia and Dr. Laurie Santos.

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%200%200'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXcjzXjfSFs0-KAIH1_Oz3KuG_mvl3diRnh2vpI7fioQDtyLVV5xcGxJQ9KFID3gNhhR0cQfvu_aRgByiCIA6l43Yru6HbZyxlvuuzw2Dinvw7qbvEwMx67-Y6tvFSu9sT2fIso1J2F4eOUYBh0jN7LfUa0?key=4CGFEYXU1NRS2XTIBkd40w)

Date: March 7th to March 15th, 2025

Location: Austin, Texas

Price: Registration details available on the website

### PLGTM Summit

If you’re looking to dive into cutting-edge strategies for product-led growth, the [**PLGTM Summit**](https://www.plgtm.com/) is the event to attend. With over **600 industry leaders and professionals** from sales, marketing, and marketing operations, this summit serves as a vibrant platform for inspiration and collaboration.

In previous years, the PLGTM Summit has demonstrated a strong track record of success, boasting a Net Promoter Score of **74**. This impressive score indicates that attendees found significant value in their experience

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%200%200'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXdKiHjsyeUolMu6O-edvC9CLzlcajfdapKeIbUXgm9ngWlrWc8GAnNB-K-iaEdogM5_YVVGoWKVrQAunDDBvRVpsdSWjQcAxVC8qg-VJV0KTDK9eCHc2oJ7VkknYgtf1Q1mUffYywWrvh0IKW6C6TtCW-U?key=4CGFEYXU1NRS2XTIBkd40w)

Date: May 14, 2025

Location: San Francisco, California

Price: Registration details available on the website

### Adobe Summit

If you work in marketing or customer experience, [Adobe Summit](https://summit.adobe.com/na/) should be your must attend.

This is a hybrid event, (in-person and online) with over 200 sessions. From Adobe’s vision for AI powered digital experiences to the innovations coming out of Adobe Labs, the event encapsulates your attention. It also delivers key notes on delivering solid customer experience.

In the Community Pavilion, you can connect with Adobe experts, speakers, and other attendees.

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%200%200'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

### SaaSOpen

[SaaSOpen](https://saasopen.com/2024/austin) is an intimate gathering event designed to foster interactive sessions. It is an exclusive, invite-only event tailored for post-revenue SaaS CEOs and CXOs with annual revenue of up to $ 500 million, making it a unique opportunity for industry leaders to share their valuable insights.

Additionally, you can join the SaaS CEO and CXO Poker Night, where you can network in a relaxed setting.

**Pro tip:** If you’re planning to attend SaaSOpen, it’s recommended to stay for SaaStock, which will happen on the following days: May 12-13, 2025.

SaaS North usually has a diverse lineup of speakers, sessions, and networking opportunities that facilitate strong connections among industry professionals.

### ISC2 Security Congress

[ISC2 Security Congress](https://www.isc2.org/professional-development/events) 2025 is where you can get ready for an immersive experience into the very latest in the world of cybersecurity. At this conference, listen to informative sessions, watch inspiring keynotes from industry leaders and gain invaluable insights with the help of plenty of tutorials. Find out how artificial intelligence shapes the security measures and how to overcome it.

And although knowledge is just a part of it, the connections you’ll make is what it’s really about. You’ll have many chances to interact with peers and leaders in the cybersecurity community to build relationships that may help grow you professionally.

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%200%200'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

Here’s what our founder had to say about the conference this year:

My speech at this year’s hashtag#ISC2SecurityCongress in Las Vegas is the culmination of numerous conversations I’ve had with over 40 cybersecurity leaders.

These insights wouldn’t have been possible without those exchanges—because, in my experience, conversations shape strategies far better than any war room discussions ever could.

If you’re wondering why you should visit these events, let us tell you.

## Importance of SaaS events

SaaS events, or any SaaS conferences, for that matter, are perfect for networking as they draw investors, business people, and experts. This facilitates a rapport that could help you fetch funding, mentorship or even collaboration. If your business is trying to find its place in the industry, these mixers and events are a great source of interaction to establish yourself.

In fact, SaaS events also focus on sharing useful information, case studies, and interesting techniques like workshops. You get to interact with the companies which have built their product from deep and get inspired to follow the same path of their success.

Finally, SaaS events foster a sense of community. They provide a space for individuals to share experiences, challenges, and successes, creating an environment of support.

We attend these events, too!

## Sprinto at SaaS events

Over the last three years, Sprinto has dived into various SaaS conferences and events, sponsoring and attending numerous gatherings that have transformed into invaluable experiences. Breaking bread with others in the industry and sharing stories about our work has created a relaxed space where learning happens organically.

Here are some of the events we have attended:

SaaS conferences are not just the events, they’re also moments of genuine connection that can be nearly impossible to quantify. It’s what truly makes them special; no pretense. You are not pressured to fit into a certain mold or put on a polished facade. Instead, everyone comes together with a shared goal: as we navigate the challenges of building our businesses.

In these conversations, you come together and realize that while we’re all dealt different cards, we all want to make sure our visions come to life. This brings a relationship and a community, which both expands your perspective and helps you to grow as an individual, but also as a collective. These experiences were interesting due to their authenticity and the shared passion surrounding them were actually the reason these were actually worthwhile.

Let’s take a look at some of the events

# Web3 Festival Unveils 2026 Speaker Lineup, Featuring Leaders from BlackRock, OKX, Solana, Sharplink and Ondo

03-23-2026 02:28 PM CET | [IT, New Media & Software](/news/categories2-1-IT-New-Media-Software.html)  

## Press release from: [Binary News Network](/news/archive/273033/Binary-News-Network.html "archive und pressrelease of Binary News Network")

/ PR Agency: ZEX PR WIRE

[![Web3 Festival Unveils 2026 Speaker Lineup, Featuring Leaders](https://cdn.open-pr.com/L/3/L323357384_g.jpg)](https://cdn.open-pr.com/L/3/L323357384_g.jpg "Web3 Festival Unveils 2026 Speaker Lineup, Featuring Leaders")

Hong Kong, 23rd March 2026, ZEX PR WIRE - Following the sellout success of its previous editions, Hong Kong Web3 Festival has announced its return on 20-23 April at HKCEC.  
   
 Hong Kong Web3 Festival ("Web3 Festival"), co-hosted by Wanxiang Blockchain Labs and HashKey Group, is Asia's premiere crypto conference since 2023. Now in its 4th year, this four-day event will once again bring tens of thousands of Web3 professionals, investors, and enthusiasts across the globe to connect, learn and be inspired.  
   
 In-Depth Discussions to Navigate the Web3 Landscape  
   
 Web3 Festival 2026 has set the agenda for Web3 growth and innovation, focusing mainly on traditional finance and crypto finance, AI + Web3, and RWAs.  
   
 The Convergence of Traditional Finance and Crypto Finance - This year's Web3 Festival brings together leading global financial institutions to engage in dialogues with crypto-native forces. Discussions will be centered on the game-changing stablecoins and the emergence of a new global payment system.  
   
 AI + Web3 - The synergistic evolution of AI and blockchain is giving rise to a new generation of financial infrastructure. As AI evolves from a "conversational assistant" into on-chain entities capable of autonomously executing economic activities-such as OpenClaw-and as protocols like x402 and standards like ERC-8004 are paving the way for an AI-driven agent economy, the boundaries of technological convergence are being pushed ever forward. Many AI-native projects, Web3 pioneers, and leading investment institutions will be gathered for dialogues exploring the future of AI and Web3 integration.  
   
 RWAs - RWA serves as a critical bridge connecting traditional finance with the digital economy. Web3 Festival 2026 unites globally renowned institutions like BlackRock, CICC, and Deloitte China Hong Kong, alongside top-tier law firms and securities organizations, to explore the practical guidance and future landscape of global RWA issuance.  
   
 Incredible Speaker Lineup Released  
   
 The conference will convene top experts and leading Web3 projects to exchange ideas, share best practices and gain perspectives on today's evolving Web3 landscape.  
   
 Featured speakers include:  
   
 Paul CHAN Mo-po, GBM, GBS, MH, JP, Financial Secretary of the Government of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region  
   
 Dr. YIP Chee Hang, Executive Director of Intermediaries, Securities and Futures Commission  
   
 Xiao Feng, Chairman of Wanxiang Blockchain, Chairman and CEO of HashKey Group  
   
 Duncan Chiu, Legislative Council Member (Technology & Innovation Constituency), Hong Kong  
   
 Zhao Yao, Distinguished Senior Research Fellow, National Institution for Finance & Development  
   
 Adeniyi Abiodun, Co-Founder & Chief Product Officer, Mysten Labs  
   
 Lily Liu, President, Solana Foundation  
   
 Joseph Chalom, CEO, Sharplink  
   
 Francis B. Zhou, CEO, Quantum Solutions

 Lennix Lai, Chief Commercial Officer, OKX Global  
   
 Bugra Celik, Head of Digital Assets and Currencies at Global Macro, HSBC  
   
 Robert Lui, Deloitte China Hong Kong Digital Asset Leader; Member of Task Force on Promoting Web3 Development  
   
 DIAO Zhihai, Head of International Business of Wealth Management, CICC  
   
 Chris Lee, Vice President, ETF China Asset Management (Hong Kong)  
   
 Gavin Wang, Managing Partner & Chief Investment Officer, SNZ Holding & SNZ Capital  
   
 Phil Kang, CEO, ZR Financial Group  
   
 David Lee, Chairman, Global FinTech Institute  
   
 Min Lin, Managing Director, Head of Global Business Development at Ondo Finance  
   
 Ru Haiyang, CEO, HashKey Exchange Business Group  
   
 Anna Liu, HashKey Tokenisation CEO, HashKey Group  
   
 Rita Liu, CEO, RD Technologies  
   
 Leo Lin, Founder, Arkreen

 Cynthia Wu, Founding Partner & Chief Commercial Officer, Matrixport  
   
 Arda Senoz, Ecosystem Lead, Alchemy Pay  
   
 Andrew Fei, Partner, King & Wood Mallesons  
   
 Lana ZHAO, Chief Representative of Hong Kong Station, Sina Finance

 Ben Zhai, CEO, Robo.ai

 Patty Tu, Web 3.0 Business Research Manager | Innovation and R&D, China Mobile (Hong Kong) Innovation Research Institute Co., Ltd  
   
 Jianqiu Chen, Executive Director, Hong Kong Robotics Group Holding Limited

 Ivy Peng, Founder, Zillion Intelligence

 \*Speakers are presented in no particular order.  
   
 A Strong Partner Network  
   
 Hong Kong Web3 Festival 2026 has forged a strategic alliance with Cyberport and the region's trade statutory body, Hong Kong Trade Development Council (HKTDC). Furthermore, the event has revealed an impressive lineup of 2026 sponsors so far, including:  
   
 Title: OKX Wallet  
   
 Platinum: Finanx AI, MSX, Qtum, TokenPocket, Zircon Securities  
   
 Co-organizer: Arkreen, RD Technologies, SNZ  
   
 Exclusive Dinner Sponsor: Sui Foundation  
   
 Gold: Alchemy Pay, MaxQuant  
   
 Primary: Anubis Chain, bitbaby, CoinP, ENI Network, HappyMeta, Hotcoin, SlowMist, Websea  
   
 Secondary: Aspire, ChainUp, Digital Reserve, Ellipal, Fufuture, Passey, PlatON, SoonTech, W3SA-HK, Zbit  
   
 Tertiary: Alpha Hunter, Black Water, Cobo Global, DeSaFa, EcoSync, Evolving Capital, iCloser, RWA.LTD, TiDB, Upay, XBIT  
   
 Open Stage: 0G, Boundless, DESUN SINGULARITY  
   
 \*Same-tiered are presented in alphabetical order.  
   
 Additionally, media outlets including BlockTempo, Caixin Global, CoinNess, Foresight News, PANews, TechFlow, Wu Blockchain, Sina Finance, ZEX PR WIRE, and Zhihu (in alphabetical order) will host exhibitions on site.  
   
 Join Hong Kong Web3 Festival on April 20 - 23 and be part of an epic Web3 journey: https://luma.com/hkweb3festival\_2026

# List of Blockchain Events, Conferences, and Hackathons 2025 – 2026

Promote your event or explore media partnerships—reach us here: [events@timesofblockchain.com](mailto:events@timesofblockchain.com)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| 10-17, 2026 | [Power Purchase Agreement (Spring Session)](https://www.infocusinternational.com/ppa-online)  Live Online  ---   Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs) operate in many different contexts. They can be used to support the development of new projects, or for the supply of energy from existing projects. They can be written by independent power producers, governments or utilities. They can operate in regions with sophisticated power exchanges and markets, and they can also operate in developing countries with a single buyer and a centralized monopoly power utility. In the sessions of this course, we cover each of these contexts. |
| Mar  04-26, 2026 | [Aleph March '26](https://aleph.crecimiento.build/)  Buenos Aires, Argentina  ---   The Aleph March ’26 is a dedicated crypto-native conference designed as a long-form builder environment for Web3 teams, researchers, and protocol developers. Unlike traditional blockchain summits that emphasize short, high-intensity sessions, Aleph focuses on extended collaboration, co-working, and co-living, creating space for projects to evolve over weeks rather than days. The program is structured around multiple touchpoints across March, enabling participants to test ideas, ship code, and receive feedback in real time. With its emphasis on hands-on collaboration, infrastructure workshops, and governance dialogues, Aleph March ’26 acts as a point of convergence for diverse crypto ecosystems, fostering deeper knowledge exchange and long-term innovation. |
| Mar  12-21, 2026 | [AI x CRYPTO EXPO 2026](https://aixcryptoexpo.com/)  Silicon Valley, USA  ---   The AI x CRYPTO EXPO 2026 in Silicon Valley is a groundbreaking event at the intersection of artificial intelligence and blockchain innovation. * January 12, 2026

Eager to explore the most compelling web3 events of 2026? We’ve sifted through countless event announcements, spoken directly with their organizers, and drawn on our industry contacts to compile this definitive list of the year’s standout blockchain and crypto conferences. From must-attend global summits to hidden gems that deliver high-value insights, these events cover the full scope of decentralized tech.

As a [development company with blockchain experience](https://redwerk.com/services/blockchain-development/) that’s collaborated with diverse clients worldwide, we knew how to prioritize the conferences capable of sparking meaningful partnerships and innovative breakthroughs. Whether you’re an investor aiming to identify the next big web3 trend, a founder polishing your pitch, or a developer hungry for cutting-edge solutions, these meetups provide the perfect environment to learn, connect, and grow. Here’s to embracing the latest in decentralization and forging powerful alliances in 2026!

## January 2026

### CFC St. Moritz, Jan 14–16, 2026, Davos, Switzerland (In-Person)

Looking for substance, not just buzzwords? CFC St. Moritz is where crypto’s dealmakers and traditional finance minds actually talk shop. A carefully curated digital assets and blockchain conference for just 250 top investors, founders, and policymakers. If you want crowd-free networking and strategic conversations, swap the busy expo floor for St. Moritz’s luxury alpine setting.

Forget the hype, focus on real impact. Here you’ll unpack the fresh intersection of blockchain, digital assets, and traditional finance: everything from post-quantum cryptography to live tokenization workshops. The discussions dive deep into tangible opportunities: tokenizing real-world assets, cross-chain DeFi for institutions, and navigating the new regulatory winds from the US and Europe.

**Pricing:**

* Invite-only

This four-day summit cuts through the noise to bring together heavyweight leaders, innovators, and decision-makers in a setting that’s equal parts sophisticated and friendly. Only the right people get in, so every conversation counts.

Set against the Swiss Alps, Web3 Hub Davos dials in on the transformative power of blockchain. Expect curated, invite-only sessions — from digital asset regulation to real-world adoption and how decentralized tech is shaking up global industries. You’ll join insiders, founders, and policymakers for genuinely meaningful discussions and all-day casual networking over refined local cuisine.

* Invite-only

## Reasons to Attend Crypto Events & Conferences in 2026

Dying to attend a crypto conference but not sure how to pitch it to your boss? Let us do the heavy lifting. We’ve put together the most convincing arguments to help you secure those tickets you’ve been eyeing. Here is why attending these events is a smart investment:

* **Cost-Effective Networking:** You don’t need the most expensive VIP ticket to get results. Often, the highest-ROI interactions occur at “side events”, such as breakfasts, happy hours, and hackathons hosted by specific projects, rather than on the main stage. A General Admission pass (or even just being in the city during the event) is often enough to access the high-value networking zones where the real deals get done.
* **The AI x Crypto Convergence:** By 2026, the merger of artificial intelligence and blockchain will be the industry’s defining narrative. Conferences are the best place to see live demos of AI agents (software that transacts autonomously) and verify how blockchain authenticates AI data. It’s the only way to move beyond social media buzzwords and understand the technology in action.
* **Building Real-World Trust:** In an industry full of anonymous founders and avatars, face-to-face interaction is a massive competitive advantage. Shaking hands and sharing a meal fast-tracks trust for partnerships, fundraising, and hiring in a way that months of Zoom calls simply cannot.
* **Early Access to “Alpha”:** Critical information, such as upcoming protocol mergers, funding news, or regulatory shifts, is often shared in private conversations days or weeks before it hits news sites or X (Twitter). Being there in person allows you to position yourself ahead of the public narrative.
* **Institutional Deal-Making:** With clear regulations now in place, traditional finance (banks and asset managers) is heavily involved. Events are no longer just for crypto natives; they are the primary meeting grounds where corporate players and web3 innovators collaborate on integrating real-world assets (like tokenized bonds) into the blockchain.
* **Talent & Recruitment:** Whether you are hiring or looking for work, events are the ultimate job market. Founders often hire developers or marketers they meet at hackathons or after-parties on the spot, as they can instantly gauge culture fit and technical competence.

## Summing Up

Web3 events serve as catalysts for innovation, where diverse communities converge to share knowledge, scout investments, and form alliances. Whether you’re a developer, entrepreneur, investor, or just web3-curious, these 2026 web3 conferences offer invaluable opportunities to stay on top of evolving trends.

# Top Web3 Events & Crypto Conferences in 2026

›[Blog](https://redwerk.com/blog/)›Top Web3 Events & Crypto Conferences in 2026

* January 12, 2026
* 20 min read

Eager to explore the most compelling web3 events of 2026? We’ve sifted through countless event announcements, spoken directly with their organizers, and drawn on our industry contacts to compile this definitive list of the year’s standout blockchain and crypto conferences. From must-attend global summits to hidden gems that deliver high-value insights, these events cover the full scope of decentralized tech.

As a [development company with blockchain experience](https://redwerk.com/services/blockchain-development/) that’s collaborated with diverse clients worldwide, we knew how to prioritize the conferences capable of sparking meaningful partnerships and innovative breakthroughs. Whether you’re an investor aiming to identify the next big web3 trend, a founder polishing your pitch, or a developer hungry for cutting-edge solutions, these meetups provide the perfect environment to learn, connect, and grow. Here’s to embracing the latest in decentralization and forging powerful alliances in 2026!

## January 2026

### CFC St. Moritz, Jan 14–16, 2026, Davos, Switzerland (In-Person)

Looking for substance, not just buzzwords? CFC St. Moritz is where crypto’s dealmakers and traditional finance minds actually talk shop. A carefully curated digital assets and blockchain conference for just 250 top investors, founders, and policymakers. If you want crowd-free networking and strategic conversations, swap the busy expo floor for St. Moritz’s luxury alpine setting.

Forget the hype, focus on real impact. Here you’ll unpack the fresh intersection of blockchain, digital assets, and traditional finance: everything from post-quantum cryptography to live tokenization workshops. The discussions dive deep into tangible opportunities: tokenizing real-world assets, cross-chain DeFi for institutions, and navigating the new regulatory winds from the US and Europe.

**Pricing:**

* Invite-only

[Go to event site](https://cfc-stmoritz.com/ "CFC St. Moritz, Jan 14–16, 2026, Davos, Switzerland (In-Person)")

### Web3 Hub Davos, Jan 19–22, 2026, Davos, Switzerland (In-Person)

If you’re after substance and smart networking — not just endless keynotes — Web3 Hub Davos is the rising star on the blockchain events calendar. This four-day summit cuts through the noise to bring together heavyweight leaders, innovators, and decision-makers in a setting that’s equal parts sophisticated and friendly. Only the right people get in, so every conversation counts.

Set against the Swiss Alps, Web3 Hub Davos dials in on the transformative power of blockchain. Expect curated, invite-only sessions — from digital asset regulation to real-world adoption and how decentralized tech is shaking up global industries. You’ll join insiders, founders, and policymakers for genuinely meaningful discussions and all-day casual networking over refined local cuisine.

**Pricing:**

* Invite-only

[Go to event site](https://web3hubdavos.com/ "Web3 Hub Davos, Jan 19–22, 2026, Davos, Switzerland (In-Person)")

### Satoshi Roundtable, Jan 29–Feb 3, 2026, Dubai, UAE (In-Person)

Looking for next-level privacy and off-the-record depth? Satoshi Roundtable is where crypto’s original visionaries gather away from spotlights, media, and pitch decks. Now in its twelfth year, this invitation-only gathering brings together CEOs, developers, founders, academics, investors, and fintech leaders in a format focused on meaningful, high-level discussion — not hype.

Forget panels and press releases — here you set the agenda. The unconference format means you’re not just an attendee but a contributor among the people steering the course of Web3, Bitcoin, and decentralized finance. It’s an all-inclusive experience: private sessions, gourmet meals, resort comfort, and spontaneous breakout groups on topics like scaling, privacy, sidechains, and quantum-proof security.

**Pricing:**

* Invite-only

[Go to event site](https://satoshiroundtable.org/ "Satoshi Roundtable, Jan 29–Feb 3, 2026, Dubai, UAE (In-Person)")

## February 2026

### Digital Assets Forum, February 5-6, London, UK (In-Person)

Digital Assets Forum is one of the major crypto conferences in Europe, bringing together asset managers, family offices, banks, allocators, and market infrastructure players for two days of private roundtables, networking, and industry-defining panels.

Expect over 1,000 finance professionals, global banks, and VCs on stage, discussing everything from tokenization to regulation, AI, market structure, and DeFi. With dedicated 1:1 meeting zones, exclusive VIP night, and curated exhibition area, this is where the future of financial innovation and compliance gets built.

**Pricing:**

* From £999 + VAT for a two-day general pass (around $1,321)
* From £1,199 + VAT for a two-day VIP pass (around $1,586)

[Go to event site](https://eblockchainconvention.com/digital-assets-forum/ "Digital Assets Forum, February 5-6, London, UK (In-Person)")

### Consensus Hong Kong, Feb 10–12, 2026, Hong Kong (In-Person)

Consensus Hong Kong is the place where Web3 and crypto mean real business. With more than 15,000 attendees from over 100 countries, it truly earns its reputation as Asia’s top blockchain summit, a gateway for global leaders and innovators to connect, strike strategic deals, and set the industry’s next direction.

This crypto event packs four dynamic stages, from high-profile keynotes to specialized hackathons and live pitch sessions. Whether you’re chasing institutional partnerships, exploring the latest in AI and digital assets, or hungry to plug into the action that bridges East and West, this is where the global crypto crowd comes to accelerate business growth and collaborate at the highest level.

**Pricing:**

* From $599 for a standard pass
* From $1,199 for a platinum pass

[Go to event site](https://consensus-hongkong.coindesk.com/ "Consensus Hong Kong, Feb 10–12, 2026, Hong Kong (In-Person)")

### ETHDenver 2026, February 17-21, Denver, Colorado, USA (In-Person)

ETH Denver is the largest annual Ethereum event, blending hackathons, workshops, and an “innovation festival” atmosphere. Core devs, [DeFi protocols](https://redwerk.com/blog/smart-contract-audit-checklist/), and L2 project teams gather here to demo fresh ideas. Beyond dev sessions, expect lively networking parties, pitch competitions, and a full-week immersion into the Ethereum ecosystem.

If you’re a developer seeking collaboration or an investor scouting the next big DeFi or NFT platform, ETHDenver is perfect. Don’t miss the final hackathon showcase — many top web3 startups trace their roots to ETHDenver’s BUIDLathon.

**Pricing:**

* Available upon registration

[Go to event site](https://ethdenver.com/ "ETHDenver 2026, February 17-21, Denver, Colorado, USA (In-Person)")

## March 2026

### Crypto Expo Europe, March 1-2, Bucharest, Romania (In-Person)

Crypto Expo Europe is one of the largest crypto conferences in Eastern Europe. It’s the real deal for anyone looking to catch the latest in blockchain and web3. Hosted at the Radisson Blu, this event attracts over 3,000 founders, devs, VCs, and company leaders across Europe and beyond, all dialed in on what’s hot in crypto, DeFi, and digital assets. You’ll hear from the big players (think Binance, OKX, Bitget) as well as new voices breaking out in the region, all while grabbing practical insights on AI, asset tokenization, and [evolving regulations](https://qawerk.com/blog/cryptocurrency-regulation-mica-dora-dac8/).

Pitch sessions and hands-on workshops set the tone for direct learning, while the show floor buzzes with honest networking and next-gen tech demos. Fresh ideas meet fast dealmaking every year at Crypto Expo, meaning you’re rubbing shoulders with those actually doing the work, not just talking about it. In Central and Eastern Europe, this is where you join the action, find real community, and stay ahead in the fastest-growing crypto hub around.

**Pricing:**

* From €69 + VAT for a standard pass (around $80)
* From €279 + VAT for a VIP pass (around $324)

[Go to event site](https://cryptoexpoeurope.com/ "Crypto Expo Europe, March 1-2, Bucharest, Romania (In-Person)")

### DC Blockchain Summit, March 17–18, 2026, Washington, DC, USA (In-Person)

The DC Blockchain Summit, March 17–18, 2026, brings together top minds in blockchain and crypto policy, from tech founders and C-suite execs to U.S. policymakers. It’s the top stage for direct dialogue as new rules and digital asset strategies get mapped out by names like Donald Trump Jr., Richard Teng, and Anthony Scaramucci. If you want the pulse on the next wave of regulation and innovation, this blockchain conference is where business truly meets the government.

**Pricing:**

* From $149 for a student/gov/active duty pass
* From $2,299 for a VIP pass

[Go to event site](https://www.dcblockchainsummit.com/ "DC Blockchain Summit, March 17–18, 2026, Washington, DC, USA (In-Person)")

### Next Block Expo, March 24–25, 2026, Warsaw, Poland (In-Person)

Next Block Expo transforms Warsaw into Europe’s most energetic crypto and blockchain hub. With over 5,000 attendees and 200 speakers, NBX is designed for hands-on learning, sharp talks, and easy mingling with investors, founders, and tech teams. You’ll see bold pitches, catch insiders from Solana and Bitget, and watch the latest in DeFi, gaming, and AI get unveiled across interactive main stages and workshops.

This edition’s matchmaking platform is lightning-fast, so you’ll actually meet the right partners and clients, not just swap business cards. If you want to tap into what’s next for web3 in Central and Eastern Europe, NBX is the blockchain event you need.

**Pricing:**

* From $48 for a standard pass
* From $545 for a VIP pass

[Go to event site](https://nextblockexpo.com/ "Next Block Expo, March 24–25, 2026, Warsaw, Poland (In-Person)")

### Ethereum Community Conference (EthCC), March 30–April 2, 2026, Cannes, France (In-Person)

The Ethereum Community Conference (EthCC) is Europe’s go-to for all things Ethereum, uniting over 6,500 attendees and 400+ speakers for four days of bold ideas, sharp workshops, and next-level networking. Startups, visionaries, and top VCs meet on the stunning Côte d’Azur to break new ground in blockchain tech, investment, and real-world solutions. The energy is pure community — open, transparent, and packed with real opportunities. This is one of the blockchain conferences that covers many different subjects and addresses different levels of comprehension through talks and workshops

[Go to event site](https://ethcc.io/ "Ethereum Community Conference (EthCC), March 30–April 2, 2026, Cannes, France (In-Person)")

## Reasons to Attend Crypto Events & Conferences in 2026

Dying to attend a crypto conference but not sure how to pitch it to your boss? Let us do the heavy lifting. We’ve put together the most convincing arguments to help you secure those tickets you’ve been eyeing. Here is why attending these events is a smart investment:

* **Cost-Effective Networking:** You don’t need the most expensive VIP ticket to get results. Often, the highest-ROI interactions occur at “side events”, such as breakfasts, happy hours, and hackathons hosted by specific projects, rather than on the main stage. A General Admission pass (or even just being in the city during the event) is often enough to access the high-value networking zones where the real deals get done.
* **The AI x Crypto Convergence:** By 2026, the merger of artificial intelligence and blockchain will be the industry’s defining narrative. Conferences are the best place to see live demos of AI agents (software that transacts autonomously) and verify how blockchain authenticates AI data. It’s the only way to move beyond social media buzzwords and understand the technology in action.
* **Building Real-World Trust:** In an industry full of anonymous founders and avatars, face-to-face interaction is a massive competitive advantage. Shaking hands and sharing a meal fast-tracks trust for partnerships, fundraising, and hiring in a way that months of Zoom calls simply cannot.
* **Early Access to “Alpha”:** Critical information, such as upcoming protocol mergers, funding news, or regulatory shifts, is often shared in private conversations days or weeks before it hits news sites or X (Twitter). Being there in person allows you to position yourself ahead of the public narrative.
* **Institutional Deal-Making:** With clear regulations now in place, traditional finance (banks and asset managers) is heavily involved. Events are no longer just for crypto natives; they are the primary meeting grounds where corporate players and web3 innovators collaborate on integrating real-world assets (like tokenized bonds) into the blockchain.
* **Talent & Recruitment:** Whether you are hiring or looking for work, events are the ultimate job market. Founders often hire developers or marketers they meet at hackathons or after-parties on the spot, as they can instantly gauge culture fit and technical competence.

## Summing Up

Web3 events serve as catalysts for innovation, where diverse communities converge to share knowledge, scout investments, and form alliances. Whether you’re a developer, entrepreneur, investor, or just web3-curious, these 2026 web3 conferences offer invaluable opportunities to stay on top of evolving trends.

At Redwerk, we’re not just following the web3 revolution — we’re actively shaping it. Our blockchain expertise is evident in Tingl, a [secure messaging app](https://redwerk.com/case-studies/tingl/) designed for private conversations and crypto payments. Its beta launch on Product Hunt gained massive traction, leading to its acquisition by a major crypto player. Blockchain is redefining digital security, and we’re committed to building innovative, user-first solutions.

With blockchain’s potential expanding across industries, we continue to push boundaries, exploring new ways to enhance security, privacy, and decentralization. If you’re looking for a partner in web3 development, [let’s connect](https://redwerk.com/contact/)!

At its heart, a zero knowledge proof feels like a cryptographic magic trick of sorts: proving you know a secret, or that a complex computation was performed correctly, without revealing the secret or the computation steps themselves.

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

You’ve likely invested in comprehensive code audits for your smart contracts. That’s essential. But if your project has a token, then a parallel layer of complex risk exists that traditional code analysis alone cannot fully address: the tokenomics.

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/security-audits.svg)

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

The emerging world of Web3 is filled with innovation and exciting opportunities, however, as with any new digital territory, it comes with its share of security risks.

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/Formal-Verification.svg)
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/Formal-Verification.svg)

Building on the blockchain means coding the future and creating smart contracts that run like clockwork – transparent, automated, and secure. But here’s the kicker: ‘immutable’ means mistakes stick. Permanently.

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/On-Chain-Monitoring.png)

Security goes beyond smart contract audits. Detect breaches instantly and receive immediate alerts for any suspicious activities with real-time on-chain monitoring.

![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Incident-response.svg)

Alerts flash of unexpected contract behaviour and funds moving where they shouldn’t.

Security is the lifeblood of trust and the foundation for long-term success in the blockchain arena. But how do you truly know if your project is secure? How do you quantify the strength of your defences against the ever-evolving threat landscape?

![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/Web3-KYC.png)

Your crypto exchange is not so dissimilar to a tightly sealed submarine, plunging into abyssal ocean trenches. Every valve, every weld, every pressure gauge must be perfect – because even the faintest flaw can lead to a catastrophic implosion!

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Industry-research.svg)
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Industry-research.svg)

Trying to keep your head above the ocean of emerging information in the blockchain space feels like a full-time job in itself, doesn’t it?

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/vara.svg)
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/vara.svg)

Comprehensive security audits, transaction monitoring, access control, and risk management solutions, all with proper documentation — everything you need to confidently achieve and maintain VARA compliance.

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/dora-c.svg)
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/dora-c.svg)

Avoid penalties, fines, and reputational risk—achieve full DORA compliance with Hashlock. We cover every critical requirement from ICT risk management frameworks and incident response to third-party scrutiny and beyond.

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Crypto-Advisory02.svg)
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Crypto-Advisory02.svg)

When looking at a Web3 project, people see the dApp, the tokens, the community, and the potential returns. What they don’t see is the ‘invisible code’ – the layers of underlying logic, the complex interactions between smart contracts and protocols, the subtle economic incentives, and the hidden dependencies.

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/Proof-of-Reserves.svg)
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/Proof-of-Reserves.svg)

Audit your system to meet the highest crypto security standard — become CCSS certified and demonstrate that your solution is robust, resilient, and built with industry best practices.

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Industry-research.png)
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Industry-research.png)

Our Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) services deliver protection against internal and external risks. Covering security strategy, data protection, system reviews, third-party risk assessments, compliance and staff training.

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Ecosystem-Hub02.svg)
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Polygon-Auditing02.svg)
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Polygon-Auditing02.svg)

Billions in value, millions of users, and constantly flowing assets across a network of interconnected layers – the Polygon ecosystem is a high-value, high-activity target.

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/BSC02.svg)
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/BSC02.svg)

Binance Smart Chain’s immense popularity is not only a metric of success, but a giant bullseye. With its low barrier to entry and high concentration of users and value, the BSC ecosystem attracts sophisticated attackers looking to capitalise on scale.

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Solana-Blockchain02.svg)
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Solana-Blockchain02.svg)

The sheer volume of value moving and stored within the Solana ecosystem is staggering – billions locked in DeFi protocols, millions in valuable NFTs, and a constant flow of high-speed transactions. This makes Solana a premier target for sophisticated attackers who understand its unique architecture and can capitalise on vulnerabilities at unprecedented speed.

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Zksync02.svg)
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Zksync02.svg)

Everyone knows ZKsync inherits Ethereum’s robust security. That’s foundational to its appeal. You trust the underlying Layer 2 infrastructure, the integrity of the rollups, the power of ZK proofs.

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Cosmos-Auditing02.svg)
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Cosmos-Auditing02.svg)

The power of Cosmos lies in sovereignty; the ability for developers to build custom chains with their own governance, validator sets, and technical stacks – while connecting seamlessly to others.

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Avalanche-Auditing02.svg)
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Avalanche-Auditing02.svg)

Assets on Avalanche move with incredible speed. That’s the promise of rapid finality. But billions in value are now flowing across distinct chains (X, P, C) and into numerous subnets, managed by users via their Avalanche wallet.

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/Stacks-Ecosystem.svg)
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/Stacks-Ecosystem.svg)

Venturing into any blockchain ecosystem, especially one as innovative as the Stacks Ecosystem, comes with its own set of unique challenges. In this regard, security isn’t just a feature, but the foundation upon which your entire project rests.

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20612'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

# Top Crypto & Web3 Conferences to Attend and Network in 2026

![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/ChatGPT-Image-Nov-27-2025-12_03_14-PM-1024x683.png)

Crypto and Web3 conferences remain one of the most powerful ways to tap directly into the people and ideas shaping the industry’s future. These gatherings bring together founders, investors, developers, marketers, regulators, and thought leaders driving the evolution of blockchain, crypto, and Web3. 

They also open the door to partnerships, funding opportunities, deal flow, fresh ideas, technical insights, and practical hacks you can apply immediately. This guide breaks down the top crypto and Web3 events for 2026, organized by region, and includes confirmed dates and locations. 

## **North America**

North America remains the beating heart of global crypto innovation and the largest institutional presence. The region hosts high-impact events that shape narratives, spark funding deals, and bring together thousands of builders, founders, and investors. Below are the top crypto and Web3 events in the USA, Canada and North America as a whole in 2026.

### **1. ETH Denver**

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201872%20669'%3E%3C/svg%3E)![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/ETH-Denver.png)

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201872%20669'%3E%3C/svg%3E)![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/ETH-Denver.png)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/ETH-Denver.png)

Marking our first entry in the North America lineup, **ETHDenver** stands as the world’s largest and longest-running Ethereum innovation festival. At this builder-first gathering, developers, founders, designers, and Web3 creatives come together to experiment, build, and push the next wave of decentralized tech.

At the heart of the event is #BUIDL City, a hybrid digital-and-physical playground where thousands collaborate on prototypes, attend workshops, join hackathons, and network across themed innovation zones. ETHDenver blends technical depth with festival energy.

**Date:** February 17–21, 2026

**Country:** USA

**Location:** National Western Center, Denver, Colorado

**Website:** [ethdenver.com](https://ethdenver.com?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

**Socials:** [@ETHDenver](https://x.com/ethereumdenver) (X), [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/company/ethdenver/)

### **2. Bitcoin 2026 – Las Vegas**

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201465%20418'%3E%3C/svg%3E)![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Bitcoin-2026-las-vegas.png)

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201465%20418'%3E%3C/svg%3E)![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Bitcoin-2026-las-vegas.png)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Bitcoin-2026-las-vegas.png)

Bitcoin 2026 is shaping up to be the global gathering for Bitcoin believers, institutions, and innovators, a three-day party-meets-summit that brings together everyone from miners and devs to macro investors and policy leaders. Passed speakers include: Michael Staylor, Jack Dorsey, and Cathie Wood. 

The event is built around a powerful narrative: mining and the convergence of Bitcoin with AI. Pass options range from General Admission (for curious Bitcoiners) to Pro and Whale Passes, which unlock enterprise stage access, VIP networking lounges, and premium experiences.

**Date:** April 27–29, 2026

**Country:** USA

**Location:** The Venetian Resort, 3355 S Las Vegas Blvd, Las Vegas, NV

**Website:** [2026.b.tc](https://2026.b.tc/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

**Socials:** [@TheBitcoinConf](http://@thebitcoinconference)

### **3. Consensus  Miami**

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201693%20670'%3E%3C/svg%3E)![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/consensu-miami.png)

With over 20,000 attendees from more than 100 countries, Consensus Miami promises 6 stages, 6 summits, more than 200 sessions, and over 500 speakers, covering cutting-edge topics across DeFi, tokenization, AI, and global finance.

**Date:** May 5–7, 2026

**Country:** USA

**Location:** Miami Beach Convention Center, Florida

**Website:**  [consensus.coindesk.com](http://consensus.coindesk.com)

### **4. Blockchain Futuristic Conference**

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201701%20513'%3E%3C/svg%3E)![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/blockchain-futuristic-conference.png)

The event will feature three main stages, expo halls, AI and Web3 tracks, bootcamps, VIP cabanas, and high-energy networking spaces.  With over 250 speakers expected, the program covers blockchain, AI, DeFi, tokenization, regulation, and the future of finance.

**Date:** July 21–22, 2026 

**Country:** Canada

**Location:** Rebel Entertainment Complex & Cabana Pool Bar, Toronto

### **5. DC Blockchain Summit**

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201861%20762'%3E%3C/svg%3E)![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/DC-blockchain-summit.png)

The DC Blockchain Summit brings together top government regulators, lawmakers, and Web3 industry leaders to influence the future of blockchain in the United States. The event is designed to foster critical conversations about regulation, innovation, and economic growth, offering direct access to members of Congress, federal agency leaders, and C-level executives from the blockchain space.

In 2025, over 1,100 attendees turned up, with 70% C-Suite representation, 30+ lawmakers, and 600+ companies already confirmed. DC Blockchain Summit 2025 notable speakers include Paul Atkins (former SEC Chairman),  Sergey Nazarov (Chainlink), Richard Teng (CEO, Binance), among others.

## **Europe**

Europe remains one of the most active and diverse regions for blockchain innovation. The region hosts some of the industry’s most influential conferences, blending regulation-focused discussions with deep technical content and enterprise blockchain use cases. Here are the top crypto and Web3 events happening across Europe in 2026.

### **1. Paris Blockchain Week**

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201582%20643'%3E%3C/svg%3E)![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Paris-Blockchain-Week.png)

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201582%20643'%3E%3C/svg%3E)![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Paris-Blockchain-Week.png)
![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Paris-Blockchain-Week.png)

Paris Blockchain Week (PBW) is one of Europe’s premier digital-asset gatherings, blending institutional Web3, policy, finance, and real-world blockchain innovation. The 2026 edition will bring together more than 10,000 attendees, including investors, regulators, enterprise leaders, and developers, in an ambitious two-day event.

Taking place at the iconic Carrousel du Louvre, PBW 2026 features high-level sessions on regulation, tokenization, stablecoins, market infrastructure, and the future of global finance.

The agenda includes curated conversations around MiCA regulation, real-world asset tokenization, custody, and decentralized finance.

**Date:** April 15–16, 2026 

**Country:** France

**Location:** Carrousel du Louvre, Paris 

**Website:** [parisblockchainweek.com](https://www.parisblockchainweek.com/)

**Socials:** [@ParisBlockWeek](https://x.com/ParisBlockWeek) ( X ), [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/company/parisblockchainweek/posts/?feedView=all)

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201900%20685'%3E%3C/svg%3E)![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/crypto-valley-conference.png)

The Crypto Valley Conference is the flagship event hosted by the Crypto Valley Association, bringing together top-tier blockchain visionaries, researchers, and industry builders in Switzerland’s renowned blockchain hub of Zug. 

The 2026 edition promises 3 stages featuring seasoned speakers, master classes, and presentations with over 10,000 expected attendees, all converging to explore Web3’s future through a blend of academic insight and practical vision.

Beyond formal sessions, the event delivers high-impact networking: expect boat cruises on Lake Zug, VIP dinners, and curated apéros that make it easier to connect with investors, researchers, founders, and policy leaders.

**Country:** Switzerland

## **Asia-Pacific**

The Asia-Pacific region remains one of the fastest-growing crypto hubs globally, driven by explosive growth in Web3 gaming, digital payments, remittances, and institutional blockchain adoption. Below are the top crypto and Web3 events for networking in APAC for 2026.

### **1. TOKEN2049 SINGAPORE**

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201756%20505'%3E%3C/svg%3E)![](https://hashlock.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Token2049.png)

[TOKEN2049](https://www.token2049.com/singapore) is widely recognized as one of the biggest crypto events in the world, attracting over 25,000 attendees from 160+ countries, 7,000 companies, 300+ speakers, and more than 500 exhibitors.

The event draws top decision-makers, founders, investors, exchanges, and innovators. Some confirmed speakers include industry leaders like Star Xu (OKX), Richard Teng (Binance), Vlad Tenev (Robinhood), and many more. 

Builders can also join TOKEN2049 [ORIGINS](https://www.token2049.com/singapore/2049-origins) (a high-stakes hackathon with funding opportunities) or TOKEN2049 [NEXUS](https://www.token2049.com/singapore/nexus-startup-competition) (a startup pitch competition offering equity-free prizes and investor access). Spots are limited, so early waitlist registration is essential.

**Date:** 7-8 October 2026   
 **Country:** Singapore  
 **Location:** Marina Bay Sands, Singapore  
 **Website:** [www.token2049.com/singapore](http://www.token2049.com/singapore)

# Crypto, Blockchain and Web3 Conferences: Your 2026 Guide

* Stefan Deeran

[Home](https://vendelux.com/) » [Blog](https://vendelux.com/blog/) » Crypto, Blockchain and Web3 Conferences: Your 2026 Guide

Crypto, blockchain and Web3 are no longer side projects. In 2026, crypto conferences are where builders, investors, and industry leaders compare notes on markets, crypto regulation, and real product traction in digital finance.

The past few years have been turbulent, but that turbulence pushed the blockchain industry closer to real institutional adoption. Digital assets now sit on the same agenda as payments, risk, and core finance strategy. The best web3 events give you a clear view of that shift, with speakers who live this work every day.

This guide walks through why these conferences still matter, the main event types you will see in 2026, how to choose where to attend, and how to use our 2026 conference calendar to support your team.

## Why Crypto, Blockchain and Web3 Conferences Still Matter in 2026

A good conference compresses months of learning into a few days. You hear how real users behave, how regulators think, and which trends are actually sticking.

For teams working with blockchain technology or lighter weight blockchain tech, these gatherings are the fastest way to see where the industry is heading. You can:

* Hear how bitcoin fits into the future of payments, savings, and the wider internet
* Watch ai powered tools for compliance, risk, and customer experience in action
* See where digital assets sit in treasury, wealth, and payments roadmaps

Many 2026 web3 conferences live at the intersection of digital commerce, digital finance, and infrastructure. They are often hosted by a mix of banks, startups, layer one teams, and cloud providers. You will see Google Cloud, Google Pay, and Google’s broader partner ecosystem show up across expo floors, side events, and private roundtables.

On top of that, strong conferences attract independent journalism, researchers, and analysts. Their coverage shapes how the broader world thinks about the space, which gives your team useful insights into reputation and narrative risk.

## Main Types of Crypto, Blockchain and Web3 Events

Not every event looks or feels the same. The 2026 calendar blends huge flagship conferences with very technical, invite only gathering formats.

### Flagship blockchain conferences

These multi day shows define the story for the year. Bitcoin, DeFi, NFTs, and the broader blockchain stack share the same stage. You will often see:

* Dozens of keynote speakers from protocols, exchanges, and traditional finance
* Panels where policymakers, regulators, and lawyers discuss policy and market structure
* Product launches that highlight practical solutions, not only white papers

Flagships are must attend if you want one place to hear from builders, executives, and investors across every layer of the stack.

### Web3 conferences and web3 events

These shows focus on apps and community. You will meet:

* Creators and developers building wallets, social, gaming, and creator tools
* Builders working on loyalty, digital identity, and fan engagement
* Industry leaders testing new strategies that connect brands to on chain activity

Expect hands on workshops, design reviews, and live product demos. Many are hosted in venues that support small group sessions and side events, which makes it easier to form real connections.

### Bitcoin and digital assets forum style events

Here the spotlight is on bitcoin, macro, and long term portfolio design. A digital assets forum will often bring together:

* Macro investors and asset managers
* Treasury and risk teams from corporates
* Service providers focused on custody, tax, and security

Panels cover policy, crypto regulation, and practical portfolio construction. If you work in wealth, treasury, or risk, these conferences are some of the most useful places to explore the benefits and limits of exposure to digital assets.

### Developer and builder focused conferences

These conferences are built around code and architecture. Attendees tend to be developers, protocol engineers, QA, and security researchers. You will see:

* Deep dive technical talks and live coding
* Breakout sessions on privacy, performance, and testing
* Labs where teams can develop integrations with new SDKs and APIs

For people building infrastructure, these are where you meet future partners, co founders, and early power users.

### Policy, regulation and public sector events

In 2026 there are more conferences for policymakers, regulators, central banks, NGOs, and legal teams. They are often hosted with think tanks or universities and focus on:

* Licensing and supervision
* Anti money laundering and market integrity
* Consumer protection and tax

If your product touches consumers, these events help you stay ahead of rule changes that affect how and where you launch.

## Who Should Attend Crypto and Web3 Events in 2026

Different teams get different value from the same conference. Here is how people across the industry use this space.

**Founders and top founders**Founders use conferences to test messaging, meet partners, and understand where capital is flowing. Full days of side meetings often do more than weeks of calls. This is especially true at events hosted near major hubs where your whole network shows up at once.

**Product teams and builders**Product managers and developers use web3 events to see real user journeys, pick up UX ideas, and watch where AI and technology can remove friction. They attend to learn, but also to recruit innovators and builders who care about the same problems.

**Risk, legal and policy teams**These teams care about stability and guardrails. They focus on tracks with policymakers, lawyers, and supervisors, then bring those learnings back to co workers who design products. The benefits show up as fewer surprises and cleaner launches later in the year.

**Enterprise and brand marketers**Marketing teams use crypto conferences to find the right community for activations and sponsorships. They look at attendees, media partners, and side events to see whether a show will support long term partnerships, not only one moment on stage.

**Data, analytics and ai teams**Analysts and data engineers look for conference tracks that cover attribution, risk scoring, and ai powered monitoring. They want clear case studies about how people use on chain data to improve products in real life.

## How 2026 Differs From Years Past

The 2026 conference landscape feels more mature than the wild run up years. The past hype has cooled, and the focus has shifted toward:

* Clear use cases that sit alongside current finance infrastructure
* Measurable outcomes for users, not only token prices
* Safer defaults and better UX

There is broad consensus that blockchain will not replace every system. Instead, it will live next to existing rails and improve specific workflows. The future looks like a mix of legacy databases, blockchain networks, and cloud platforms from vendors like Google and other global partners.

That mix makes 2026 conferences especially helpful for mapping where you fit. You see which segments have real demand, which remain experimental, and which are starting to see serious institutional adoption.

## How To Choose The Right Crypto, Blockchain and Web3 Conferences

Your team cannot be everywhere. A simple filter helps you pick the right events on a crowded calendar.

1. **Start with goals**Decide why you want to attend. Hiring, raising capital, learning about crypto policy, finding integration partners, or growing your community all point you toward different conference formats.
2. **Check speakers and formats**Scan the program for speakers who have shipped things in the real world. Look for a balance of product leaders, policymakers, technologists, and independent journalism voices. When in doubt, search names on google, watch past talks, and see whether they go beyond buzzwords.
3. **Study who the event is hosted by**A conference hosted by a protocol, by a media brand, or by a trade group will each have a different feel. That host list, plus repeat partners, tells you a lot about how the event runs and whether it fits your brand.
4. **Look at attendees and partners**Check which companies, funds, and communities showed up last year. If the attendees and returning partners line up with your ICP, that is a good sign you can build connections that outlive the show.
5. **Match the format to your team**A huge expo is great for sales teams that care about leads and meetings. Smaller web3 conferences with roundtables are better for leaders who need honest conversations about risk and roadmaps. Technical workshops and labs are better for engineers who want to ship.

## How To Use Vendelux To Plan Your 2026 Crypto and Web3 Strategy

Manually tracking every conference site, sponsor list, and speaker update can eat your time. Vendelux helps you bring that work into one place, so you stay focused on strategies and outcomes.

**With Vendelux you can:**

* Search crypto, blockchain, and web3 events worldwide, then filter by region, audience, or venues

* Track which shows led to meetings, deals, or media coverage, so next year’s calendar is based on proof, not guesswork

Instead of guessing which conference is worth the trip, you see it in the data.

## Getting the Most From This 2026 Crypto, Blockchain and Web3 Events Calendar

This guide is the front door to a curated list of 2026 crypto and blockchain conferences across the globe. It focuses on events that:

* Bring together credible speakers, seasoned leaders, and serious investors
* Offer real networking opportunities, side events, and hosted meetings
* Support deep learning through panels, workshops, and technical content

Used well, this calendar becomes more than a set of dates. It is a planning tool that helps your team succeed, build better partnerships, and stay close to the people shaping the future of the blockchain and Web3 space.

# Crypto Conferences in Europe 2026: 10 Events Worth Your Time
