![best crypto conferences 2026 featured image](https://planxconf.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/best-crypto-conferences-2026a-e1760012854957.jpg)

The **2026 crypto events** are going to be larger than ever. Investors, officials, founders, developers and people of the world will also convene to exchange ideas.

The leading events such as **TOKEN2049**, **Consensus**, **EthCC** and **PlanX** Dubai will unite the most prominent voices in blockchain to converse, educate, and network.

This guide lists 15 of the **best crypto conferences in 2026**, including who should attend, what to expect, and why each conference is important. You will also get a calendar of the month-by-month breakdown, and how to better budget and get the best ROI on your trip.

## **How to Use This Guide**

It will work towards ensuring that you get the best out of crypto conferences in 2026. Being proactive will help you to prioritize the activities that are relevant to your objectives and prevent wastage of time and budget.

* Shortlist between 4 and 6 events aligned to your business objectives, business growth phase, and regions you are interested in.
* Select one of them with the help of the How to pick section, preparation checklist, and follow-up plan.
* Travel by stack with the month-by-month to cut down the expenses and make the most of the networking plans.
* Always visit the official conference website to get the latest dates, ticket info, and pitch deadlines.

## **Top Blockchain & Crypto Conferences in 2026**

The **biggest crypto conferences in 2026** will gather top names in blockchain, from DeFi and NFTs to Web3 and regulation.

Each event is a chance to learn new things, meet people, and see where the blockchain world is headed.

### 1. [TOKEN2049 Singapore](https://www.token2049.com) – Marina Bay Sands, Singapore – Oct 1–2, 2026

**TOKEN2049 Singapore** will be the first major crypto-conference in Asia and it will be held at the **Marina Bay Sands**. It will also attract upwards of 25,000 individuals, 7,000+ blockchain firms, and over 300 speakers in 2026.

It is a time where serious deals are made, as **CEOs**, **founders** and **VCs** have face-to-face meetings to explore funding, partnerships, and product launches. Most of the visitors are there with the sole aim of investing or entering APAC markets.

There are such topics as DeFi protocols, centralized exchanges, layer-1 infrastructure, Web3 funding trends, and real-world asset tokenization. **Blockchain Ecosystem Week Singapore** will also include side discussions, private networking, and nighttime parties by leading projects and funds, in addition to the main event.

### 2. [TOKEN2049 Dubai](https://www.dubai.token2049.com) – Madinat Jumeirah, UAE – Apr 29–30, 2026

**TOKEN2049 Dubai** represents a crucial intermediary between East and West and offers a venue, where international projects, funds and institutions converge in the center of the MENA region. The event is meant to be held on **April 29-30, 2026**, in Madinat Jumeirah with the focus being on business development, institutional access, and experiential conference format.

This is especially useful to protocols, market makers, funds, and founders interested in expanding their operations throughout the **Middle East** and **North Africa**. Previous editions have attracted close to **15,000 participants,** and **4,000 businesses,** which has provided them with a quality environment to make connections and form partnerships across regions.

The pro-cryptocurrency attitude of Dubai and the welcoming ecosystem of this city position this conference as one of the most effective events of the year in case of expansion and visibility of both upcoming and mature markets.

### 3. [Consensus Miami](https://consensus.coindesk.com) – Miami Beach Convention Center, USA – May 5–7, 2026

**Consensus Miami** has been ranked as one of the most powerful crypto conferences in the US, with policy, institutional finance, and advanced crypto and blockchain innovation all united on a single platform.

The event will take place **May 5-7, 2026, at the Miami Beach Convention Center** and will address the most important areas of interest, such as DeFi, stablecoins, CBDCs, regulation, and institutional adoption.

Having **over 15,000 attendees**, a solid turnout of U.S. policymakers and substantial media coverage, Consensus creates a one-of-a-kind blend of high-level deal-making and regulatory impact that has made it one of the pillars of the global crypto calendar.

### 4. [Paris Blockchain Week](https://www.parisblockchainweek.com) – Carrousel du Louvre, Paris – Apr 14–16, 2026

**Paris Blockchain Week** is now the leading cryptocurrency event in Europe, which will take place at the Carrousel du Louvre April 14-16, 2026. The blockchain conference is dedicated to DeFi, tokenized finance, **AI x Web3 integration**, and MiCA regulation and is necessary to all teams working in the quickly-changing digital asset landscape of the EU.

It is most appropriate in startup, institutional, and businesses interested in harmonizing with the rules of licensing and new regulatory tracks. Over **9,500 participants and 420+ speakers** are anticipated to attend the event, which will be a mixture of deep regulatory knowledge, and strong builder and enterprise-driven tracks.

### 5. [Ethereum Community Conference (EthCC)](https://ethcc.io/) – Palais des Festivals, Cannes – Mar 30–Apr 2, 2026

**EthCC** is an event in Europe that is dedicated to **Ethereum**, and will take place in **March 30 – April 2 2026** in the Palais des Festivals in Cannes.

It is the most technical and high-signal Ethereum conference in the world, being focused on the Ethereum roadmap, developer tooling, layer-2 scaling, client development, and current research.

Its target audience consists mostly of builders, researchers, infrastructure founders, and developers in need of first-hand exposure to the latest innovations in the ecosystem. Cannes is also established as the host through the **end of 2028** demonstrating the vision of the event in the long run.

By the year 2025, the conference had 6,500 accredited members and over 400 speakers, and this number is likely to increase to even more in 2026.

### 6. [DC Blockchain Summit](https://www.dcblockchainsummit.com) – Washington, DC, USA – Mar 17–18, 2026

**The DC Blockchain Summit** is the biggest blockchain/cryptocurrency conference in the United States, planned to run, **March 17-18, 2026,** in Washington, DC.

It is entirely regulatory-focused, concerned with U.S. competitiveness, but also the point of intersection for crypto-financial policy, and essential to stakeholders in the country’s digital asset framework.

It draws a significant number of **C-suite executives**, compliance teams, legal blockchain professionals, exchanges, stablecoin issuers, and fintech executives interested in reaching out to policy makers.

Participants are likely to have access to legislators, industry regulators and influential staff on the Hill, which presents an exceptional opportunity to influence the narratives that determine the industry.

### 7. [Bitcoin Conference](https://b.tc/conference) – The Venetian, Las Vegas, USA – Apr 27–29, 2026

The **Bitcoin Conference** is the flagship global gathering dedicated exclusively to Bitcoin, taking place at The Venetian in Las Vegas on April 27–29, 2026. Unlike broader crypto shows, its agenda is entirely **Bitcoin-focused**, covering mining, the Lightning Network, custody solutions, ETFs, and macro-regulatory issues that impact adoption.

Late April 2026 includes a dense cluster of major events. PlanX (Dubai), the Bitcoin Conference (Las Vegas), and TOKEN2049 (Dubai) are all scheduled within the **same week (April 27–29)**. If you’re planning to attend, consider travel routes and time zone shifts, and book early.

The audience brings together **developers, miners, institutional allocators, and ecosystem founders**, making it the largest single-platform for the Bitcoin community. The 2025 edition [drew more than 10,000 attendees](https://b.tc/conference), per published estimates.

It also serves as a platform for high-profile announcements and keynotes, often shaping the Bitcoin narrative for the year ahead. For anyone serious about the future of blockchain, this conference stands as the must-attend event on the calendar.

### 8. [BTC Prague](https://btcprague.com/) – PVA EXPO Prague, Czech Republic – Jun 11–13, 2026

**BTC Prague** has quickly established itself as one of Europe’s largest Bitcoin-only gatherings, returning to the PVA EXPO in Prague on **June 11–13, 2026**. The conference focuses on the most important aspects of Bitcoin’s future, including scaling solutions, layer-2 development, mining, energy use, and financial sovereignty.

Its audience is made up of **Bitcoin developers, node operators, miners, and strong advocates of self-custody**, making it highly technical and crypto community-driven. Unlike general crypto and blockchain events, BTC Prague keeps a narrow focus on Bitcoin, creating blockchain space for in-depth discussions and collaboration.

It is recognized for combining technical workshops with thought leadership, attracting both industry veterans and new innovators.

### 9. [CfC St. Moritz](https://cfc-stmoritz.com) – Suvretta House, Switzerland – Jan 14–16, 2026

**CfC St. Moritz** is among the most exclusive digital asset conferences worldwide, taking place at the Suvretta House in Switzerland on **January 14–16, 2026**. It is designed for an audience of ultra-high-net-worth investors, family offices, institutional funds, and later-stage founders seeking strategic partnerships and capital.

The program focuses on macro trends, the blockchain and digital asset market structure, and policy developments in the crypto space, with carefully selected panels and fireside chats. Attendance is capped at around **250 participants**, making the networking highly curated and personal.

Confirmed speakers for 2026 include representatives from **Franklin Templeton**, DRW, and Binance leadership, reinforcing its reputation as a closed-door setting where high-value deals and strategic conversations take place.

### 10. [WAGMI Miami](https://wagmi.miami) – Downtown Miami, USA – Jan 20–23, 2026

The flagship event of **Miami Blockchain Week** is WAGMI Miami, which will be back in downtown **Miami January 20-23, 2026**. The cryptocurrency conference is known to combine DeFi, cultural innovation, and education and establish itself as both an industry event and a community party.

It draws a wide range of builders, investors and innovators who wish to be educated by the most influential faces throughout the day and connect with the **nightlife of Miami in the evening.** It has now become a traditional part of the crypto scene in the city, putting a professional hub within reach in terms of accessible cultural exchange.

### 11. [NFT Paris](https://www.nftparis.xyz) – Grand Palais Éphémère, Paris, France – Feb 05–06, 2026

**NFT Paris** has grown into Europe’s largest NFT-focused gathering, returning to the Grande halle de la Villette on **February 05–06, 2026**. The event explores the intersection of NFTs, tokenized assets, Ordinals, crypto gaming, and the AI-powered creator economy, drawing a wide range of stakeholders across the potential of innovative blockchain technology.

It is ideal for consumer brands, gaming studios, luxury houses, and IP-driven Web3 and blockchain and cryptocurrency teams who want to connect with both developers and creative communities. With more than **20,000 visitors and 400+ speakers expected**, the scale makes it a central meeting point for the global NFT ecosystem.

Beyond panels and Blockchain use cases, the conference emphasizes partnerships between traditional industries and blockchain builders, offering rare opportunities to explore how digital ownership and tokenization are being integrated into mainstream markets.

### 12. [Consensus Hong Kong](https://consensus-hongkong.coindesk.com) – Hong Kong – Feb 10-12, 2026

**Consensus Hong Kong** is the newest expansion of the CoinDesk brand, building on the success of its U.S. counterpart. The blockchain event serves as a **gateway to Asia**, with a focus on institutional adoption, cross-border deal-making, and policy development**s** across the region.

It is especially relevant for global teams seeking liquidity, strategic partnerships, and regulatory clarity in Hong Kong and the wider APAC markets.

The debut edition sold out, underscoring the strong demand for a high-quality forum that connects East and West under an established brand. The **2026 conference** is expected to continue this momentum, bringing together international investors, builders, and policymakers.

### 13. [European Blockchain Convention](https://eblockchainconvention.com/) – Barcelona, Spain – Oct 16–17, 2026

The **European Blockchain Convention (EBC)** has become one of the most important institutional-facing events in Europe, taking place in Barcelona on **October 16–17, 2026**. The conference highlights the latest in tokenization, stablecoins, AI agents, and institutional ETF adoption, paired with a structured meetings program that makes it highly results-driven.

It is ideal for **EU-focused founders, fintech partners, and institutional allocators** who want to access capital and partnerships in the region. The event is known for its efficiency, with more than 6,000 delegates and 10,000+ pre-scheduled one-on-one meetings expected.

Top speakers from finance, policy, and technology ensure strong content alongside deal flow. For founders and investors targeting European markets, EBC delivers a unique blend of conference learning and curated networking at scale.

### 14. [Blockchain Forum Moscow](https://blockchain.forum/en/) – Crocus Expo, Moscow – Apr 14–15, 2026

The **Blockchain Forum Moscow** is the largest blockchain and Web3-focused event in the CIS region, scheduled for April 14–15, 2026 at the **Crocus Expo**. The forum covers a wide scope of topics including Web3 development, crypto trading, and large-scale mining, reflecting the region’s unique position as a hub for both users and infrastructure.

It is well suited for **global teams exploring CIS market entry, mining partnerships**, and new user acquisition. With 20,000+ attendees expected, it serves as the flagship crypto gathering in Russia and surrounding markets.

Beyond panels and exhibitions, the event emphasizes deal-making and regional insights, giving international companies exposure to one of the most active but often underrepresented audiences in global crypto.

### 15. [PlanX Conference](https://planxconf.com) – Dubai, UAE – Apr 27–29, 2026

PlanX is built around **sovereignty and real-world structuring**, with private access to migration advisors, legal architects, and capital setup specialists.

It’s not just about theory – attendees book **pre-arranged legal clinics**, join **sovereignty working groups**, and enter **peer-reviewed strategy circles**.

But what sets it apart is the follow-through: curated offsites, one-on-one mandate reviews, and private deal rooms where entity structures, asset protection frameworks, and residency paths are actively finalized.

The promise is simple: you arrive with questions, and you leave mid-process – with your advisors, paperwork, and next steps already in motion.

## How to Pick the Best Crypto Conferences for You (and Get ROI)

Picking the right conference depends on clear goals, your stage of growth, and how much you want to invest. The framework below makes it easier to decide where to go and how to get the most out of it.

### Set Your Primary Objective

Your ROI starts with knowing what you want to achieve. Each event offers different strengths, so define your main purpose before booking.

**Deal flow:** Customers, partners, exchanges, market makers, investors

**Learning:** Technical deep dives at EthCC and BTC Prague, or compliance focus at DC Summit and Paris Week

**Visibility:** PR opportunities, speaking slots, hackathons, pitch programs

**Expansion:** Regional reach in APAC (Singapore/Hong Kong), MENA (Dubai), EU (Paris/Barcelona), U.S. (Miami/Las Vegas)

### Stage and Niche Fit

Not all conferences are built for the same audience. Matching your stage and niche helps avoid wasted spend and ensures you’re in the right rooms.

* Builders and devs: EthCC, BTC Prague
* Institutional allocators: CfC St. Moritz, EBC, Consensus
* Policy/compliance teams: DC Summit, Paris Week (MiCA)
* Consumer and NFT projects: NFT Paris, WAGMI Miami
* Global BD at scale: TOKEN2049, Consensus Miami, PlanX

### Geography, Visas, and Banking Access

Travel logistics and access to financial hubs can be just as important as the conference itself. Planning ahead ensures smooth entry and maximized value.

* Choose upcoming events in hubs with strong banking and entity options (Dubai, Singapore, EU)
* Check visa requirements early; embassy slots can take months
* Align trips with bank, legal, and accelerator meetings adjacent to the conference

### Budget and Simple ROI Model

A clear budget keeps expectations realistic. Thinking in ROI terms helps you measure if the event is truly worth it.

* Costs: tickets, flights, hotels, booths/sponsorship, swag, team hours
* ROI formula: (Weighted pipeline value from event) – (Total cost)
* Tactics: use early-bird codes, share booths, book off-peak lodging, leverage airline passes, rely on meeting tools

### Prep, Presence, and Follow-Up

The real value of conferences comes from preparation and post-event discipline. Success depends on strong planning before you arrive and fast action after.

* Pre-book 70–80% of meetings via event apps and intro lists
* Carry one-pagers, product demos, compliance docs (KYC/AML)
* After the event: send 48-hour follow-ups, convert warm intros, set a 30-day nurture cadence

### Compliance and Cross-Border Strategy

For founders scaling internationally, compliance and structuring can’t be an afterthought. Some conferences build support for this directly into their program.

* PlanX Dubai offers legal clinics on tax, banking, residency, and second citizenship
* Bring a data room: cap table, entity docs, AML/KYC, residency plans
* Book advisor roundtables early via [PlanX](https://planxconf.com)

## 2026 Themes Shaping Crypto Events

Crypto conferences in 2026 are defined by a few clear themes that show where the industry is heading.

* Institutional adoption: Banks and asset managers take center stage in panels and deal rooms.
* Regulatory clarity: EU’s MiCA rollout and evolving U.S. rules dominate policy tracks.
* Tokenization/RWAs: Crypto world assets emerge as a leading topic across global agendas.
* AI x Web3: Conferences spotlight agentic AI systems, data provenance, and creator tools.
* Geographic diversification: APAC, MENA, EU, and U.S. each host packed calendars with unique focuses.

## Conclusion

The **crypto conference calendar for 2026** shows how global and diverse the industry has become, with major gatherings in APAC, MENA, Europe, and the U.S. Each event offers a different mix of deal flow, learning, and visibility, making it possible to choose the ones that match your goals and stage.

## DIRECTORY

![](https://deeptechtimes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Screenshot-2025-12-02-at-7.50.55-PM.png)

### [GFTN FORUM JAPAN 2026 February 24-27, 2026 Tokyo](https://gftnforum.jp)

GFTN Forum Japan is a flagship collaboration with the Japan Financial Services Agency, Japan Fintech Week, Fincity.Tokyo, JPX, METI, Digital Agency, and the Monetary Authority of Singapore. Now in its third edition, it is the platform for policymakers, financial leaders, investors and founders to drive Fintech innovation in Japan, across Asia and beyond. This year’s focus: connecting financial corridors fueling technology, innovation, entrepreneurship, and capital flows, with deep dives into agentic AI, quantum computing and digital assets in the context of global economic and geopolitical shifts. The programme is designed to unlock business, investment and partnership opportunities across the public and private sectors.

![](https://deeptechtimes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Screenshot-2025-12-02-at-7.35.10-PM.png)

### [BLACK SWAN SUMMIT AUSTRALIA March 23-25, 2026 Perth](https://australia.blackswansummit.com/?__hstc=197349872.999df3bdac840095c282a6d83db4a386.1764675279160.1764675279160.1764675279160.1&__hssc=197349872.1.1764675279161&__hsfp=1578638873)

The Black Swan Summit Australia is an annual R&D foresight forum that focuses on the impact of disruptive technologies, such as AI, quantum computing, blockchain (Web3) and climate tech. The event is held in Perth, Western Australia, and aims to bridge the gap between research, industry, and policy to navigate systemic risks and build resilient economies. The summit explores transformative technologies, global green and sustainable financial technology R&D, and their effects on industries and society. It convenes a variety of thought leaders, including academics, technologists, policymakers, investors and industry experts. The format includes panels, roundtables and workshops. Hosted by the Global Finance & Technology Network (GFTN), the event takes place primarily at the University of Western Australia (UWA).

The XCION 13th Bali CXO Summit will feature a panel titled “From human vulnerability to human firewall: The future of cyber resilience”, which focuses on how organisations can transform human behaviour from a cybersecurity risk into a strategic defence layer. The panel explores behavioural science, continuous learning and automation as tools for building culture-driven cyber resilience at the CXO and board level. Sponsored by SiberMate, the discussion brings together CXOs, cybersecurity and people leaders to discuss how human risk can be measured, managed and scaled as part of enterprise security. The session sits within XCION’s broader mission to convene senior leaders around leadership, innovation and emerging risks in the digital era.

# 6th International Conference on Advances in Mechanical Engineering (ICAME-2026)

## ICAME-2026

The **6th International Conference on Advances in Mechanical Engineering** (ICAME 2026) will be held in person from March 18 to 20, 2026. The primary objectives of the conference are to promote research and developmental activities in Mechanical Engineering and to facilitate the exchange of scientific knowledge among academicians, researchers, developers, engineers, students, and practitioners from across the world. ICAME 2026 will serve as an international platform where experts and enthusiasts can present their work, share innovative ideas, and discuss emerging challenges in the field. By bringing together diverse perspectives, the conference aims to inspire collaborations, strengthen academia–industry linkages, and foster advancements that contribute to both technological progress and societal development.

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20564%20412'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

Organised by

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20600%20203'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

Department of Mechanical Engineering  
SRM Institute of Science and Technology

In association with

![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20366%20100'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

University of Split  
Faculty of Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering and Naval Architecture  
Ruđera Boškovića 32, 21000 Split, Croatia

ICAME 2026 places a strong emphasis on advancing research and innovation aligned with the UN Sustainable Development Goals, particularly **SDG 7 (Affordable and Clean Energy), SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation, and Infrastructure)**, **SDG 12 (Responsible Consumption and Production) and SDG 13 (Climate Action)**. The conference aims to promote advanced discussions and collaborations in sustainable manufacturing, renewable energy, circular economy, and climate-resilient technologies. ICAME 2026 seeks to inspire solutions that advance technology while ensuring environmental responsibility and achieving a lasting societal impact.

ICAME 2026 invites contributions in (but not limited to) the following broad areas:

## 24th ISME Conference on Mechanical Engineering Innovations for Vikasit Bharat

## December 28 - 30, 2026 IIT Guwahati

![Logo](images/iitg_logo_writeup.jpg)

## **About Us**

Mechanical engineering is a foundational discipline that blends physics, materials science, and engineering principles to design, analyze, and innovate systems that power modern industry, from energy and transportation to manufacturing and robotics. Over the past decade, mechanical engineering has evolved from a predominantly hardware-centric discipline into a highly interdisciplinary field integrating data-driven modeling, advanced materials, and intelligent systems. Advances in computational methods, additive manufacturing, automation, and sustainable technologies have expanded its role in addressing complex challenges in energy, mobility, healthcare, and national development. In this context, the ISME Conference on Mechanical Engineering provides a vital platform for research scholars to showcase their emerging ideas, novel methodologies, and innovative solutions to the mechanical engineering community.

The 24th edition of the ISME Conference on Mechanical Engineering (ISME 2026) will be organized at the [Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati](https://iitg.ac.in/) during December 28 - 30, 2026. The conference is organized by the Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Guwahati. The conference provides a unique platform to bring together researchers and industry professionals working in the field of Mechanical Engineering and to share expertise through contributed paper presentations, interactive poster sessions, keynotes, and invited talks.

The theme of ISME 2026, "Innovations for Vikasit Bharat," reflects a collective commitment to propelling India towards becoming a developed nation by 2047. It underscores the pivotal role of mechanical engineering innovations—ranging from sustainable manufacturing to advanced energy solutions—in building a self-reliant and technologically advanced India. This conference aims to bring together the brightest minds to strategize how engineering breakthroughs can accelerate national growth, ensuring a prosperous and resilient future for the country. The overall theme of the conference, to be held at IIT Guwahati, is classified into the following technical tracks:

**Track – 01:** Machine Design

**Track – 02:** Materials, Manufacturing and Industrial Engineering

**Track – 03:** Thermal, Fluid and Energy Engineering

**Track – 04:** AI/ML in Mechanical Engineering

## **About ISME**

[ISME](https://www.ismeindia.org/) was setup on 26 February 1976 with registration No. 7980 under the government of NCT of Delhi, India. The society aims at providing a forum for exchanging ideas, technological developments, and inspiring mechanical engineers to pursue new directions in the broad field of Mechanical Engineering. For achieving the aims since the inception of the ISME, the society has been holding annual ISME conferences on Mechanical Engineering which have been quite popular amongst the research fraternity of academia and industries. The ISME has been one of the most active technical organizations of the country, which has been publishing three journals, holding workshops and various courses at regular interval of time. ISME is now actively engaged in creating student chapters in different engineering institutes and universities.

Previous editions of ISME: [23rd ISME (2025)](https://lnmiit.ac.in/isme-2025/), [21st ISME (2023)](https://events.pes.edu/10674/), [20th ISME (2022)](https://sites.google.com/iitrpr.ac.in/isme20/about)

## **Extended Abstract/Full-length Paper Submission**

Author(s) can submit extended abstract(s)/full-length paper(s) in one of the following submission options in ISME 2026.

* **Option 1: Full-length Paper Submission**: Author(s) can submit full-length paper(s) of 6 to 8 pages.
* **Option 2: Extended Abstract Submission**: Author(s) can submit extended abstract(s) of 2 pages (maximum). On acceptance of extended abstract(s), author(s) will be invited to submit full-length paper(s) of 6 to 8 pages. However, the submission of full-length paper(s) is optional.

## **Registration**

* At least one of the authors of accepted extended abstract/paper must register for the conference in order to present it.
* If more than one author of a paper presentation/publication wishes to attend the conference, they need to register separately.

* Registration fee is non-refundable and non-transferable.
* Please keep a receipt of the transaction details.
* Please note that ISME 2026 will not be responsible for any loss, financial or otherwise, caused by improper transactions conducted online. While the organizers have taken all reasonable precautions to ensure a safe online transaction, it is the participant’s responsibility, while paying the registration fee, to secure their user IDs, passwords, Paper ID, etc.

## About IIT Guwahati

The campus of [IIT Guwahati](https://www.iitg.ac.in) is on the northern banks of Brahmaputra and abuts the North Guwahati town of Amingaon. The campus is on a 700 acres (2.8 km sq.) plot of land around 20 km from the heart of the city. It has the Brahmaputra on one side and hills and vast open spaces on others. IITG fraternity welcome you to one of the most beautiful and green campus with a Campus Tour Video prepared by one of our student clubs: Anchorenza & RadioG club.

For more information on the campus, visit [IITG Campus Maps](https://www.iitg.ac.in/campusmap/)

## How to reach IIT Guwahati

**From Airport**: IIT Guwahati is around 30 min drive from the airport. Getting to the IIT Guwahati campus from the airport will cost about Rs. 550-700 in app-based (Ola, Uber, ASTC City Cabs) reserved taxi, which is available at the airport. Additionally, prepaid taxi services are also available right outside the arrivals gate.

**From Railway Station**: For commuting from the railway station to the IIT Guwahati campus, taxi and auto-rickshaws are available. IIT Guwahati has also its own transportation facilities to ply to and fro from the campus to the city. The bus starting point is opposite to Hotel President, Panbazar, which is at 15 minutes walking distance from the railway station. The updated time-table will be posted week prior to the conference dates.

## Weather in December

The city of Guwahati enjoys a subtropical humid climate. The average high temperature is 29° C and average minimum is 19° C. During the month of December, the mercury reading of city of Guwahati remains around 10° C to 23° C. Winters have scanty rainfall and the mornings and afternoons are usually misty and foggy.

Please check the following [link](https://www.accuweather.com/en/in/guwahati/186893/weather-forecast/186893) for the detailed weather information and forecast.

## **Exploring North East**

The eight states of [North East India](https://necouncil.gov.in/nec-project-sector/tourism) are blessed with scenic natural beauty, comfortable weather, rich biodiversity, rare wild life, historical sites, distinct cultural and ethnic heritage and most importantly, warm and welcoming people. The region offers unforgettable visits for tourists interested in wild life, religious, cultural and ethnic tourism, river cruises, golf and a host of others. There is also great scope for mountaineering, trekking and adventure tourism in the region.

Northeast India is home to many wildlife sanctuaries such as Kaziranga (which is famous for the one horned rhinoceros), Manas, Nameri, Orang, Dibru Saikhowa National Parks in Assam, Namdhapha National Park in Arunachal Pradesh, Balpakram National Park in Meghalaya, Keibul Namjao National Park in Manipur, Intanki National Park in Nagaland, Khangchendzonga National Park in Sikkim. Brahmaputra river flows through the length of Assam where tourists can enjoy memorable river cruises and the flowing rivers of Arunachal Pradesh which feed the Brahmaputra offering incredible white water rafting experiences.

## Places to visit in Guwahati

**Kamakhya Temple** atop the Neelachal hill, an ancient temple associated with many mythological stories. Initially, it was a place of worship for the Kiratas: early settlers of Assam, belonging to Sino-Tibetan origins. The earliest temple was established around 8th to 9th century A.D., which was brought to ruins in the 15th century. Construction of the existing structure was started in the mid 16th century.

**Pobitora Wildlife Sanctuary**: A wildlife sanctuary on the southern bank of the Brahmaputra in Morigaon district in Assam and 35 kms from Guwahati.

**Deepor Beel**: Protected wetlands harbouring rich biodiversity within the city's premises.

## Nearby Places

**Kaziranga National Park**: Kaziranga National Park is a world heritage site where the major attraction includes close look at rhino, swamp deer, thousands of local and migratory birds flocking in wetlands and the scenic beauty of Karbi Anglong Hills. Kaziranga is about 200 km east to Guwahati. Road trip to Kaziranga in itself is a wonderful ride flanked by rice fields and betel nut trees on either side of the road. The national park is not open to tourists for about five months from April to October as a precaution due to warning of floods. December is perhaps the best time to visit the Kaziranga national park as the climate is mild and dry. The chances of spotting rhinos are more in winter as the grass burn off and the background becomes clearer. You can visit the official website of [Kaziranga National Park](https://kaziranga.nptr.in/) for details.

**Manas National Park**: A tiger reserve and a world heritage site, the park harbours more than 20 endangered species and is famous for its unique scenic beauty. About 130 km north to Guwahati, the Manas National Park gives you the opportunity to take a peak at Bhutan which is across the Manas river flowing through the national park. Manas is famous for birding activities, elephant rides and river rafting in the pristine Manas river. There are several hotels, bed and breakfasts and even resorts right at the gates of Manas National Park. You can visit the official website of [Manas National Park](https://www.manasnationalparkonline.in/) for details.

**Majuli**: Assam is famous for Majuli, the world's largest river island. Majuli situated in the midst of river Brahmaputra, is the centre of Vishnava culture. There are over fifteen Vaishnava monasteries or satras on Majuli. The major satras are Kamalabari, Natun Kamalabari, Auniati, Garmur, Samoguri, Dakhinpat and Bengenaati. These satras are regarded as the main centres for Assamese art, music, dance, drama, handicrafts, literature and religion etc. Auniati is famous for its considerable collection of Assamese old utensils, jewellery and handicrafts. Upper Majuli is inhabited by tribes like the Mising and the Deoris and is the centre of a living heritage of colourful costumes and festivals. Plenty of migratory birds of great varieties are also seen. For more information, visit this [website](http://majulilandscape.gov.in/tourism.php).

**Shillong**: Shillong, the capital city of Meghalaya state, is also called the Scotland of East because of its natural beauty, has been one of the important tourist destinations in the north-east. The amazing series of waterfalls, the mystic mountain peaks, crystal clear water lakes, breathtakingly beautiful golf courses, museums and zoo - Shillong has a different facade for everyone. Towering pines and gurgling mountain streams, awesome waterfalls that go by the name of Elephant, Crinoline, Sweet Falls, and the twin Bishop and Beadon are the major attraction of Shillong. The state website has all the relevant details for various places in and around [Shillong](https://www.meghalayatourism.in/destinations/shillong/).

**Sualkuchi**: Assam produces three unique varieties of silks: the Golden Muga, the White Pat and the warm Eri Silks, that are grown all over the state and find their way to Sualkuchi, 32 kms from Guwahati. Sualkuchi is one of the world's largest weaving villages often called the Manchester of the East. The entire population here is engaged in weaving exquisite silk fabrics. A renowned centre of silk production, particularly known for Muga - the golden silk of Assam which is not produced anywhere else in the world.

Some of the main tourists circuits identified based of difference themes and starting from Guwahati are as follows:

* Guwahati – Kaziranga – Nameri – Dirang – Tawang – Bomdila – Tezpur – Guwahati (Tawang Arunachal circuit 8 nights/9 days)
* Guwahati – Cherrapunji – Shillong – Mawlynnong – Guwahati (Meghalaya circuit 5 nights/6 days)
* Guwahati – Hajo – Sualkuchi – Borduwa – Sivasagar – Majuli – Jorhat (Assam circuit 5 nights/6 days)
* Guwahati – Kaziranga – Kohima – Imphal – Moreh - Guwahati (Manipur circuit 5 nights/6 days)

You can visit the following links for more details on the north-eash tourism

* [Assam Tourism Official Website](https://assamtourism.gov.in/)
* [Meghalaya Tourism Official Website](https://www.meghalayatourism.in/)
* [Arunachal Pradesh Tourism Official Website](https://arunachaltourism.com//)

# Mechanical Conferences in India 2026

India is rapidly emerging as a premier global destination for
**Mechanical** innovation and collaboration. In 2026, the country will host a wide range
of conferences bringing together international researchers, industry pioneers, and academic professionals
to explore the latest breakthroughs and real-world applications in Mechanical.

## 2026 Mechanical Conferences in India – Overview

More than **11429** upcoming
**Mechanical** conferences are scheduled across India.
Based on current scheduling trends, **Pune, Bangalore, Chennai, New Delhi, Mumbai** has emerged as the
leading professional hub, hosting approximately
**14.72%** of all specialized events.
For professionals aiming to maximize networking opportunities and knowledge exchange,
**May** stands out as the most active month for the
Mechanical sector in India.

## Why Attend a Mechanical Conference in India?

Attending these conferences offers valuable opportunities for high-level networking,
academic collaboration, and professional growth. Whether you are a researcher, industry
professional, academic, or student, these 2026 Mechanical conferences provide
a platform to share ideas, discover emerging technologies, and build meaningful
professional connections.
Beyond the technical sessions and expert talks, participants can also experience the
dynamic academic and cultural environment that makes India a leading
destination for international conferences and professional development.

Attending a [Mechanical Conference in India](Https://www.allconferencealert.com/india/mechanical) offers valuable opportunities for networking, paper presentations, workshops, panel discussions, and keynote sessions led by renowned speakers. Participants will gain insights into emerging developments, best practices, and future directions in **Mechanical**, while also experiencing the academic, cultural, and professional environment of **India**. Whether you are a student, researcher, professor, or industry professional, these **[conferences in India](Https://www.allconferencealert.com/india)** during 2026 support learning, career growth, and international exposure in the field of Mechanical.

### Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

#### Which city in India hosts the most Mechanical conferences?

Based on our 2026 data, **Pune** is the leading hub for
**Mechanical** events in **India**, followed by
Pune, Bangalore, Chennai, New Delhi, Mumbai. This city offers the best infrastructure and networking opportunities
for professionals in this field.

#### What is the best time of year to attend a Mechanical conference in India?

The peak season for **Mechanical** conferences in
**India** is **May**, when the highest
concentration of international summits occurs.

#### How can I get an invitation letter for a Mechanical conference in India?

Most organizers listed on AllConferenceAlert provide official invitation letters for visa purposes
after successful registration. Contact the event coordinator via the “View Event” link.

#### How can I submit a research paper for a Mechanical conference in India?

Most **Mechanical** events in **India** are currently
accepting submissions. Select an event and check the “Call for Papers” section.

#### What are the top venues for Mechanical conferences in India?

In **India**, most **Mechanical** gatherings are held
in premium convention centers or 5-star hotels, especially in
**Pune**.

#### Are these Mechanical conferences in India open to international participants?

Yes, the **11429** conferences listed for 2026 are international
platforms. If traveling to **Pune**, apply for your
conference visa at least 4–6 weeks before the
**May** peak season.

# Guide to Engineering Conferences in 2026

By  [Farheen Gani](#)   Reviewed By [Jocelyn Blore](#)

**Updated December 22, 2025** [Editorial Values](https://www.onlineengineeringprograms.com/editorial-values)

Engineering is an exceptionally dynamic sector. It is continually changing and expanding. Experts, professionals, and academics in the field must keep pace with the latest developments. Conferences provide professionals in various engineering disciplines with knowledge about cutting-edge tools, technology, and skills in the field. They are also an excellent opportunity for professionals to connect with peers worldwide.

Due to the extensive nature of engineering, conference topics are varied. Some broadly encompass engineering as a whole, while others focus more specifically on a specific subsector, such as hardware, software, nuclear energy, big data, climate change, communication, automotive technology, and sustainable technology. Regardless of the topic, most larger events pick a theme that changes annually or breaks out into several tracks.

There are also many activities at conferences. Depending on the type of event, attendees can partake in workshops, listen in on seminars, network with peers, visit expo halls to meet vendors, and even enroll in competitions. Those interested in playing a larger part can sponsor an event, buy a booth to display their product or service, or even submit a paper or apply for a speaking position.

As a vendor, conferences are an excellent opportunity to speak to customers, get product feedback, meet potential business partners or hires, and learn valuable information about the field. It is one of the best ways to gain insider knowledge about the sector while forging long-lasting connections that may otherwise be difficult to come by.

The following guide lists 18 top engineering conferences taking place around the world in 2026.

## International Conference on Civil and Environmental Engineering

The [International Conference on Civil and Environmental Engineering](https://researchworld.org/Conference/25024/I2C2E/) (I2C2E) is a platform for researchers, industrial participants, budding students, academicians, and engineers to exchange their research findings with global experts. This year, the conference invites researchers and academicians to submit their papers on structural engineering, construction engineering, environmental engineering, hydrology, geophysics, and civil engineering, among others.

The conference allows attendees to share their experiences and ideas with peers from various parts of the world to help delegates foster business relations for collaborations in the future.

* **Location:** Las Vegas, Nevada
* **Date:** January 17-18, 2026
* **Paper submission deadline:** December 26, 2025
* **Audience:** Academicians, engineers, budding students, industrial participants, and researchers from around the world
* **Cost:** Listeners ($250); authors – bachelors ($360); Authors – PhD. /post doc./masters ($380); authors – academician/practitioner/industrialists ($400); additional page ($30); extra proceeding ($50); additional paper ($250)

## $3,500 Name Badge Insert

Have your company logo on the bottom of every attendee's name badge. Excellent visibility opportunity on everyone's conference badge. *(Limit one sponsor)*

## $4,500 Women in Engineering Panel and Reception

Join ASME in fostering a welcoming and supportive environment for all engineers by sponsoring the Women in Engineering Panel and Reception. This engaging and inspiring program brings together leaders, innovators, and emerging voices in engineering to share experiences, spark conversations, and build a strong community in the field.

Sponsors will receive recognition during the panel and reception and can have a small table for literature and a pop-up banner.

## $6,000 Conference Lanyards

Gain maximum visibility with colorful badge lanyards displaying your company name and/or logo. Your neck cords are available during registration. Company to supply the lanyards. SOLD

## $7,000 Recharge Lounge

Company logo will be displayed in the lounge where attendees can charge their devices, play games, and relax. A table provided for company literature and a monitor available for looping content shown during exhibit hall open hours.

## $8,000 Rising Stars

Honoring early-career researchers who have received recognition through NSF CAREER, ONR YIP, AFOSR YIP, DARPA YFA, and NIH New Innovator Awards, the sponsor will have the opportunity to have a 3-minute presentation during the Rising Stars Event. Be in front of the Rising Stars of Mechanical Engineering.

## CUSTOM SPONSORSHIPS

Contact [ASME](mailto:exhibits@asme.org) to discuss the sponsorship that works best for you. ASME will work with you to customize a sponsorship that allows you the best visibility and return on your investment.

**Reserve today: [exhibits@asme.org](mailto:exhibits@asme.org)**

# Sponsorship and exhibition opportunities

The Institution of Mechanical Engineers (IMechE) offers you the opportunity to engage with our members across all industry sectors.

The IMechE will organise 24 technical conferences in 2026; these are content-led, technical events that are designed to update and inform engineers and specialists operating across multiple industry sectors, including Aerospace, Automotive, Biomedical, Construction, Manufacturing, Power, Process, and Railway.

We invite you to partner your organisation with the IMechE's conferences and become a part of our growing and highly engaged engineering communities in 2026. For further details of how your organisation and brand can get involved, simply [complete our interest form](https://r1.dotdigital-pages.com/p/7L7Z-79P/sponsorship-and-exhibition-form) and a colleague in our team will be in contact to discuss your requirements.

## Opportunities include:

We are happy to discuss any other elements in order to enhance your engagement and meet your specific objectives.

# Connect with Your Aerospace Engineering Community

## ASME's 4th Annual Aerospace Structures, Structural Dynamics, and Materials Conference (SSDM) gathers aerospace structures, structural dynamics, and materials engineers and researchers from industry, academia, and government agencies to discuss technical advancements in this growing sector. The program is designed to not only showcase the latest research but also encourage meaningful dialogue in a dynamic setting.

ASME's 4th Annual Aerospace Structures, Structural Dynamics, and Materials Conference (SSDM) gathers aerospace structures, structural dynamics, and materials engineers and researchers from industry, academia, and government agencies to discuss technical advancements in this growing sector. The program is designed to not only showcase the latest research but also encourage meaningful dialogue in a dynamic setting.

## Attend SSDM to:

* ***Meet your next R&D partner:*** Connect with government agencies.
* ***Advance your products:*** Choose among hundreds of technical presentations to discover new ways to build, test, and develop aerospace technologies
* ***Facilitate product advancement:*** Participate in sessions focused on applied technological design and development
* ***Expand your knowledge in specialized topic areas:*** Discover new avenues in aerospace structures, structural dynamics, or material
* ***Promote your company:*** Connect with aerospace industry decision makers in person to stay ahead of your competition

## SSDM Program Tracks include:

**Structures** - The Structures Track contains a wide range of topics in the broad area of aerospace structures, which are individually organized by leading researchers in the corresponding field.

**Structural Dynamics** - The Structural Dynamics Track contains research topics of responses of flexible aircraft and components under various dynamic loads from different disciplines.

**Materials** - The Materials Track covers many traditional topics on fatigue and fracture, mechanics, computational materials, nanomaterials, manufacturing, and experimental characterization as well as emerging fields within materials.

## List of 2026 International Engineering Conferences (Mechanical, Civil, Electrical, AI)

Planning for2026 conferences now is a strategic move, as many submission deadlines fallbetween late 2025 and early 2026.  
 Below is acurated list of upcoming 2026 engineering conferences confirmed or highlylikely to be indexed by **EI Compendex** and **Scopus**. They arecategorized into **"Flagship Society Events"** (high prestige,organized by major bodies like IEEE, ASME, ASCE) and **"SpecializedInternational Conferences"** (focused topics, generally accessible).  
 **1. Top-TierFlagship Society Conferences (High Prestige)**  
 These are the"must-attend" events for career advancement. They are organized byglobal professional societies, ensuring rigorous peer review and reliableindexing.

| **Conference** | **Dates** | **Location** | **Focus Area** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **IEEE ICRA 2026** (Intl. Conf. on Robotics and Automation) | **June 1–5, 2026** | Vienna, Austria | Robotics, AI, Automation |
| **IEEE/CVF CVPR 2026** (Conf. on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition) | **June 2–6, 2026** | Denver, CO, USA | Computer Vision, AI, Deep Learning |
| **ASME OMAE 2026** (Intl. Conf. on Ocean, Offshore & Arctic Engineering) | **June 7–12, 2026** | Tokyo, Japan | Mechanical, Ocean, Civil Engineering |
| **IEEE PES General Meeting 2026** | **July 19–23, 2026** | Montreal, Canada | Power Systems, Energy, Electrical Eng. |
| **ASCE Structures Congress 2026** | **Apr 29 – May 1, 2026** | Boston, MA, USA | Civil, Structural Engineering |
| **AIChE Annual Meeting 2026** | **Nov 8–12, 2026** | Minneapolis, MN, USA | Chemical Engineering, Materials Science |
| **IEEE ECCE Europe 2026** (Energy Conversion Congress & Expo) | **Sept 14–18, 2026** | Valencia, Spain | Power Electronics, Energy Conversion |

---

**2.Specialized International Conferences (By Field)**  
 Theseconferences are excellent for specific niche research and often have clear,published proceedings indexed by EI/Scopus.  
 **Electrical,Computer & AI Engineering**  

* **ICMLT 2026 (11th Intl. Conf. on Machine Learning Technologies)**
  + **Date:** May 20–22, 2026
  + **Location:** Berlin, Germany
  + **Focus:** Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Data Mining.
* **ICCTA 2026 (12th Intl. Conf. on Computer and Technology Applications)**
* **Date:** June 17–19, 2026
* **Location:** Vienna, Austria
* **Focus:** Software Engineering, Computational Intelligence.
* **Date:** March 27–29, 2026
* **Location:** Yokohama, Japan
* **Focus:** Electronics, Control Systems, Signal Processing.
* **Date:** March 27–29, 2026 (Submission Deadline approx. March 17)
* **Location:** Chongqing, China
* **Focus:** IoT, Big Data, Cloud Computing.
* **ICECC 2026 (9th Intl. Conf. on Electronics, Communications and Control Engineering)**
* **EITCE 2026 (10th Intl. Conf. on Electronic Information Technology and Computer Engineering)**

**Mechanical,Aerospace & Robotics**  

* **ICMAE 2026 (17th Intl. Conf. on Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering)**
  + **Date:** July 15–18, 2026
  + **Location:** London, UK
  + **Focus:** Aerodynamics, Materials, Propulsion.
* **ICMRE 2026 (12th Intl. Conf. on Mechatronics and Robotics Engineering)**
* **Date:** March 2–4, 2026
* **Location:** Oldenburg, Germany
* **Focus:** Mechatronics, Haptics, Robotics.

* **Location:** Hiratsuka, Japan
* **Focus:** Control Engineering, Intelligent Systems.

  + **Focus:** Supply Chain, Operations Research, Industrial Manufacturing.

**3. ImportantTips for Verification**  
 Beforesubmitting, always perform these checks to avoid "predatory"conferences:  

1. **Check Previous Years:** Look up the conference's **2024 or 2025 proceedings** in the EI Compendex or Scopus database. If the previous years are indexed, the 2026 edition is highly likely to be as well.
2. **Verify the Organizer:** Flagship events are organized by societies like **IEEE, ACM, ASME, ASCE, or AIChE**. Specialized events should ideally be sponsored by a university or a recognized technical institute.
3. **Official Website:** Ensure you are on the official domain (e.g., ending in .org or hosted on a university/society subdomain). Avoid generic sites that list hundreds of unrelated conferences.

## International Engineering Conferences in Europe 2026

Attending the engineering conferences in Europe 2026 is beneficial for students, scholars, professionals, vendors, exhibitors, and engineers.
... Read More

Here, you will learn about the latest technology and industry skills important for adapting to the changing engineering industry. The guest speakers are esteemed college professors and experienced engineers who can help you learn nanotechnology, AI-driven machinery, robotics, design engineering, data science, cloud computing, and more. Here, you can exchange ideas with industry peers and get your research reviewed by experts. This is a great opportunity to travel to wonderful places where these conferences are held. So, why wait? Register for an engineering conference now.

##### Commonly Asked Questions

## What are engineering conferences in Europe in 2026 about?

Engineering conferences in Europe in 2026 focus on emerging research, real-world applications, and cross-border collaboration. Experts, practitioners, and academics share trends, tools, and case studies shaping engineering today. Sessions often include keynotes, panels, workshops, and poster presentations designed for practical learning and networking. International Conference Alerts provides timely email notifications.

## What are the benefits of attending engineering conferences in Europe?

Attending engineering conferences in Europe helps you discover new ideas, validate strategies with peers, and expand your professional network. You gain actionable insights, partner leads, and exposure to innovative methods. These events also help you spot funding, research, or career opportunities across different European hubs. International Conference Alerts provides timely email notifications.

## How do I choose the right engineering conference in Europe?

Start by clarifying your goals—skills, research visibility, or partnerships. Check the agenda, speaker lineup, and submission guidelines. Review past editions, attendee profiles, and formats. Pick events aligned with your specialization, budget, and travel plans, while prioritizing programs that offer hands-on sessions or focused tracks. International Conference Alerts provides timely email notifications.

## Are online or hybrid participation options available for engineering conferences in Europe?

Yes. Many organizers across Europe offer hybrid or fully virtual access, with live-streamed talks, recorded sessions, and digital networking. Virtual poster rooms and one-to-one meetings help remote delegates connect. Check each event’s access pass for on-demand availability, platform features, and time-zone friendly scheduling. International Conference Alerts provides timely email notifications.

## Who should attend engineering conferences in Europe?

These conferences suit researchers, practitioners, industry leaders, policymakers, entrepreneurs, and students pursuing engineering. They’re ideal for teams planning projects, startups validating products, and academics exploring collaborations. Newcomers benefit from foundational tracks, while experts can present work, chair sessions, or lead workshops. International Conference Alerts provides timely email notifications. You can subscribe free to receive schedules, venues, and topic updates.

## How can I register for engineering conferences in Europe?

Find an event on International Conference Alerts, open the listing, and follow the official registration link. Compare ticket types, early-bird deadlines, and abstract submission windows. Complete the form with accurate details and payment method. Save confirmation emails and join organizer mailing lists for pre-event updates and access instructions. International Conference Alerts provides timely email notifications.

## What topics and trends will engineering conferences in Europe highlight in 2026?

Expect strong focus on real-world impact, ethics, sustainability, and data-driven decision-making in engineering. Programs often highlight interdisciplinary projects, responsible innovation, and practical deployment. Methodologies, standards, and best practices will feature alongside demos, tool comparisons, and case studies from leading European institutions and companies. International Conference Alerts provides timely email notifications.

## How can I stay updated about upcoming engineering conferences in Europe?

Subscribe free on International Conference Alerts to receive curated email updates on new listings, venues, and deadlines. Bookmark relevant pages and set alerts for your topic. Follow organizers’ social channels, and check calls for papers or exhibitor announcements to secure speaking, showcasing, or partnership opportunities early. International Conference Alerts provides timely email notifications.

## Sponsorship opportunities

### *NAFEMS E​astern Europe Conference 2026*

# **Sponsorships**

## Join us in Budapest

We would like to extend an invitation to your company to be part of the NAFEMS Eastern Europe Conference 2026. There are several outstanding opportunities available for your company to sponsor or exhibit at the conference, giving you exposure to a targeted audience of delegates, who are directly involved in simulation, analysis, and design.

## Team with NAFEMS

As the only International Association for the Engineering Analysis Community, NAFEMS is widely held to be the leading independent source of information and training for engineering analysts and designers at all levels. Sponsors and exhibitors will have the chance to promote their participation prior to the event through the various sponsorship packages we are offering.

Access the list of companies that have had shown interest in our previous regional events.

## Sponsorship options

We understand that different organisations have different requirements and objectives when sponsoring an event. With this in mind, we have decided to offer four levels of sponsorship.

### Gold sponsor

*3000 EUR (net) for NAFEMS members | 4000 EUR (net) for non-members*

### Silver sponsor

*2000 EUR (net) for NAFEMS members | 3000 EUR (net) for non-members*

### Online Sponsor

*1200 EUR (net) for NAFEMS members | 2200 EUR (net) for non-members*

### Exhibitor

*1200 EUR (net) for NAFEMS members | 2200 EUR (net) for non-members*

# Mechanical Conferences in Singapore 2026

Singapore is rapidly emerging as a premier global destination for
**Mechanical** innovation and collaboration. In 2026, the country will host a wide range
of conferences bringing together international researchers, industry pioneers, and academic professionals
to explore the latest breakthroughs and real-world applications in Mechanical.

## 2026 Mechanical Conferences in Singapore – Overview

More than **467** upcoming
**Mechanical** conferences are scheduled across Singapore.
Based on current scheduling trends, **Singapore, Ang Mo Kio, Sengkang, Outram Rd, Bukit Batok** has emerged as the
leading professional hub, hosting approximately
**97.42%** of all specialized events.
For professionals aiming to maximize networking opportunities and knowledge exchange,
**May** stands out as the most active month for the
Mechanical sector in Singapore.

## Why Attend a Mechanical Conference in Singapore?

Attending these conferences offers valuable opportunities for high-level networking,
academic collaboration, and professional growth. Whether you are a researcher, industry
professional, academic, or student, these 2026 Mechanical conferences provide
a platform to share ideas, discover emerging technologies, and build meaningful
professional connections.
Beyond the technical sessions and expert talks, participants can also experience the
dynamic academic and cultural environment that makes Singapore a leading
destination for international conferences and professional development.

Attending a [Mechanical Conference in Singapore](Https://www.allconferencealert.com/singapore/mechanical) offers valuable opportunities for networking, paper presentations, workshops, panel discussions, and keynote sessions led by renowned speakers. Participants will gain insights into emerging developments, best practices, and future directions in **Mechanical**, while also experiencing the academic, cultural, and professional environment of **Singapore**. Whether you are a student, researcher, professor, or industry professional, these **[conferences in Singapore](Https://www.allconferencealert.com/singapore)** during 2026 support learning, career growth, and international exposure in the field of Mechanical.

### Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

#### Which city in Singapore hosts the most Mechanical conferences?

Based on our 2026 data, **Singapore** is the leading hub for
**Mechanical** events in **Singapore**, followed by
Singapore, Ang Mo Kio, Sengkang, Outram Rd, Bukit Batok. This city offers the best infrastructure and networking opportunities
for professionals in this field.

#### What is the best time of year to attend a Mechanical conference in Singapore?

The peak season for **Mechanical** conferences in
**Singapore** is **May**, when the highest
concentration of international summits occurs.

#### How can I get an invitation letter for a Mechanical conference in Singapore?

Most organizers listed on AllConferenceAlert provide official invitation letters for visa purposes
after successful registration. Contact the event coordinator via the “View Event” link.

#### How can I submit a research paper for a Mechanical conference in Singapore?

Most **Mechanical** events in **Singapore** are currently
accepting submissions. Select an event and check the “Call for Papers” section.

#### What are the top venues for Mechanical conferences in Singapore?

In **Singapore**, most **Mechanical** gatherings are held
in premium convention centers or 5-star hotels, especially in
**Singapore**.

#### Are these Mechanical conferences in Singapore open to international participants?

Yes, the **467** conferences listed for 2026 are international
platforms. If traveling to **Singapore**, apply for your
conference visa at least 4–6 weeks before the
**May** peak season.

## PICUP Fintech Conference and Awards

Indian Banks’ Association (IBA) and the Federation of Indian Chambers of Commerce and Industry (FICCI) are jointly organising the 6th edition of the PICUP Fintech Conference and Awards on 23rd April 2026 at Federation House, FICCI, New Delhi. The Boston Consulting Group (BCG) is the Knowledge Partner for the event.

Shri M. Nagaraju, Secretary, Department of Financial Services, Government of India, has kindly agreed to deliver the Inaugural Address at the conference.

India’s fintech revolution stands at the threshold of a transformative decade - one defined by Generative AI (GenAI), digital public infrastructure (DPI), and policy-driven innovation. As fintechs evolve from early disruptors to resilient enterprises, the focus is shifting from survival to scale, access to intelligent inclusion, and national success to global impact.

The conference will bring together leading fintech innovators, banks, NBFCs, insurance companies, technology providers, and policymakers on a common platform to explore how India’s fintech ecosystem can drive this next phase of transformation - uniting innovation, trust, and technology to power inclusive, sustainable, and globally scalable financial growth. The event will feature insightful discussions, demonstration sessions, and an awards ceremony recognizing excellence in fintech innovation.

[Visit Website](https://picupfintech.in/)

# List of fintech conferences in 2026

If you’re interested in expanding your understanding of the Fintech industry, consider attending some of the most anticipated events in 2026

As the [**financial industry**](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GfeBJdEOnZ4) continues to evolve, staying ahead of the latest trends, innovations, and challenges is crucial. At Qubika, we understand the importance of being at the forefront of this fast-paced sector, which is why we actively engage in top fintech events worldwide. These conferences offer valuable insights and opportunities for collaboration, helping shape the future of financial services.

Looking ahead to 2026, we’ve curated a list of recommended [**fintech**](https://qubika.com/industries/financial-services/) conferences that stand out for their content, speakers, and networking potential. Whether you’re a financial leader, a tech innovator, or a fintech enthusiast, these events will provide essential knowledge and connections to keep you competitive in the industry.

We invite you to explore our Financial Services offerings, with expertise in core banking, BaaS integrations, payments, and GenAI-enhanced financial solutions.

**[Qubika’s Finance Studio](https://qubika.com/industries/financial-services/)** empowers organizations to build modern, [AI-driven financial services](https://qubika.com/blog/data-security-in-ai-driven-financial-services/), ensuring you’re ready for the future of finance.

  Bitcoin MENA is the leading Bitcoin conference in the Middle East, returning to ADNEC Centre Abu Dhabi on December 7-8, 2026. The event brings together global leaders, regional policymakers, institutional investors, high-net-worth individuals, and Bitcoin enthusiasts to explore the transformative potential of Bitcoin across the MENA region. Features include multiple stages (Main Stage, Proof of Work Stage, Enterprise Stage), the largest Bitcoin showcase expo hall in the region, Bitcoin for Corporations programming, exclusive Whale Lounge and Whale Night events, premium networking with matchmaking tools, and speakers from leading companies including Strategy, Binance, government cyber security councils, blockchain centers, and major financial institutions. The conference offers three pass types: General Admission, Pro Pass with enterprise access and networking tools, and premium Whale Pass with VIP access and front-row seating.

The fintech India expo is a perfect platform for you to showcase your products and services to an engaged audience that are looking for new business opportunities and partnerships. It also helps domestic Fintechs in their journey to collaborate with companies from across the globe.

![Blockchain Solutions](images/Blockchain-Home-Icon.jpg)

###### Blockchain Solutions

The global blockchain market size is projected to grow from USD 20.1 billion in 2024 to USD 248.9 billion by 2029. It is transforming transaction transparency, security, and efficiency across financial sectors.

![AI & Machine Learning](images/AI-Bharat.jpg)

###### AI & Machine Learning

AI in Fintech market is projected to grow from USD 17.0 billion in 2024 to USD 70.1 billion by 2033, with a CAGR of 17.0%. AI is increasingly being adopted in fintech to automate financial services, with one of its most significant applications being fraud detection.

![Payment Technologies](images/Digital-Banking-Home-Icon.jpg)

###### Payment Technologies

India's digital retail payment sector is set to double to US$ 7 trillion by 2030, driven by UPI and other digital transactions. Experience the latest advancements in personal and business transactions.

![RegTech](images/Digital-Wallets-Home-Icon.jpg)

###### RegTech

The regulatory technology market was valued at USD 18.02 billion in 2022 and is expected to reach the value of USD 710.56 billion by 2030. FIE will showcase the fore-front of RegTech in 2025.

![InsurTech](images/E-commerce-FT-Home-Icon.jpg)

###### InsurTech

India's InsurTech sector is expected to grow by 17% annually to reach $307 Bn by 2030 and this is the perfect platform to explore it.

![WealthTech](images/Education-and-Training-Home-Icon.jpg)

###### WealthTech

Gain insights into India’s WealthTech sector which is expected to triple in size by 2030 to over USD 31 billion.

![Cybersecurity Solutions](images/Financial-Institutions-Home-Icon.jpg)

###### Cybersecurity Solutions

India's cybersecurity market is set to grow by 13.01% (2024-2029), reaching $5.29bn by 2029. Explore cutting-edge technologies safeguarding financial data and digital infrastructure from emerging threats.

![SaaS](images/Forex-Home-Icon.jpg)

###### SaaS

Network with India's emerging SaaS market as it is suspected to hit $50 billion mark by 2030, driven by AI and start-up innovation.

![CPaaS](images/Insurance-Home-Icon.jpg)

###### CPaaS

Showcasing the India communication platform as a service (CPaaS) market which is estimated to reach USD 1.97 billion by 2028.

![Digital Banking Solutions](images/Lending-Home-Icon.jpg)

###### Digital Banking Solutions

Explore the future of banking with cutting-edge digital solutions that redefine customer experience and operational efficiency.

![Payment Gateways](images/Payment-Gateways-Home-Icon.jpg)

###### Payment Gateways

Dive into advanced payment gateway solutions designed to facilitate secure, real-time transactions across industries.

![Fintech Infrastructure](images/Private-Equity-Home-Icon.jpg)

###### Fintech Infrastructure

Bringing together foundational tech such as APIs, cloud computing, payment gateways & data management.

![Provident Funds](images/Provident-funds-home-icon.jpg)

###### Data Analytics Tools

Explore the data analytics market in India as it is expected to reach a projected revenue of US$ 21,286.4 million by 2030.

![Lending Solutions](images/Lending-funds-home-icon.jpg)

###### Lending Solutions

Fintech lending is set for massive global growth by 2030, with projections hitting nearly $5 trillion, especially in Asia, with India's market alone potentially exceeding $1.3 trillion

![Taxation Solutions](images/Taxation-solutions-home-icon.jpg)

###### Taxation Solutions

Explore fintech-driven platforms streamlining tax filing, reporting, and compliance for businesses and individuals alike.

![AI in Fintech](images/AI-Fintech-home-icon.jpg)

###### AI in Fintech

The global artificial intelligence in fintech market size was valued at USD 9.45 billion in 2021 and is projected to reach USD 41.16 billion by 2030. With AI revolutionising industries, this is the perfect platform to showcase AI in Fintech.

## About **‘BFSI & FinTech Summit 2026’**

India’s BFSI and FinTech sector was transforming the economy through digital infrastructure, AI-driven decisioning, robust cybersecurity, and inclusive financial solutions powered by UPI, Aadhaar, and Account Aggregators. Private credit was emerging as a key capital source, creating new opportunities for businesses and investors. Dun & Bradstreet’s **BFSI & FinTech Summit 2026** brought together leaders from banking, financial services, fintech, regulators, and technology providers to explore bold ideas and big shifts shaping India’s financial future under the theme:
**"Big Shift, Bold Ideas: Shaping the Financial Future for India."**

## The Publication - **‘Big Shift, Bold Ideas: Trends Shaping India’s Financial Future’**

**An exclusive report that uncovers the trends shaping the future of India’s
BFSI industry. The publication will cover:**

Impact of consolidation and retail participation on liquidity and investment flows

AI-led evolution, cybersecurity, and data governance in BFSI

Role of insurance and credit in driving inclusion and risk protection

Emergence of private credit as a key capital source and structured solutions

[George El-Hage](/pages/about-george) [🔗 LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/georgeelhage/)

Founder, Wave Connect | Helped finance teams at Fortune 500 companies deploy digital business cards

I've worked with finance teams - from boutique advisory firms to global banks - for the past six years, helping them replace paper cards with digital ones. Finance conferences are where relationships turn into deals, and I built this calendar to help you plan your year.

If you're looking for **finance conferences in 2026**, I put together the most complete calendar of finance conferences, fintech trade shows, banking summits, and wealth management events happening across the United States this year. Whether you're a CFO, fintech founder, financial advisor, or banking executive, there's something on this list for you.

I organized everything by sub-niche - fintech, corporate finance, banking, wealth management, accounting, and capital markets - so you can quickly find the events that match your role. One tip before you dive in: if you're attending any of these, set up a [digital business card for your finance team](https://wavecnct.com/pages/teams) before you go. I've seen finance professionals hand out hundreds of paper cards at Money20/20 and AFP, only to lose half those connections by the time they get home. A QR code on your phone gets you instant follow-ups. For event-specific networking strategies, check out my guide on [how to capture and convert leads at trade shows](/blogs/news/how-to-capture-leads-at-a-trade-show).

Here's every major finance and fintech event in the U.S. for 2026.

* **25+ events:** Every major finance conference, fintech summit, and banking event in the U.S. for 2026
* **Organized by category:** Fintech, CFO/Corporate Finance, Banking, Wealth Management, Accounting, and Capital Markets
* **Quick-reference table:** All events with dates, locations, focus areas, and estimated costs
* **Full details per event:** Dates, venues, attendee counts, CPE/CPD info, costs, and registration links
* **Networking tips:** How to maximize ROI from every finance conference you attend

## How to Use This Guide

I've organized every event by sub-niche - **Fintech & Payments**, **CFO & Corporate Finance**, **Banking & Financial Services**, **Financial Advisor & Wealth Management**, **Accounting & Audit**, and **Investment & Capital Markets** - so you can jump straight to the categories that matter most to your role.

Each event card includes dates, location, cost, attendee estimates, and a direct registration link. Bookmark this page - I'll update it monthly as new events are announced and dates are confirmed.

## Fintech & Payments Conferences

If you're building, investing in, or buying fintech products, these are the events where the entire payments and financial technology ecosystem converges. From Money20/20's massive expo floor to Finovate's rapid-fire demo format, this is where deals happen.

### [Money20/20 USA](https://us.money2020.com/)

**Dates:** October 18-21, 2026  
 **Location:** The Venetian, Las Vegas, NV  
 **Attendees:** 11,000+  
 **Cost:** Standard Pass from $2,899 | Platinum Pass $5,995

The single biggest fintech event in the world. Money20/20 USA brings together senior leaders from banks, fintechs, payments companies, tech firms, and regulators for four days of deal-making. The 2026 content pillars focus on borderless financial architecture, agentic AI in finance, embedded commerce, and trust infrastructure. One in three attendees is C-suite, and 85% of the world's top 20 banks send teams. If you can only go to one fintech conference, this is the one. Book early - hotel blocks sell out months ahead.

[Official Website →](https://us.money2020.com/)

### [FinovateSpring](https://www.finovate.com/spring/)

**Dates:** May 5-7, 2026  
 **Location:** Sheraton San Diego Resort, San Diego, CA  
 **Attendees:** 1,200+  
 **Cost:** Conference Day from $1,299 | All-Access $2,898

Finovate's signature format: 50+ companies each get exactly 7 minutes to demo their product live on stage. No slides, no pitch decks - just working technology. If you're a banker evaluating vendors, a VC looking for fintech deal flow, or a product leader who wants to see what's shipping, FinovateSpring is one of the most efficient ways to scan the landscape. The audience skews senior - 600+ attendees come from banks, credit unions, and financial institutions.

[Official Website →](https://www.finovate.com/spring/)

### [FinovateFall](https://www.finovate.com/fall/)

**Dates:** September 9-11, 2026  
 **Location:** New York Marriott Marquis, New York, NY  
 **Attendees:** 1,500+  
 **Cost:** From $1,299

The East Coast counterpart to FinovateSpring, typically larger and with a heavier Wall Street presence. FinovateFall 2026 features 60+ live product demos and 120+ speakers covering AI, CX, payments, open banking, lending, and wealth management. The New York location means easy access for the massive financial services community in the tri-state area.

[Official Website →](https://www.finovate.com/fall/)

### [The Financial Brand Forum](https://financialbrandforum.com/)

**Dates:** April 13-15, 2026  
 **Location:** Las Vegas, NV  
 **Attendees:** 3,000+  
 **Cost:** From $1,995

Described as "the best conference in the banking world," the Financial Brand Forum focuses on growth strategies and innovation for banks and credit unions. The content is practical and action-oriented - sessions cover digital transformation, AI-powered customer experiences, marketing for financial institutions, and competitive positioning. If you're a bank CMO, head of digital, or credit union executive, this one's tailored for you.

[Official Website →](https://financialbrandforum.com/)
