## CFO & Corporate Finance Events

These are the conferences built for finance leaders - CFOs, VPs of Finance, FP&A directors, and controllers. The focus is strategic: cost optimization, AI in finance, growth investment, and building resilient finance organizations. If you sit in a corner office or aspire to one, start here.

### [Gartner Finance Symposium/Xpo](https://www.gartner.com/en/conferences/na/cfo-finance-us)

**Dates:** May 27-29, 2026  
 **Location:** Gaylord National Resort, National Harbor, MD  
 **Attendees:** 300+ CFOs and finance executives  
 **Cost:** From $4,175

Gartner calls this "The World's Most Important Gathering of CFOs and Finance Executives" - and the audience backs that up. This is a CFO-exclusive community where sessions are shaped by Gartner's data-driven research. The 2026 theme is "Autonomous Finance" - building resilient, AI-driven enterprises. Sessions cover cost optimization, high-value AI use cases for finance, digital skills, and accelerating finance transformation. The National Harbor location, just outside D.C., makes it accessible for policy-adjacent conversations too.

[Official Website →](https://www.gartner.com/en/conferences/na/cfo-finance-us)

### [FEI Financial Leaders Forum](https://www.financialexecutives.org/)

**Dates:** May 17-19, 2026  
 **Location:** San Antonio Marriott River Center, San Antonio, TX  
 **Attendees:** 500+  
 **Cost:** FEI Members $1,195 | Non-members $1,695

Formerly known as the Financial Leadership Summit, FEI's flagship event brings together CFOs, controllers, treasurers, and tax directors. The renamed "Financial Leaders Forum" reflects the evolving role of finance professionals as strategic leaders. It's more intimate than Gartner's event but equally focused on real-world finance leadership challenges. The River Walk setting in San Antonio doesn't hurt either.

[Official Website →](https://www.financialexecutives.org/)

### [FMA Applied Finance Conference](https://www.fma.org/2026-applied-finance-conference)

**Dates:** May 15, 2026  
 **Location:** St. John's University, New York, NY  
 **Cost:** From $250

A more academic, focused event hosted at St. John's Manhattan campus. The FMA Applied Finance Conference is smaller by design - fewer papers, more in-depth presentations and discussion. Great for academics, researchers, and finance practitioners who want rigorous, peer-reviewed insights rather than vendor-driven content. Now in its fourteenth year.

[Official Website →](https://www.fma.org/2026-applied-finance-conference)

### [Milken Institute Future of Finance](https://milkeninstitute.org/events/future-finance-2026)

**Dates:** March 3-4, 2026  
 **Location:** Washington, DC  
 **Cost:** Invitation-based

A high-level gathering from the Milken Institute that brings together investors, policymakers, and innovators to explore how financial systems can better support economic mobility. The 2026 event focuses on outcomes-based investments, economic resilience, and the intersection of policy and finance innovation. If you can get an invitation, the room is packed with decision-makers.

[Official Website →](https://milkeninstitute.org/events/future-finance-2026)

### [AFP Annual Conference](https://conference.financialprofessionals.org/)

**Dates:** October 2026 (dates TBD)  
 **Location:** TBD  
 **Attendees:** 6,500+  
 **Cost:** From $1,495  
 **CPE Credits:** Yes - CTP and FP&A certified credits available

The Association for Financial Professionals hosts the largest treasury and finance conference in the U.S. AFP covers treasury management, payments, FP&A, risk, and corporate finance. It's the kind of event where you'll find your entire peer group - from senior treasury managers to FP&A directors to CFOs. The expo floor is packed with banking partners, treasury tech vendors, and payment providers. Great for CPE credit accumulation too.

[Official Website →](https://conference.financialprofessionals.org/)

## Banking & Financial Services Conferences

From community banking to global trade finance, these events are where banking professionals gather to discuss regulation, risk management, payments innovation, and the future of the industry. If you're in banking, at least one of these should be on your calendar.

### [ABA Risk and Compliance Conference](https://www.aba.com/training-events/conferences/risk-and-compliance-conference)

**Dates:** May 5-7, 2026  
 **Location:** Charlotte Convention Center, Charlotte, NC  
 **Attendees:** 2,000+  
 **Cost:** ABA Members from $1,395

The American Bankers Association merged their Risk Management Conference and Regulatory Compliance Conference into this combined powerhouse event. It covers risk strategy, regulatory compliance, AI integration, emerging threats, and cost-saving opportunities. If you're in banking compliance, risk, or BSA/AML, this is the annual gathering you don't skip. The Charlotte location puts you in one of the largest banking hubs in the country.

[Official Website →](https://www.aba.com/training-events/conferences/risk-and-compliance-conference)

### [ABA Conference for Community Bankers](https://aba.com/training-events/conferences/conference-for-community-bankers)

**Dates:** February 15-17, 2026  
 **Location:** Orlando, FL  
 **Attendees:** 1,500+  
 **Cost:** ABA Members from $1,295

Focused entirely on community banking - the issues, opportunities, and competitive dynamics that community bank leaders face. Sessions cover everything from M&A strategy to digital banking for smaller institutions to competing with fintechs. The peer networking is the real draw - you're in a room full of people running banks similar to yours.

[Official Website →](https://aba.com/training-events/conferences/conference-for-community-bankers)

### [BAFT Global Annual Meeting](https://baft.org/event/2026-global-annual-meeting/)

**Dates:** 2026 (dates TBD)  
 **Location:** TBD  
 **Attendees:** 500+

The Bankers Association for Finance and Trade hosts its flagship gathering for senior leaders across transaction banking, trade finance, and payments. Following a record-setting 2025 conference, BAFT continues to attract the decision-makers shaping international trade finance. Great for anyone in correspondent banking, trade services, or cross-border payments.

[Official Website →](https://baft.org/event/2026-global-annual-meeting/)

### [American Banker Payments Forum](https://payments-forum.americanbanker.com/)

**Dates:** 2026 (dates TBD)  
 **Location:** Grand Hyatt, San Francisco, CA  
 **Cost:** From $2,295

Focused on the next era of money movement - real-time cross-border payments, digital assets moving from experimentation to execution, and AI in financial services. The event brings together leaders navigating payments modernization under pressure from regulators, customers, and partners. A more curated, executive-level format than the mega conferences.

[Official Website →](https://payments-forum.americanbanker.com/)

![Blog illustration](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0559/6246/8523/files/finance-fintech-conferences-usa-2026-body-4.jpg?v=1774467403)

## Financial Advisor & Wealth Management Conferences

The financial advisor conference circuit is one of the most active in all of finance. Whether you're an RIA, fee-only planner, or wirehouse advisor, these events offer CE credits, practice management insights, and vendor access. I've included the big national events plus a few niche picks that advisors consistently rate highly.

### [T3 Technology Conference](https://t3conferences.com/)

**Dates:** March 9-12, 2026  
 **Location:** Hyatt Regency, New Orleans, LA  
 **Attendees:** 1,000+  
 **Cost:** From $1,295

Called "#1 in wealthtech conferences" by industry analysts, T3 is where financial advisors and enterprise firms go to see the full technology landscape in one place. From bleeding-edge tech demos to keynotes on the future of financial services, T3 offers an unmatched view of what's coming. Now in its 23rd year, the conference has earned a reputation for surfacing the tools that advisors actually adopt. If you're evaluating tech for your practice, this is where to start.

[Official Website →](https://t3conferences.com/)

### [Morningstar Investment Conference](https://www.morningstar.com/business/events/morningstar-investment-conference)

**Dates:** June 17-18, 2026  
 **Location:** Navy Pier, Chicago, IL  
 **Attendees:** 2,000+  
 **Cost:** From $499

Now in its 38th year, the Morningstar Investment Conference delivers what advisors and investors need most - clarity, independent perspective, and actionable strategies. The 2026 agenda tackles today's pressing investment challenges head-on, with speakers from across the industry. The Chicago Navy Pier location is iconic, and the price point is one of the most accessible for a major finance event. If you're an advisor or investment professional, this conference punches well above its weight relative to cost.

[Official Website →](https://www.morningstar.com/business/events/morningstar-investment-conference)

### [Schwab IMPACT](https://impact.schwab.com/)

**Dates:** October 27-29, 2026  
 **Location:** Boston, MA  
 **Attendees:** 4,000+  
 **Cost:** From $899

The premier event for RIAs and independent recordkeepers who custody with Schwab. IMPACT consistently draws the industry's largest gathering of independent advisors, making it the single best place to see where the RIA channel is heading. The content covers practice management, investment strategy, technology, and business growth. The exhibitor hall is enormous, and the networking opportunities are unmatched in the RIA space.

[Official Website →](https://impact.schwab.com/)

### [NAPFA Spring National Conference](https://www.napfa.org/community/future-conferences)

**Dates:** May 6-9, 2026  
 **Location:** Hilton Minneapolis, Minneapolis, MN  
 **Attendees:** 400+  
 **Cost:** NAPFA Members from $695

## Tips for Getting the Most Out of Finance Conferences

I've attended and worked with teams at dozens of finance conferences over the past six years. Here's what separates the people who get ROI from their conference investment from those who don't.

**1. Set specific meeting goals before you go.** Don't just show up and hope for the best. Most major finance conferences - Money20/20, Schwab IMPACT, Global Alts - have meeting scheduling tools built into their event apps. Use them. Block 30-minute meetings with 8-10 people you want to connect with before you even arrive. If you're looking for tips on maximizing event networking, check out the [best digital business card options for events and conferences](/blogs/news/best-digital-business-card-for-events-and-conferences).

**2. Track your CPE and CPD credits in advance.** Many finance conferences offer continuing education credits, but you often need to register for specific sessions to earn them. Check which sessions qualify before the event and build your schedule around them. AICPA ENGAGE, FPA Summit, and AFP all have robust CPE programs.

**3. Follow up within 48 hours.** The single biggest mistake I see finance professionals make is waiting too long to follow up after a conference. You met someone meaningful at the AFP Conference? Send a personalized message within 48 hours while the conversation is still fresh. A [Wave Connect digital card](https://wavecnct.com/pages/teams) makes this easier - you can see exactly who saved your contact info and when.

**4. Budget beyond the registration fee.** Hotel, flights, meals, and client entertainment add up fast. A Money20/20 trip can easily run $5,000-$7,000 all-in. Factor the full cost into your ROI calculation, and prioritize events where the attendee profile matches your target audience.

**5. Attend at least one event outside your niche.** A fintech founder attending T3? A CFO at Finovate? Cross-pollination drives the best ideas. Some of the most valuable connections I've seen happen when someone steps outside their usual conference circuit. Our guide to [technology conferences in the U.S.](/blogs/news/technology-conferences-trade-shows-usa-2026) has more cross-industry options worth exploring.

**6. Maximize the expo floor early.** Expo floors at conferences like AICPA ENGAGE and the Financial Brand Forum get crowded by mid-afternoon. Hit the floor in the first hour it opens, when vendors are fresh and you can have real conversations instead of rushed pitches.

## FAQ: Finance Conferences in 2026

### What is the biggest finance conference in the U.S.?

**Money20/20 USA (Las Vegas, October) is the largest fintech and payments conference with 11,000+ attendees.** For treasury and corporate finance, AFP Annual Conference draws 6,500+. For accounting, AICPA ENGAGE attracts 4,000+ CPAs and firm leaders. Schwab IMPACT is the largest for independent financial advisors with 4,000+ RIAs.

### How much do finance conferences cost?

**Prices range from $250 (FMA Applied Finance) to $5,995 (Money20/20 Platinum Pass).** Most mid-tier finance conferences fall in the $800-$2,000 range. Some events like Milken Global Conference and iConnections Global Alts are invitation-only. Early-bird registration typically saves 15-25%.

### Can I earn CPE credits at these conferences?

**Yes - many finance conferences offer CPE, CE, and CPD credits.** AICPA ENGAGE offers extensive CPE across tax, audit, and technology tracks. FPA Summit provides CFP, CIMA, and CPWA credits. NAPFA conferences and AFP Annual Conference also offer continuing education. Check each event's website for specific credit programs and qualifying sessions.

### What should I bring to a finance conference?

**A digital business card (or NFC card), a portable charger, business-appropriate attire, and a light laptop or tablet.** Finance conferences tend to skew more formal than tech events. Skip the stack of paper cards - a QR code on your phone is more efficient and ensures you capture every contact digitally.

### What's the difference between Money20/20 and Finovate?

**Money20/20 is a massive 11,000+ attendee fintech event focused on networking and deal-making. Finovate is a smaller, demo-driven event where 50+ companies present live product demos in 7 minutes.** Money20/20 is where you build relationships; Finovate is where you evaluate technology. Many fintech professionals attend both.

### Are fintech conferences worth it for traditional bankers?

**Absolutely.** Events like FinovateSpring and the Financial Brand Forum are specifically designed for banks evaluating fintech partnerships. 600+ FinovateSpring attendees come from banks and credit unions. The vendor landscape at these events directly addresses banking digital transformation needs.

### How do I network effectively at finance events?

**Pre-schedule meetings through the event app, set a goal of 8-10 meaningful conversations per day, and follow up within 48 hours.** Quality beats quantity. One deep conversation with the right person is worth more than 50 card exchanges. Use a digital business card to track who saved your info and prioritize your follow-ups accordingly.

### Do finance conferences offer virtual attendance?

**Many do, but the value of in-person attendance is significantly higher for finance events.** AICPA ENGAGE offers a live-online option. Money20/20 and Finovate typically have limited virtual components. For relationship-driven industries like finance, the hallway conversations, dinners, and impromptu meetings are where the real value lives.

## Tips for Getting the Most Out of Finance Conferences

I've attended and worked with teams at dozens of finance conferences over the past six years. Here's what separates the people who get ROI from their conference investment from those who don't.

**1. Set specific meeting goals before you go.** Don't just show up and hope for the best. Most major finance conferences - Money20/20, Schwab IMPACT, Global Alts - have meeting scheduling tools built into their event apps. Use them. Block 30-minute meetings with 8-10 people you want to connect with before you even arrive. If you're looking for tips on maximizing event networking, check out the [best digital business card options for events and conferences](/blogs/news/best-digital-business-card-for-events-and-conferences).

**2. Track your CPE and CPD credits in advance.** Many finance conferences offer continuing education credits, but you often need to register for specific sessions to earn them. Check which sessions qualify before the event and build your schedule around them. AICPA ENGAGE, FPA Summit, and AFP all have robust CPE programs.

**3. Follow up within 48 hours.** The single biggest mistake I see finance professionals make is waiting too long to follow up after a conference. You met someone meaningful at the AFP Conference? Send a personalized message within 48 hours while the conversation is still fresh. A [Wave Connect digital card](https://wavecnct.com/pages/teams) makes this easier - you can see exactly who saved your contact info and when.

**4. Budget beyond the registration fee.** Hotel, flights, meals, and client entertainment add up fast. A Money20/20 trip can easily run $5,000-$7,000 all-in. Factor the full cost into your ROI calculation, and prioritize events where the attendee profile matches your target audience.

**5. Attend at least one event outside your niche.** A fintech founder attending T3? A CFO at Finovate? Cross-pollination drives the best ideas. Some of the most valuable connections I've seen happen when someone steps outside their usual conference circuit. Our guide to [technology conferences in the U.S.](/blogs/news/technology-conferences-trade-shows-usa-2026) has more cross-industry options worth exploring.

**6. Maximize the expo floor early.** Expo floors at conferences like AICPA ENGAGE and the Financial Brand Forum get crowded by mid-afternoon. Hit the floor in the first hour it opens, when vendors are fresh and you can have real conversations instead of rushed pitches.

## FAQ: Finance Conferences in 2026

### What is the biggest finance conference in the U.S.?

**Money20/20 USA (Las Vegas, October) is the largest fintech and payments conference with 11,000+ attendees.** For treasury and corporate finance, AFP Annual Conference draws 6,500+. For accounting, AICPA ENGAGE attracts 4,000+ CPAs and firm leaders. Schwab IMPACT is the largest for independent financial advisors with 4,000+ RIAs.

### How much do finance conferences cost?

**Prices range from $250 (FMA Applied Finance) to $5,995 (Money20/20 Platinum Pass).** Most mid-tier finance conferences fall in the $800-$2,000 range. Some events like Milken Global Conference and iConnections Global Alts are invitation-only. Early-bird registration typically saves 15-25%.

### Can I earn CPE credits at these conferences?

**Yes - many finance conferences offer CPE, CE, and CPD credits.** AICPA ENGAGE offers extensive CPE across tax, audit, and technology tracks. FPA Summit provides CFP, CIMA, and CPWA credits. NAPFA conferences and AFP Annual Conference also offer continuing education. Check each event's website for specific credit programs and qualifying sessions.

### What should I bring to a finance conference?

**A digital business card (or NFC card), a portable charger, business-appropriate attire, and a light laptop or tablet.** Finance conferences tend to skew more formal than tech events. Skip the stack of paper cards - a QR code on your phone is more efficient and ensures you capture every contact digitally.

### What's the difference between Money20/20 and Finovate?

**Money20/20 is a massive 11,000+ attendee fintech event focused on networking and deal-making. Finovate is a smaller, demo-driven event where 50+ companies present live product demos in 7 minutes.** Money20/20 is where you build relationships; Finovate is where you evaluate technology. Many fintech professionals attend both.

### Are fintech conferences worth it for traditional bankers?

**Absolutely.** Events like FinovateSpring and the Financial Brand Forum are specifically designed for banks evaluating fintech partnerships. 600+ FinovateSpring attendees come from banks and credit unions. The vendor landscape at these events directly addresses banking digital transformation needs.

### How do I network effectively at finance events?

**Pre-schedule meetings through the event app, set a goal of 8-10 meaningful conversations per day, and follow up within 48 hours.** Quality beats quantity. One deep conversation with the right person is worth more than 50 card exchanges. Use a digital business card to track who saved your info and prioritize your follow-ups accordingly.

### Do finance conferences offer virtual attendance?

**Many do, but the value of in-person attendance is significantly higher for finance events.** AICPA ENGAGE offers a live-online option. Money20/20 and Finovate typically have limited virtual components. For relationship-driven industries like finance, the hallway conversations, dinners, and impromptu meetings are where the real value lives.

Description

Industry and legal experts discuss the regulatory and legislative developments most likely to influence blockchain innovation, digital asset markets and prediction platforms, with particular focus on the evolving jurisdictional roles of the SEC and CFTC.

Understanding Debanking: Risks, Regulation, and Emerging Considerations

Details

[![](https://assets-global.website-files.com/65f8444971cd47495049b747/66079a8340e39c6921b149b4_facebook%20(2).svg)](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=d2-437NHfVw%2F&title=Understanding Debanking: Risks, Regulation, and Emerging Considerations %3F)   [![](https://assets-global.website-files.com/65f8444971cd47495049b747/66079a8340e39c6921b149b9_linkedin%20(1).svg)](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=d2-437NHfVw%2F&title=Understanding Debanking: Risks, Regulation, and Emerging Considerations &summary=)   [![](https://assets-global.website-files.com/65f8444971cd47495049b747/66079a83af7f72c2d5d38144_Capa_1.svg)](https://twitter.com/share?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=d2-437NHfVw%2F&title=Understanding Debanking: Risks, Regulation, and Emerging Considerations &summary=)

Description

In collaboration with Jenner & Block, AFC’s Fintech Forward webinar will focus on debanking and the evolving regulatory, legal, and policy landscape surrounding access to financial services. Join industry and legal experts for a timely discussion on what debanking means for fintechs and financial institutions, emerging risks, and practical considerations for navigating compliance while supporting responsible innovation, including recent enforcement trends and what stakeholders should be preparing for in the months ahead.

To CFPB or Not to CFPB, That Is the Question: Navigating Uncertainty and Regulatory Developments

Details

[![](https://assets-global.website-files.com/65f8444971cd47495049b747/66079a8340e39c6921b149b4_facebook%20(2).svg)](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8N1dk7SDeIU%2F&title=To CFPB or Not to CFPB, That Is the Question: Navigating Uncertainty and Regulatory Developments%3F)   [![](https://assets-global.website-files.com/65f8444971cd47495049b747/66079a8340e39c6921b149b9_linkedin%20(1).svg)](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8N1dk7SDeIU%2F&title=To CFPB or Not to CFPB, That Is the Question: Navigating Uncertainty and Regulatory Developments&summary=)   [![](https://assets-global.website-files.com/65f8444971cd47495049b747/66079a83af7f72c2d5d38144_Capa_1.svg)](https://twitter.com/share?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8N1dk7SDeIU%2F&title=To CFPB or Not to CFPB, That Is the Question: Navigating Uncertainty and Regulatory Developments&summary=)

Description

The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau’s existence and regulatory agenda continues to evolve, creating uncertainty for financial services companies navigating new and proposed rules. This webinar will examine the latest CFPB regulatory developments, with a focus on recently issued regulations that may face shifting timelines or enforcement questions.Panelists will discuss the latest on the funding showdown, chances for a future CFPB, key rules expected to be in play, including the Section 1033 open banking rule and earned wage access, as well as other notable CFPB actions. Join AFC and industry experts for a practical conversation on what these developments mean for fintech companies and financial institutions, how to manage regulatory risk, and how to prepare amid ongoing uncertainty.

# List of fintech conferences in 2026

If you’re interested in expanding your understanding of the Fintech industry, consider attending some of the most anticipated events in 2026

As the [**financial industry**](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GfeBJdEOnZ4) continues to evolve, staying ahead of the latest trends, innovations, and challenges is crucial. At Qubika, we understand the importance of being at the forefront of this fast-paced sector, which is why we actively engage in top fintech events worldwide. These conferences offer valuable insights and opportunities for collaboration, helping shape the future of financial services.

Looking ahead to 2026, we’ve curated a list of recommended [**fintech**](https://qubika.com/industries/financial-services/) conferences that stand out for their content, speakers, and networking potential. Whether you’re a financial leader, a tech innovator, or a fintech enthusiast, these events will provide essential knowledge and connections to keep you competitive in the industry.

We invite you to explore our Financial Services offerings, with expertise in core banking, BaaS integrations, payments, and GenAI-enhanced financial solutions.

**[Qubika’s Finance Studio](https://qubika.com/industries/financial-services/)** empowers organizations to build modern, [AI-driven financial services](https://qubika.com/blog/data-security-in-ai-driven-financial-services/), ensuring you’re ready for the future of finance.

#### FinTech conferences 2026

# Best FinTech Conferences to Attend in 2026 [Calendar]

* [tech conference](/blog/?tag=tech conference)
* [FinTech](/blog/?tag=fintech)
* [FinTech Conference](/blog/?tag=fintech conference)
* [technology events](/blog/?tag=technology events)

[Dariusz Jaroń](/blog/?author=Dariusz%20Jaroń)

21 Nov 2025. 17 minutes read

FinTech loves face-to-face talks. However, for IT decision-makers, attending the wrong event is a serious waste of time. We've filtered the noise to bring you a definitive, high-signal list for accessing the future of financial tech in 2026. These conferences aren't time-fillers; they are where you validate your tech stack, secure strategic integrations, and get a preview of the roadmaps your competitors *wish* they saw.

In this guide, we focused on highlighting the standout, high-impact conferences globally, with an emphasis on the US and London: major hubs for the industry's most influential gatherings. Note that many events scheduled for late 2026 are still being finalized and will be updated as details become available.

## Top FinTech events to consider in 2026

Ready to stay ahead in the industry and drive innovation? Here is our breakdown of the must-attend [FinTech](https://softwaremill.com/services/fintech-software-development/) events for 2026, scheduled month-by-month for optimal planning.

### Fintech Retreat 26

**When?** January 25-26, 2026 at Golden Gate Club, San Francisco, USA

[Fintech Retreat](https://www.fintechretreat.com/) is a small-scale summit hosting approximately 175 senior participants, including investors, founders, regulators, and digital assets professionals.

The event's format emphasizes in-depth, off-the-record discussions in a high-trust setting. Organizers prioritize focused interaction over broad presentations, featuring formats like an open mic session, hot seats AMA with industry leaders, and technical interactive sessions on key areas such as payments and AI in finance.

It takes place at the Golden Gate Club in San Francisco, concurrently running alongside DeFi Retreat with overlapping networking opportunities.

### Finovate Europe 2026

**When?** February 10-11, 2026 at Intercontinental 02, London, UK

It is the continent’s premier executive event, built for decision-makers shaping financial services. Its core value is market-ready innovation, evidenced by 30+ live demos and over 100 speakers.

The format is intentionally fast-paced and curated, packing 1000+ senior-level attendees into a high-density environment to efficiently connect leaders with tangible solutions across the ecosystem, covering AI, payments, embedded finance, and RegTech.

Networking at [Finovate Europe](https://informaconnect.com/finovateeurope/) is crucial, driven by the presence of 700+ C-level executives, heads, and VPs from top institutions like HSBC, Barclays, and Citi. This density ensures your time is spent forging strategic connections.

### FiNext Conference

**When?** February 11, 2026 at Le Meridien Dubai Hotel & Conference Centre, Dubai, UAE

FiNext Conference & Expo functions as a comprehensive digital hub designed to connect the entire global financial ecosystem under one roof. It specifically targets a diverse group: startups, SMEs, incumbent tech providers, financial organizations, and investors. The event's primary goal is facilitating interaction, allowing stakeholders to share experiences, forge partnerships, and learn collaboratively.

The conference agenda covers critical operational and strategic areas for IT decision-makers and executives, including core banking, payments, cybersecurity, commercial payment strategies, and cloud IT platforms for financial institutions.

[FiNext](https://finextcon.com/) attracts a broad, engaged audience: financial professionals seeking trend adoption, entrepreneurs exploring collaboration, investors scouting for the next breakthrough, and policymakers gaining insight into evolving regulatory frameworks.

### Africa Payments & RegTech Forum

**When?** February 12, 2026 at Hilton Sandton, Johannesburg, South Africa

[Africa Payments & RegTech Forum](https://dev.events/conferences/africa-payments-and-reg-tech-forum-g4helhyw/) is positioned as the top event for financial services professionals focused on the African continent. Gathering banking, FinTech, and compliance leaders from Africa and globally, the forum directly addresses how payment innovation and robust digital regulation are driving continental economic transformation and financial inclusion. Held in Johannesburg, this summit connects regulators, banks, and innovators to shape regional policy and market direction.

The conference agenda is highly specialized, offering clear insights into key regional drivers: AI, digital payments, blockchain, RegTech, cybersecurity, and biometric authentication. This forum is mandatory for professionals seeking the latest trends in compliance and payments specific to the African market. Its value lies in gathering global key influencers, strategists, and thought leaders in this niche, ensuring unmatched networking concentrated solely on regulatory and payments technology.

### FinTech Weeks (Dubai & Amsterdam)

**When?** February 16-17, 2026, Dubai UAE, February 26-27, 2026, Amsterdam, Netherlands

Organized by the same group, these two events offer a strategic, multi-regional view of the financial future. The 2nd Fintech Week Dubai (February 16–17, 2026) capitalizes on the UAE’s dynamic environment, focusing specifically on the central theme of payments and security. It serves as a premier global event for networking and insights in one of the world's fastest-growing financial hubs.

The series continues with the 3rd [Fintech Week](https://fintech.peoplevents.uk/) & Expo Amsterdam (February 26–27, 2026). This edition centers on "Trust, Transformation & Tech: The Future of Financial Innovation." Both summits target the entire ecosystem, including professionals from banking, insurance, retail, regulators, startups, and VCs, and feature over 50 main sponsors and exhibitors from across the financial services and tech sectors.

### TradingTech Summit London

**When?** February 26, 2026 at Hilton Canary Wharf, London, UK

The [TradingTech Summit London](https://a-teaminsight.com/events/tradingtech-summit-london/) is an established, technology-centric forum now in its 15th year, dedicated exclusively to the Capital Markets trading technology and market data industry. The event convenes professionals from the sell-side and buy-side to address the practical challenges driving technology transformation. The focus is heavily weighted towards infrastructure modernization, specifically the practicalities of legacy system migration and re-platforming, alongside unlocking ROI through data monetization.

This summit features focused streams offering deep dives into actionable strategies for performance and agility. Key topics for IT decision-makers include architecting for ROI, optimizing high-performance trading infrastructure for competitive edge, and integrating AI to redefine the human-machine dynamic in trading workflows. New streams for 2026 will also explore composable trading platforms, hybrid cloud optimization for the front office, and the strategic imperative of data monetization and tokenization.

### MoneyLIVE Summit

**When?** March 9-10, 2026 at Business Design Centre, London, UK

The [MoneyLIVE Summit](https://moneylive-insights.com/events/summit/) is the global banking and payments event that sets the industry agenda. Hosted in London, the recognized FinTech capital of the world, it unites industry leaders at the top of their game. The summit’s strength is its executive focus: 60% of its 1,700+ attendees are C-level, director, VP, or head of seniority, ensuring high-value discussions and networking.

The content is cutting-edge, featuring 200+ speakers from influential institutions like Standard Chartered Bank, ING, Monzo, and Uber. Key themes are spread across six stages, covering strategic areas such as AI, open finance, payments, digital identity, CBDCs, and counterfraud. The event also features a dedicated Startup City hub, designed to accelerate scale-up growth with exclusive networking zones and booths.

The networking continues at the official after party, where those all-important conversations truly happen.

### Merchant Payments Ecosystem

**When?** March 17-19, 2026, InterContinental, Berlin, Germany

[MPE Berlin](https://www.merchantpaymentsecosystem.com/) is recognized as Europe’s home for merchant payments, gathering over 1,600 global payment professionals. Its core strength lies in amplifying merchant voices, with over 500 merchants expected to attend. The 2026 theme, "Transformation & Mirroring," focuses on how the industry (PSPs, banks, regulators) reflects the needs of merchants amid changes driven by AI, data, and real-time rails. The agenda is built for both strategy and execution, covering checkout experiences, fraud, acceptance strategies, and payment infrastructure.

The event emphasizes practical interaction through increased micro-networking formats, including more roundtables and workshops, ensuring genuine connection over scale. Key technical and strategic program lenses include AI in payments, orchestration, PSD3/PCI, embedded finance, and Open Banking/A2A. New for 2026 is the MPE NXT pillar, a professional-development track offering hands-on training pods, masterclasses, and speed mentoring focused on skills development in the age of AI.

### Fintech Americas

**When?** March, 24-26, 2026 at Hotel Fontainebleau, Miami Beach, USA

[FinTech Americas](https://www.fintechamericas.co/en/conference-miami/) Miami is recognized as the top FinTech strategy conference focusing on the Latin American region. The event connects global leaders with regional FinTech innovators, providing insight-driven programs specifically designed for CEOs, Founders, and financial institutions actively expanding within LATAM. The core objective is delivering a competitive edge through a highly focused agenda.

The conference structure is built around specialized content tracks to maximize strategic value. Keynotes and main stage talks shape the macro-view, while dedicated tracks, including the FinTech Track (for disruptors), the Insurtech Track (for insurance innovators), and the Leadership Track (for decision-makers), ensure content relevance. The format emphasizes practical problem-solving through Discussion Labs, which are interactive, small-group sessions focused on generating actionable solutions for real-world business and technological challenges.

### PAY360

**When?** March, 25-26, 2026 at Excel, London, UK

Organized by The Payments Association, [PAY360](https://pay360event.com/) is Europe's largest dedicated payments event, gathering over 6,000 professionals under one roof. The summit is defined by its massive scale and comprehensive coverage of the entire payments ecosystem, bringing together policymakers, regulators, banks, merchants, big tech, and processors. With over 1,500 conference delegates and 55% VP and above representation, this event ensures high-level networking and engagement across the community.

The conference boasts an extensive program with 200+ global speakers across more than 200 sessions, all 100% dedicated to payments. The agenda is critical for IT leaders, covering vital strategic and operational topics, including regulation, fraud prevention, cybersecurity, AI, blockchain, and embedded finance. Given the vast scope of exhibitors and attendees, PAY360 is the ultimate venue for both deep industry immersion and discovering solutions across the entire value chain.

### FinTech Meetup Las Vegas

**When?** March 30 - April 1, 2026, at Mandalay Bay, Las Vegas, USA

[FinTech Meetup](https://fintechmeetup.com/home/) distinguishes itself not as a typical conference, but as the industry’s most productive networking event built entirely for growth and partnerships. The entire experience is powered by breakthrough technology designed for results. It attracts a comprehensive ecosystem including thousands of banks, credit unions, FinTechs, VCs, and service providers.

The event's core value is its Meetings Program, which uses a double opt-in matchmaking system to drive over 50,000 qualified, one-to-one meetings between 5,000+ attendees, including over 1,000 CEOs and founders. This format ensures no wasted time and high-impact connections, making it mandatory for senior leaders serious about pipeline, securing real partnerships, and achieving immediate ROI through efficient, targeted collaboration.

### Money 20/20 Asia

**When?** April 21-23, 2026, Queen Sirikit National Convention Center, Bangkok, Thailand

[Money20/20 Asia](https://asia.money2020.com/) is widely recognized as the region’s #1 quality FinTech show, attracting 4,000 leaders from over 75 countries across payments, banking, FinTech, and crypto. The event is a central hub where the most influential decision-makers, including C-Suite executives, investors, and policymakers, gather specifically to get business done.

The summit’s core strength lies in its unmatched networking density: one in three attendees holds a C-Suite position. This ensures strategic engagement among the 1200+ attending companies and 130+ sponsors. Attendees can expect deep regional insights from over 350 speakers and take advantage of the purposefully-curated networking spaces designed to facilitate high-value conversations and help decision-makers lock in business success across the dynamic Asian financial ecosystem.

### FinTech Summit Middle East

**When?** April 22, 2026, Amman, Jordan

[Summit’s](https://fintechsummitme.com/) goal is to become the Levant’s and North Africa’s most influential conference, specifically focusing on how digital currencies and blockchain technologies are redefining the regional financial ecosystem. Held on April 22, 2026, in Amman, the summit serves as the ultimate platform for bridging the gap between traditional finance and the decentralized economy. It gathers 350+ professionals including top innovators, regulators, investors, and digital leaders from the Middle East.

The agenda is highly specialized, highlighting how CBDCs (Central Bank Digital Currencies), stablecoins, and crypto payments are transforming banking, payments, and cross-border commerce across the Middle East. The summit provides crucial insights through visionary keynotes, engaging panel discussions, and the chance to see cutting-edge demos and product launches. Attendees are there to procure and collaborate with the latest technology, gaining expert advice and connecting with key players to take their digital strategy to new heights.

### FinovateSpring

**When?** May 5-7, 2026, at Sheraton San Diego Hotel & Marina, San Diego, USA

[FinovateSpring](https://informaconnect.com/finovatespring/) is the leading US West Coast FinTech event, renowned for its format built around rapid-fire 7-minute live demos of new technologies. Organized by Informa Connect, the conference efficiently connects banks, financial institutions, and investors directly with innovators. The focus is strictly on innovative FinTech and impactful connections, delivered in a fast-paced, curated format refined over 15 years.

The event attracts a highly senior audience, crucial for generating real business. Over 1,200 senior-level attendees participate, with 800+ holding C-level, Head of, Director, or VP positions. With 50+ live FinTech demos and thousands of meetings taking place, FinovateSpring is the tactical platform for banking decision-makers seeking to efficiently vet market-ready technologies and forge partnerships in the dynamic US West Coast FinTech landscape.

### Banking Transformation Summit

**When?** May, 19-20, 2026 at Tobacco Dock, London, UK

The [Banking Transformation Summit](https://bankingtransformationsummit.com/) is the premier forum dedicated exclusively to banks and building societies, serving as the uniting place to discuss the future of financial services. Now in its 6th year, the event attracts over 1,000 attendees, with a highly focused audience: 75% of delegates represent banks and building societies. This strong banking presence ensures direct, high-quality networking with senior decision-makers in the sector.

Agenda centers on critical, forward-looking themes vital for IT leaders, including AI, data strategy, customer experience, and regulatory shifts. Insights are delivered through keynotes, panels, and interactive TableTalks. The event is hosted in a premium venue and utilizes focused theatres and breakout spaces to encourage natural networking and discovery, providing a high-touch environment optimized for engagement and direct dialogue with senior banking leadership.

### Money 20/20 Europe

**When?** June 2-4, 2026, RAI, Amsterdam, Netherlands

Recognized as Europe's numer 1 FinTech Event, [Money 202/20 Europe](https://europe.money2020.com/) is the central hub for the region's most influential leaders from banking, payments, technology, policy, startups, and crypto. The event is engineered for action, focusing on where partnerships form and deals close over three high-impact days. Its scale is significant, attracting over 7,400 attendees from more than 100 countries, representing 2,300+ companies.

The summit’s defining feature is the high concentration of decision-makers: one in three attendees holds a C-Suite position. This density ensures that your time is spent engaging with individuals who call the shots. With over 450 speakers and 330+ sponsors, the event provides extensive visibility and access to a global network, making it the strategic location for executives seeking to maximize their pipeline, grow existing relationships, and shape the future of European FinTech.

### Viva Technology

**When?** June 17-20, 2026, Paris Expo Porte de Versailles, Paris, France

[Viva Technology](https://vivatechnology.com/) (VivaTech), hosted in Paris, is Europe's largest event dedicated to startups and technology. It serves as a massive hub where innovation takes center stage, bringing together 180,000 attendees, 4,000 exhibitors, and a complete ecosystem including corporations, public institutions, and investors from 170+ countries. While broad, this scale offers unmatched exposure to cross-industry technological advancements and future business models.

The event is built for business acceleration, with a specific, high-impact focus on the investment community. It attracts over 3,600 investors seeking the next unicorns among the 14,000+ participating startups. Notably, 75% of startups meet the right investors here. VivaTech tailors the experience for decision-makers with investor-only events and spaces, making it a critical strategic stop for identifying breakthrough technologies and forging essential VC and corporate partnerships.

**When?** July 8-10, 2026, Victoria, Seychelles

This high-level forum is dedicated to fostering collaboration and growth across the critical Afro-Eurasian financial technology corridor. The agenda is crafted to provide expert insights on the most urgent needs and emerging opportunities, including critical developments in regulation, digital payments, AI, and cybersecurity.

The event features an extensive exhibition showcasing cutting-edge innovations (blockchain, RegTech, InsurTech) and emphasizes diverse networking opportunities. This unique setting allows executives to move beyond traditional reports, it is where insight meets impact, enabling senior leaders to sharpen their strategy, navigate compliance, and scale solutions in this rapidly evolving, cross-continental market.

### FinovateFall

**When?** September 9-11, 2026, at Marriott Marquis Times Square, New York, USA

Following the popular FinovateSpring event on the West Coast, organizers invite decision-makers to the East Coast for [FinovateFall](https://informaconnect.com/finovatefall/). This conference maintains the signature Finovate format, focusing on showcasing innovative FinTech that banks can implement today. The highly curated, fast-paced agenda avoids wasting time, delivering actionable insights and technologies.

The event is built for efficient, high-level engagement. It brings together over 2,000 senior decision-makers, including more than 1,000 representatives from banks and investors, with 475+ financial institutions represented in total. A core feature is the exhibition of 60+ live demos, allowing executives to quickly vet new technologies.

Thousands of meetings take place, making FinovateFall the strategic fall checkpoint for connecting with senior-level experts and planning future technology implementation.

### Money20/20 Middle East

**When?** September 14-16, 2026, at RECC, Riyadh, Saudi Arabia

Following its global counterparts, [Money20/20 Middle East](https://money2020middleeast.com/) establishes itself as the region's heartbeat of financial innovation and the fastest-growing FinTech event in the world. Held in Riyadh, this summit is where bold ideas meet big business in a rapidly expanding market. The event attracts a massive scale of influence, bringing together 451 brands, 450 speakers, 1,051 investors, and 157 startups to define the future of money, finance, and technology in the Middle East.

This event is designed for strategic positioning and business creation. It offers global leaders unrivaled regional exposure and direct connections with key decision-makers and trailblazers. Attendees benefit from visionary keynotes and high-impact networking opportunities. For executives seeking to capitalize on regional growth, Money20/20 Middle East is a mandatory stop.

### Sibos 2026

**When?** September 28 - October 1, 2026, Miami Beach Convention Center, USA

Organized annually by SWIFT, [Sibos](https://www.sibos.com/) is the premier gathering for the global financial community. While not strictly a FinTech conference, it is the defining forum for discussions that govern the sector, focusing heavily on financial infrastructure, cross-border payments, banking innovations, and liquidity management. The event attracts an elite audience of executives from major global banks, technology providers, regulatory experts, and policymakers.

Sibos moves strategically across major global financial hubs (with recent/future stops including Frankfurt in 2025, followed by Singapore, Paris, and Dubai). For 2026, Sibos visits Miami for the first time, utilizing the Miami Beach Convention Center. The choice highlights Miami’s increasing importance as a financial center in the USA, known for its expertise in international trade, wealth management, and banking.

This provides IT leaders with crucial insights into the evolving regulatory and technological landscape driving fundamental changes in global finance.

### Money 20/20 USA

**When?** October 18-21, 2026, Las Vegas, USA

As the final major stop in the global franchise (alongside editions in Asia, Europe, and the Middle East), [Money20/20 USA](https://us.money2020.com/) is the largest FinTech conference in North America. Held in Las Vegas, it serves as the essential meeting point where senior leaders from banks, payments, tech, retail, and startups gather to accelerate the future of financial technology. The event is intentionally structured to supercharge deal flow and drive immediate business results.

The conference is a high-impact platform designed for decision-makers (including CTOs) seeking to accelerate deals, build partnerships, and raise their company's profile. Attendees gain insights into the latest trends: spanning digital banking, AI, blockchain, regulation, and cybersecurity, from hundreds of speakers and a massive exhibition area. For any leader focused on growth and market strategy in the North American landscape, this is the paramount opportunity to introduce innovations and form strategic alliances.

### Web Summit Lisbon

**When?** November 9-11, 2026, MEO Arena and FIL, Lisbon, Portugal

One of the world's largest technology events, serving as a dynamic hub where the future takes shape. Attracting over 71,000+ attendees, it offers a front-row seat to groundbreaking innovations across all tech sectors. Crucially for FinTech decision-makers, the summit features a dedicated FinTech Track, alongside focused content on AI, payments, and digital transformation. The show floor is where global success stories like Revolut and Stripe once began, connecting ambitious founders with investors and industry leaders.

While the scale is immense, [Web Summit](https://websummit.com/) prioritizes effective connections. It utilizes an AI-powered recommendation system to help executives and founders find the people who matter most based on shared interests and goals. Beyond the exhibition, the event is famous for its curated after-hours networking; informal meetups and industry gatherings where "the best deals are made over dinner, not in boardrooms." Additionally, attendees gain expert knowledge through masterclasses led by visionaries from global giants like Meta, Microsoft, and Dell.

### Singapore FinTech Festival

**When?** November 18-20, 2026, EXPO, Singapore

The [Singapore FinTech Festival](https://www.fintechfestival.sg/) (SFF) is Asia's preeminent FinTech event, distinguishing itself through its massive scale and institutional backing by the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS). Attracting over 65,000 attendees from 134 countries, SFF is a vital global hub where leaders in finance, technology, and policy converge to define the future of financial services.

It provides an unparalleled platform for exploration, featuring six topical content stages and a comprehensive week-long schedule of side events, exclusive workshops, and roundtables. The agenda covers critical strategic themes for IT decision-makers, including ESG, digital currencies, artificial intelligence in finance, and cybersecurity. SFF fosters connections among policymakers, financial institutions, and startups, ensuring both high-level insights and unmatched networking opportunities to drive digital transformation across the region.

More H2 2026 events will be added once scheduled.

## Top FinTech conferences: Where should you go?

Now that you have the essential calendar, remember this: the true ROI of a FinTech conference often happens *outside* the keynote hall.

Before you commit your travel days, get sharp on your objective: Are you looking for a crucial vendor to solve that integration headache, a strategic partnership, or intelligence on where your main competitor’s CTO is focusing next?

Prioritize deep dive sessions that align with your goals, and seek out meaningful dialogue with peers and industry leaders. Always look for high-value networking opportunities and long-term growth. Pick your spots intentionally, and show up ready to connect to make those discussions count. We’ll definitely visit some of those events.

Just hop on the M2 line of the Metro via Vanløse or catch a cab for a quick ride. If you’d like to take a taxi, you can reach the city centre in around 20-30 minutes. Prices typically range from 250-350 DKK, depending on the traffic. Copenhagen is also easily reachable by ferry from other Nordic countries, including Norway and Sweden. - By Bus Bus 37 “Raffinaderivej” – 5 min walk from the venue Bus 31 “Holmbladsgade” – 10 min walk from the venue To find more information about public transport by bus navigate to https://www.rejseplanen.dk - By Metro M2 line (towards Copenhagen Airport). Get off at Lergravsparken St. or Øresund St. – 10 min walk from the venue To find more information about public transportation by metro navigate to https://www.rejseplanen.dk

# List of fintech conferences in 2026

If you’re interested in expanding your understanding of the Fintech industry, consider attending some of the most anticipated events in 2026

As the [**financial industry**](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GfeBJdEOnZ4) continues to evolve, staying ahead of the latest trends, innovations, and challenges is crucial. At Qubika, we understand the importance of being at the forefront of this fast-paced sector, which is why we actively engage in top fintech events worldwide. These conferences offer valuable insights and opportunities for collaboration, helping shape the future of financial services.

Looking ahead to 2026, we’ve curated a list of recommended [**fintech**](https://qubika.com/industries/financial-services/) conferences that stand out for their content, speakers, and networking potential. Whether you’re a financial leader, a tech innovator, or a fintech enthusiast, these events will provide essential knowledge and connections to keep you competitive in the industry.

We invite you to explore our Financial Services offerings, with expertise in core banking, BaaS integrations, payments, and GenAI-enhanced financial solutions.

**[Qubika’s Finance Studio](https://qubika.com/industries/financial-services/)** empowers organizations to build modern, [AI-driven financial services](https://qubika.com/blog/data-security-in-ai-driven-financial-services/), ensuring you’re ready for the future of finance.

# The 37 Biggest Fintech Conferences, Events & Trade Shows in 2026

Posted by [Jim Bruene](https://fintechlabs.com/author/jbrune/ "Posts by Jim Bruene") | Mar 17, 2026

The 2026 conference season is now officially underway. In fact, nearly one-quarter of the biggest fintech conferences have already concluded. But the biggest events are still coming on the horizon.

We’ve always viewed conferences as a vital part of the business innovation process, the serendipity to see new ideas and think outside the box. But how do you decide which ones to attend? To help you make good choices, start by reviewing our curated list of the top fintech global conferences for 2026. These are the biggest and best events in the financial services space for technologists, product managers, developers, marketers and C-Suite execs at both financial institutions and fintech firms.   
(*Note: See below for specialized financial technology calendars including [insuretech](https://fintechlabs.com/the-biggest-insurtech-events-conferences/), [banktech, regtech](https://fintechlabs.com/the-biggest-banking-mortgage-banktech-events-conferences/) and more*.)

Event details are up-to-date at the time of publication, but always check with the event for the latest information.

# FinTech Event Calendar 2026

*Last updated: 16 April 2026. Curated by the FinTech Profile editorial team, covering fintech since 2015.*

We've mapped out the fintech calendar for the rest of 2026: **41 events** across **14 countries**, from London to Riyadh to Amsterdam. ## Frequently Asked Questions

### What are the biggest fintech conferences in 2026?

The largest fintech conferences in 2026 by expected attendance are: **GITEX GLOBAL 2026 (Fintech Track)** in Dubai (100000+ attendees, 7–11 December 2026); **Web Summit 2026 (Fintech Track)** in Lisbon (70000+ attendees, 9–12 November 2026); **Money20/20 Middle East 2026** in Riyadh (38500+ attendees, 14–16 September 2026); **Seamless Middle East 2026** in Dubai (25000+ attendees, 12–14 May 2026); **Seamless Saudi Arabia 2026** in Riyadh (25000+ attendees, 17–18 November 2026). These events attract senior leaders from banking, payments, and financial technology worldwide.

### Which cities host the most fintech events in 2026?

The leading fintech event hubs in 2026 are: **London** (10 events), **Dubai** (6 events), **Riyadh** (4 events), **Amsterdam** (3 events). London remains the dominant European fintech conference destination, while Dubai and Riyadh are rapidly growing as Middle East fintech hubs.

### What fintech conferences are happening in the UK in 2026?

There are 10 fintech conferences in the UK in 2026, all based in London. These include Innovate Finance Global Summit (IFGS) 2026 (21 April 2026), Payments Leaders' Summit UK (Spring) (28–29 April 2026), Banking Transformation Summit Europe 2026 (19–20 May 2026), Global RegTech Summit 2026 (20 May 2026), and 6 more. Topics span AI in financial services, open banking, payments innovation, and regulatory technology.

### What fintech conferences are happening in the Middle East in 2026?

The Middle East hosts 16 fintech events in 2026 across Amman, Riyadh, Doha, Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Jeddah, Bahrain. Major events include Dubai FinTech Summit 2026 (8000+ attendees), Seamless Middle East 2026 (25000+ attendees), Jeddah Fintech Week 2026 (7000+ attendees), Money20/20 Middle East 2026 (38500+ attendees), Seamless Saudi Arabia 2026 (25000+ attendees), GITEX GLOBAL 2026 (Fintech Track) (100000+ attendees), Abu Dhabi Finance Week 2026 (20000+ attendees). The region is a fast-growing centre for Islamic finance, digital banking, and payments innovation.

### Are there any free fintech conferences in 2026?

Yes, 8 upcoming fintech events offer free admission: **Payments Leaders' Summit UK (Spring)** in London (28–29 April 2026); **IFN Uzbekistan Forum 2026** in Tashkent (12 May 2026); **Seamless Middle East 2026** in Dubai (12–14 May 2026); **iFX EXPO International 2026** in Limassol (16–18 June 2026); **Payments Leaders' Summit EU** in Amsterdam (30 September – 1 October 2026); **Payments Leaders' Summit UK (Autumn)** in London (20–21 October 2026); **Seamless Saudi Arabia 2026** in Riyadh (17–18 November 2026); **FinTech Connect 2026** in London (1–2 December 2026). Some require qualification as senior industry professionals or pre-registration for free expo passes.

### Which fintech conferences have open calls for speakers?

There are currently no open calls for speakers listed. Conference organisers typically open CFPs 4–6 months before the event. Check back regularly or [contact us](/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#91f4e6f0ffd1f7f8ffe5f4f2f9e1e3fef7f8fdf4bff2fefc) if you know of an open CFP we should list.

### What fintech conferences are happening in Europe in 2026?

Europe hosts 9 fintech events in 2026 across France, Netherlands, Denmark, Cyprus, Portugal. Highlights include Web Summit 2026 (Fintech Track) in Lisbon, Money20/20 Europe 2026 in Amsterdam, iFX EXPO International 2026 in Limassol.

## 2. [Fintech Meetup](https://fintechmeetup.com/home)

*Best for high-volume networking for partnerships and business development in fintech.*

![Fintech Meetup](https://media.designrush.com/tinymce_images/997377/Screenshot-2026-03-06-at-9.07.30_PM.jpg "Fintech Meetup")

Source: Fintech Meetup

* **Date:** March 29 – April 1, 2026
* **Medium:** In-person
* **Location:** The Venetian Resort, Las Vegas, Nevada, USA
* **Key Speakers:**
  + Aaron Slettehaugh – Senior Vice President Product, Ripple
  + Angela Strange – General Partner, Andreessen Horowitz
  + Kyle Hauptman – Chairman, National Credit Union Administration
* **Price:**
  + Free hosted passes available for qualified banks, credit unions, and merchants.
  + $1,800 for banks and credit unions & retailers and merchants
  + $3,500 for general attendees

Fintech Meetup is one of the largest fintech industry gatherings, designed specifically for partnerships and business development.

Unlike [traditional conferences](https://www.designrush.com/agency/digital-marketing/trends/digital-marketing-conferences) that rely primarily on hallway networking, the event uses a double-opt-in matchmaking system that helps attendees schedule one-on-one meetings in advance.

The agenda focuses on practical industry issues such as [AI adoption in banking](https://www.designrush.com/agency/ai-companies/trends/how-many-companies-use-ai), embedded finance, real-time payments, cross-border infrastructure, and regulatory shifts.

In 2026, the event also introduces new formats like TableTalks, a small peer-led discussions with no presentations, and the Banking CMO Summit, an invite-only program for leaders in financial services.

*[Get your ticket to join Fintech Meetup.](https://fintechmeetup.com/page/get-your-ticket)*

UK FinTech Week is one of the United Kingdom’s major fintech gatherings.

Organized by Innovate Finance, the event draws more than 1,500 attendees from over 70 countries. Its main event is the [Innovate Finance Global Summit](https://www.innovatefinance.com/ifgs2026/secureyourticket/).

This year’s program focuses on how fintech is evolving alongside regulation, open banking, stablecoin policy, and the growth of new fintech companies.

The week also includes multiple events, such as:

Regulators are also strongly involved in the event, with dedicated office hours where startups can meet directly with officials from the Financial Conduct Authority.

## Fintech Conferences FAQs

### 1. Which fintech conferences in 2026 are best for networking and partnerships?

Events like Fintech Meetup, Money 20/20 USA, and Fintech Americas focus on helping attendees connect.

They offer tools for scheduling meetings, one-on-one sessions, and curated networking opportunities with executives, startups, and investors.

### 2. Where can I see the latest fintech products and live demos?

FinovateSpring and FinovateFall are great for hands-on demos of working fintech products.

Attendees can see technologies in action, talk to the teams behind them, and get an early look at innovations before they reach the wider market.

### 3. Which conferences provide insights into fintech regulation and policy?

UK Fintech Week, Singapore FinTech Festival, and SIBOS are good options for regulatory and policy updates.

They feature talks with regulators and panels on topics like open banking, stablecoins, fraud prevention, and regional rules.

### 4. Are there fintech conferences focused on crypto, blockchain, and digital assets?

Yes, Consensus by CoinDesk is the main event for crypto and blockchain professionals.

It covers DeFi, tokenization, stablecoins, and blockchain’s overlap with AI, attracting both startups and institutional investors.

### 5. Which conferences are best for startups or operators?

Startups and fintech practitioners may prefer Fintech NerdCon, Fintech Generations, and Fintech Talents Festival.

These events offer peer-led sessions, workshops, and discussions focused on practical skills and real-world applications.

Get the hottest Fintech Singapore News once a month in your Inbox

Singapore has emerged as a prominent fintech hub in Southeast Asia, capitalizing on its effective regulatory framework, robust infrastructure, and its large pool of highly skilled and experienced finance professionals.

In 2025, Singapore [maintained](https://fintechnews.sg/121819/singapore-fintech-festival-2025/asean-fintech-funding-2025-report/) its position as the region’s top recipient of fintech funding, securing over US$725 million in the first nine months of the year, and accounting for 87% of ASEAN’s total funding. The country also represented more than half of all deals, primarily driven by activity in blockchain for financial services and investment technology.

As this landscape continues to expand and evolve, Singapore remains a significant hub for fintech events, attracting industry leaders from around the globe to explore opportunities and address emerging challenges.

Among the numerous conferences scheduled in the upcoming months, the following 8 stand out as the most significant gatherings in 2026. These events are set to convene leading decision-makers, regulators, and innovators to shape the future of the sector.

![](https://fintechnews.sg/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Fireblocks-5ppl-600x500-1.gif)

#### Fintech Events to Attend in Singapore in 2026

### Nexgen Banking Summit 2026

*March 12, 2026*

*Singapore*

The [Nexgen Banking Summit 2026](https://techtrekevents.com/NBS2026/), scheduled for March 12, 2026 in Singapore, will focus on the future of intelligent transformation within financial services. The conference will gather digital banking leaders, data strategists, product innovators, and artificial intelligence (AI) decision‑makers to discuss how banks are integrating advanced AI technologies, automation, and real‑time intelligence into their core systems.

The summit will highlight ways to accelerate productivity and innovation by connecting core banking platforms, chat logs, compliance documents, and transaction data, thereby fostering smarter collaboration and faster execution. It will also emphasize the delivery of hyper‑personalized customer journeys and the empowerment of employees with intelligent tools that improve resolution speed and deepen engagement.

Attendees will learn how AI, large language models (LLMs), and predictive analytics are reshaping decision making across retail, commercial, and investment banking. They will also gain insight into building scalable, secure, genAI‑powered infrastructures that can evolve alongside digital banking strategies.

In addition to the main program, the summit will offer opportunities for one‑on‑one meetings with senior banking executives and fintech leaders who are actively exploring AI‑driven financial services, embedded finance platforms, and core‑banking modernization. Roundtable discussions will allow participants to lead focused dialogues on subjects such as real‑time payments, regtech compliance, and digital transformation, while also gathering direct feedback from decision‑makers.

### TMT Finance APAC 2026

*May 06-07, 2026*

*Singapore*

[TMT Finance APAC 2026](https://events.tmtfinance.com/asia/) will take place in Singapore on May 06 and 07, 2026. Formerly known as the TMT M&A Forum APAC, this conference promises a premier gathering for senior dealmakers involved in mergers and acquisitions (M&A), financing, and investment across digital‑infrastructure sectors such as data centers, fibre, telecoms, towers, and cloud services throughout the Asia‑Pacific (APAC) region.

The two-day event is expected to bring together 350 senior industry, investment, and advisory leaders, featuring 75 speakers who will deliver 25 sessions and facilitate roughly seven hours of networking each day.

Attendees can expect a focused agenda built around key thematic tracks:

Speakers will include:

In addition to the conference, TMT Finance APAC 2026 will provide a platform for industry stakeholders to meet, discover emerging deal opportunities and strategic trends, and forge new partnerships on site. Participants will also have the chance to raise their corporate profile, showcase thought leadership, generate leads, and gain recognition through the conference awards.

### Asia Tech x Singapore 2026

*May 20-22, 2026*

*Capella, Singapore*

[Asia Tech x Singapore (ATxSG) 2026](https://asiatechxsg.com/) will be held in Singapore from May 20 to 22, 2026. Jointly organized by the Infocomm Media Development Authority of Singapore (IMDA) and Informa and supported by the Singapore Tourism Board, ATxSG 2026 will mark the sixth anniversary of one of Asia’s premier technology gatherings, bringing together government officials, industry leaders, and innovators to discuss the intersection of technology, society and the digital economy.

The three-day event will be structured around three core components.

ATxEnterprise, hosted at Singapore Expo and run by Informa, will serve as the commercial hub of the conference, showcasing broadcast technology, telecommunications infrastructure, satellite communications and enterprise solutions. It will provide a marketplace for more than 22,000 global tech, broadcasting, telecom, satellite and startup leaders to exhibit innovations, forge partnerships and explore digital‑transformation opportunities.

ATxSummit, hosted by IMDA at Capella Singapore, will serve as the apex event of ATxSG, comprising two distinct experiences: The Plenary and The Village.

The Plenary will present an invitation-only conference exploring critical themes including AI, governance and safety, quantum computing, and sustainability and compute; while the Village will curate exclusive government-to-government and government-to-business roundtables where senior government officials, industry leaders and academia will share digital challenges, alongside symposiums, workshops, showcases and networking to cultivate digital partnerships between the public and private sector.

Finally, ATxInspire, a companion series to ATxSummit, will host a year-round series of thought-provoking discussions and presentations by industry leaders, government officials, and academics. These events will focus on cutting-edge advancements in technology, empowering the tech community to stay ahead of the curve.

### SuperAI 2026

*June 10-11, 2026*

*Marina Bay Sands, Singapore*

[SuperAI 2026](https://www.superai.com/) will take place on June 10 and 11, 2026 at Marina Bay Sands in Singapore. The conference aims to showcase the transformative power of AI by gathering visionaries, developers, startups, enterprises, researchers and policymakers from around the world. It’s expected to bring together more than 10,000 attendees representing over 100 countries, alongside roughly 1,500 AI‑focused companies and upwards of 150 speakers.

SuperAI 2026 will highlight six themes that are defining the current AI landscape: frontier models; robotics and embodied AI; AI infrastructure; AI applications in finance; biotech and healthtech; and the broader global impact of AI on society. These themes will be explored through keynote talks, panel discussions and demonstrations that illustrate how AI is reshaping industries and everyday life.

Speakers will include Balaji Srinivasan, founder, investor and author of The Network State; Dwarkesh Patel, host of the Dwarkesh Podcast; Tao Cheung, co‑founder of Manus AI; Edward Snowden, noted whistleblower; and Felix Shang, director at Unitree.

### ITC Asia 2026

*June 30-July 02, 2026*

*Sands Expo and Convention Centre, Singapore*

[ITC Asia 2026](https://fintechnews.sg/fintech-events-singapore/event/itc-asia-2026/) will be held from June 30 to July 02, 2026 at the Sands Expo and Convention Centre in Singapore. This three‑day conference will gather more than 1,500 senior insurance leaders from around the world, with roughly 80% occupying VP‑level or higher positions. Over 200 inspirational speakers will address the rapid evolution of Asia’s insurance sector and the strategic imperatives needed to stay competitive.

The program will be organized around key thematic tracks:

Two main stages will host keynote addresses, “in‑the‑hot‑seat” sessions, a startup pitch competition and fireside chats with insurers, regulators and technology providers. Curated education will be delivered through kickoff summits, tailored tracks and a variety of interactive formats. Finally, networking opportunities will include a dedicated one-to-one meeting zone, roundtable discussions and Ask‑Me‑Anything sessions.

### Tech Week Singapore 2026

*September 29-30, 2026*

*Sands Expo Convention Centre, Singapore*

[Tech Week Singapore 2026](https://www.singaporetechnologyweek.com/) will take place on September 29 and 30, 2026, at the Sands Expo Convention Centre. The two-day program is expected to attract more than 29,000 business leaders, visionaries and decision‑makers across all technology verticals, over 500 exhibitors and more than 500 international speakers and thought‑leaders who will showcase solutions and share insights.

Tech Week Singapore 2026 will consolidate five co‑located conferences: Cloud and AI Infrastructure Asia, DevOps Live!, Cyber Security World Asia, Data Centre World Asia and Big Data and AI World Asia. Together, these tracks will cover the full spectrum of modern enterprise technology, from cloud and AI infrastructure to development operations, cybersecurity, data center strategies and big data analytics.

Attendees can therefore expect a blend of product demonstrations, expert panels and networking opportunities designed to help organizations learn, connect and shape their future strategies.

### TOKEN2049 Singapore 2026

*October 07-08, 2026*

*Marina Bay Sands, Singapore*

[TOKEN2049 Singapore 2026](https://fintechnews.sg/fintech-events-singapore/event/token2049-singapore-2026/) will be held on October 07 and 08, 2026 at Marina Bay Sands. Promoted as the definitive global gathering for the cryptocurrency and Web 3 ecosystem, the event will occupy all five floors of the iconic venue, creating a pop‑up city that hosts immersive experiences, side events and a festival‑like atmosphere.

The event is expected to bring together decision makers from more than 160 countries, with over 60% of attendees holding C‑level roles. Organizers anticipate over 1,000 side events and a schedule of panels, workshops and networking sessions that aim to shape the worldwide trajectory of crypto and decentralized technologies.

Highlights will include the TOKEN2049 Origins Hackathon, a 36‑hour challenge that will guides participants from concept to functional prototype while providing mentorship and the opportunity to pitch for prizes, funding and global recognition, and the Nexus Startup Competition, which will offer the hottest Web 3 startups a stage to pitch for equity‑free prizes, meet top investors and potentially launch their ventures on a global platform.

### Singapore Fintech Festival (SFF) 2026

*November 18-20, 2026*

*Singapore EXPO, Singapore*

The [Singapore Fintech Festival (SFF) 2026](https://fintechnews.sg/fintech-events-singapore/event/singapore-fintech-festival-sff-2026/) will take place from November 18 to 20, 2026 at the Singapore EXPO. Organized as a global gathering of policy makers, financiers, and technology innovators, the three‑day event aims to foster connections and collaborations across the financial services ecosystem.

The program will span six thematic stages that cover emerging trends such as blockchain, AI, and other cutting‑edge financial technologies. Attendees will get to participate in panels, keynotes, roundtables, workshops, and a week‑long series of side events designed to deepen learning and networking.

SFF 2026 will also offer a variety of exhibition options, including standard and premium booths, international pavilions, dedicated startup and technology zones, and bespoke activations for demonstrations, workshops, or private events. It will also provide ample opportunities for business development and collaboration.

## When is Singapore FinTech Festival 2026?

Singapore FinTech Festival 2026 will run from November 12 - 14, 2026.

## What type of event is Singapore FinTech Festival 2026?

Singapore FinTech Festival 2026 is a leading international conference and exhibition dedicated to financial technology, banking innovation, and regulatory advancements in digital finance. The event features keynote presentations, expert panel discussions, investment-focused workshops, and an expansive exhibition floor showcasing the latest solutions in payments, blockchain, AI-driven finance, and cybersecurity.

Singapore FinTech Festival typically attracts tens of thousands of attendees from across the globe, including fintech executives, bank leaders, venture capitalists, technology vendors, and regulatory authorities—all eager to discover the latest trends, forge strategic partnerships, and gain insights into the future of financial technology.

# The 37 Biggest Fintech Conferences, Events & Trade Shows in 2026

Posted by [Jim Bruene](https://fintechlabs.com/author/jbrune/ "Posts by Jim Bruene") | Mar 17, 2026

The 2026 conference season is now officially underway. In fact, nearly one-quarter of the biggest fintech conferences have already concluded. But the biggest events are still coming on the horizon.

We’ve always viewed conferences as a vital part of the business innovation process, the serendipity to see new ideas and think outside the box. But how do you decide which ones to attend? To help you make good choices, start by reviewing our curated list of the top fintech global conferences for 2026. These are the biggest and best events in the financial services space for technologists, product managers, developers, marketers and C-Suite execs at both financial institutions and fintech firms.   
(*Note: See below for specialized financial technology calendars including [insuretech](https://fintechlabs.com/the-biggest-insurtech-events-conferences/), [banktech, regtech](https://fintechlabs.com/the-biggest-banking-mortgage-banktech-events-conferences/) and more*.)

Event details are up-to-date at the time of publication, but always check with the event for the latest information.
